purpose: Unified dashboard seed data prepared from AusTender JSONL and Prompcorp Tenders.Net HTML snapshots.

fields.id: Stable record id in source:id format.
fields.title: Opportunity title.
fields.description: Plain-text summary/details for table/detail view and keyword filtering.
fields.sector_primary: Primary Prompcorp-facing sector bucket: facility_management, construction, cleaning, other.
fields.sector_tags: One or more sector tags inferred from title/details.
fields.service_line_relevance: True if record maps to Prompcorp core sectors.
fields.view_bucket: Dashboard tab bucket: active, upcoming, recently_closed, archived.
fields.days_to_close: Days from as_of_date to close date.
fields.estimated_value_aud: Parsed monetary amount when a reliable amount-like token exists; null otherwise.
fields.value_band: Range bucket for filtering and charts.
fields.contact_email: First email parsed from contact block when present.
fields.first_seen_at: First snapshot date observed in provided files.
fields.last_seen_at: Last snapshot date observed in provided files.
fields.seen_count: Number of uploaded snapshots containing the record.

The next Aged Care release goes into production 29th June 2026 (aka Release 14). The Department is seeking a partner that can provide penetration testing deliverables as per the Statement of Requirements. Essential criteria 1. The risk associated with the quotation / proposal. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: LH-06203 - 1 Lead Manager - Strategy and Content Content Strategists develop a content strategy that steers the creation of unambiguous, meaningful, cohesive, engaging, and sustainable content. They focus on the planning, structure, process, creation, delivery, and governance of content. Key duties and responsibilities • Provide strategic oversight and manage the delivery of Family Safety high-priority pieces of work. • Manage the alignment between project activities, evidence inputs and project key milestones. • Strengthen governance, risk management , change management and transformation across the program. • Develop a whole-of-program consolidated view of all work to assist leadership in driving a joined up and efficient approach across all internal and external stakeholders. • Embed succession plans and good governance structures in line with departmental compliance requirements. Essential criteria 1. The extent to which the Potential Supplier’s Response meets the Customer’s Requirement set out in the Request for Quote (RFQ). 2. opportunities.title: Invite only: Business analyst
opportunities.description: LH-06229 - 1 Business Analyst Job details This opportunity has been sent to selected suppliers only. Should DHA require other/further/new submissions DHA will make direct contact. Unsolicited emails will not be answered. The Business Analyst role at Defence Housing Australia (DHA) involves conducting analysis, design, and specification of new and existing IT systems to support business operations. The role requires gathering business requirements, facilit ating workshops, and translating business needs into technical specifications. The Business Analyst provides expert advice to system users, investigates complex issues, and recommends solutions to improve processes and enhance customer service. This position acts as a bridge between DHA’s business areas and the software development team, ensuring effective collaboration and successful project outcomes Key duties and responsibilities • Acquire and apply extensive knowledge of DHA's business operations and enterprise applications to gather business requirements for systems development, maintenance and enhancement. • Demonstratable experience analysing entities that operate short- and long-term property asset management lifecycles. • Drive evolution of business capabilities that support asset management , and that affect organisational policies, processes and ICT systems. • Demonstratable experience in understanding and leveraging the capabilities of finance management systems including TM1 and TechnologyOne. • Support business design activities including workshop facilit ation, stakeholder interviews and deliver engaging presentations • Analyse, interpret and compose business rules and requirements into functional specifications, acceptance criteria and other technical documents including offering specialist advice in the context of the solution design and business process improvement. • Conduct and assist with definition, analysis and testing of business systems including the investigation and resolution of application system problems • Prepare, document and quality review workflows for new or enhanced systems. • Advise on methods to improve processes and remove non value-add activities. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only. E: ictprocurement@dha.gov.au Deadline for asking questions Tuesday, 21 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time

opportunities.title: Invite only: Lead enterprise security architect
opportunities.description: LH-06222 - One (1) Lead Enterprise security Architect Job details security Architects design, review, test and assure the technical environment, solution designs and solution implementations in accordance with current and emerging security best practice. They contribute to the technical architecture and security policies and practices. Key duties and responsibilities The Specified Personnel will be responsible for providing the following deliverables including but not limited to: Directing and guiding stakeholders to ensure security is built into departmental capabilities; Reviewing and assessing the security design of ICT systems, applications and capabilities; Providing strategic direction for technology security capabilities; Contributing to the security perspective of the wider strategic plan of the department; Designing assessments and feedback documents in the approved Departmental manner; Producing strategic and security related documentation; Providing out of hours support during exception circumstances. This entails: The specified personnel to be available, and in the ‘required state of readiness’ to perform extra duty outside of standard business hours’ The ‘required state of readiness’ includes the requirement for specified personnel to: Respond within 30 minutes of notification of requirement to attend or as directed by Director, Technology Architecture. Maintain compliance with the departments Professional Standards and Integrity Framework. Which includes but is not limited to, ensuring services are not performed whilst impaired by any legal or illegal drugs or alcohol. Specified Personnel will be expected to transfer skills and knowledge to departmental staff and ensure appropriate documentation is stored in accordance with the Department’s recordkeeping policy and practices. The Specified Personnel will be responsible for producing the following deliverables, including but not limited to: • Requirements documentation (Functional/non-functional); • Architecture artefacts; • Enterprise level strategies and roadmaps; • Reporting; • Executive briefings; • Strategic and security documentation; • security Architecture pack; • security Requirements. All deliverables performed by the specified personnel must: • be performed or delivered promptly and without delay in accordance with the timeframes notified by the buyer from time to time; and • meet the assessment and performance criteria set out in any monitoring and performance assessment processes notified to the seller from time to time. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior security analyst
opportunities.description: PCS-03796 - Senior security Analyst Requirements Stewardship Division within the Australian Submarine Agency (ASA) is responsible for assuring the safety, security , regulatory compliance, and long-term sustainability of Australia’s Nuclear-Powered Submarine (NPS) enterprise. Given the criticality, sensitivity, and trilateral nature of the NPS program, senior security leadership is required to coordinate protective security , cyber security , and information assurance activities across delivery programs, regulators, partners, and government stakeholders. The security Analyst (SFIA Level 6) will provide senior security leadership and specialist advice to support delivery of Submarine Rotational Force‑West operational readiness, Australian sovereign operational service and Australian depot operational capability. The role ensures that protective security , cyber security , and information assurance activities are integrated, risk‑informed, and aligned with Delivery Program objectives, regulatory obligations, and trilateral partner requirements across the NPS enterprise. The security Analyst will operate with a high degree of autonomy and influence and report to the Director of Stewardship Program management Office. Applicants/Vendors must: • Be an Australian, US or UK citizen • Have and maintain a minimum Australian NV1 clearance issued through AGSVA • Be prepared to undergo additional security screening to determine the suitability to obtain and maintain access to ASA compartments • Be based in Canberra, ACT • Vendors provide no more than two candidates for consideration. • Each candidate must submit 500 words explaining their demonstrated suitability for the position at a SFIA 6 level with a current CV and two referees. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Marine pollution emergency response
opportunities.description: WS5633235152 - Marine Pollution Emergency Response Open tender for supply of marine pollution response equipment, including offshore storage bladders and boom systems, to support NSW offshore and coastal emergency response capability. Project goals The objective of this project is to procure specialised marine pollution response equipment to strengthen NSW’s offshore, nearshore and coastal emergency response capability. This includes enhancing the State’s ability to contain, recover and manage oil and hazardous material spills in a timely and effective manner. The project aims to address identified capability gaps by acquiring compliant, fit-for-purpose and certified equipment that meets operational, technical and safety requirements. It will support rapid deployment, improve first-strike response readiness, and ensure interoperability with national maritime response frameworks. A key goal is to ensure ongoing compliance with legislative obligations under the Marine Pollution Act 2012 and the National Plan for Maritime Environmental Emergencies. The procurement also seeks to reduce environmental, reputational and financial risks associated with inadequate spill response capability. Additionally, the project will deliver value for money through a competitive open tender process, considering whole-of-life costs, warranty support and equipment reliability, while ensuring timely delivery and operational readiness across NSW. Scope The scope includes the supply, delivery and commissioning of specialised marine pollution response equipment to support offshore, nearshore and coastal operations across NSW. Key items include offshore oil storage bladders, rapid deployment boom systems and fully equipped regional response trailers for containment and recovery operations. Suppliers may also be required to provide associated services such as transportation, assembly, testing and demonstration, warranty and defects support, and optional training. All equipment must meet defined technical, safety and performance standards, and be suitable for reliable deployment in marine emergency response environments. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Gaurav Chauhan E: Gaurav.Chauhan2@transport.nsw.gov.au

opportunities.description: RFT - Integrated Enforcement System and Solution Burwood Council invites tenders for the provision, installation, and ongoing support of Regulatory Enforcement Software, Secure Data Storage, and License Plate Recognition (LPR) Vehicle Enforcement Solutions. As a minimum, Council seeks a fully operational and Revenue NSW approved enforcement solution that includes enforcement software, LPR capability, and secure data storage, with seamless integration across paid parking, permit, and enforcement systems. opportunities.description: RFT 2025/26-800317 - Design Consultancy services - Pedestrian bridge & off-road shared path - Wentworth Falls (previously advertised as Design Consultancy services for the Construction of Off-Road Shared Path at Wentworth Falls) Blue Mountains City Council seeks to engage a suitably qualified multidisciplinary consultancy firm or a consortium of contractors to provide detail design services and related studies for the Get NSW Active Wentworth Falls Connectivity Project. The scope for the route extends from the northern edge of the town centre (Station St), crossing the railway line and continuing north along Blaxland Road. At the intersection of Blaxland Road and Lake Street, the route turns west and continues along Lake Street, connecting to the lake via Apanie Lane. High level scope summary is as follows: • Detailed design and documentation of shared path infrastructure totalling approx. 600m in length and including: - Off road shared paths - On road shared zones - Elevated, standalone shared path bridge crossing the western railway line - Connections to existing transport infrastructure network - Incorporation of place making elements such as landscap ing, seating, bike racks • Predominant 3m shared path width • Designed in alignment with Get NSW Active design guidance • Alternative design solutions to address constraints to be negotiated with TfNSW • Provide all surveys, investigations, reports, and approvals needed to support the design • management of the approval process for design of shared path bridge over Sydney Trains asset s TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Rail project works (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Inland Rail - Albury to Illabo (A2I) And Stockinbingal to Parkes (S2P) Overview About Inland Rail Inland Rail is a fast freight backbone stretching more than 1,700km that will transform how we move goods around Australia by providing transit times of less than 24 hours between Melbourne and Brisbane. It will better link businesses, manufacturers and producers to national and global markets and generate new opportunities for existing and emerging industries. Comprising 13 individual projects, Inland Rail is the largest freight rail infrastructure project in Australia and will enable safer, less congested highways, fewer carbon emissions, cheaper freight costs and new economic opportunities for our regions. Inland Rail is a shot in the arm for Australia’s economy, generating $18b in economic benefits and more than 21,500 direct and indirect jobs. The Australian Government selected the Australian Rail Track Corporation (ARTC) to deliver Inland Rail, in partnership with the private sector. Albury to Parkes Enhancement Projects The Albury to Parkes section involves extensive enhancements to specific sites across the 355km of existing track running from Albury to Illabo and Stockinbingal to Parkes. The enhancements will provide the necessary vertical and horizontal clearance along the existing ‘Main South Line’ corridor to support the safe running of double-stacked freight trains up to 1,800m long and 6.5m high. The Albury to Parkes section is divided into two segments: · Albury to Illabo · Stockinbingal to Parkes In mid-2022, ARTC appointed Martinus Rail as one of three elected contractors to bid for the Albury to Parkes enhancement projects. Martinus Rail is currently preparing its competitive bid for these projects. About Martinus Martinus are an Australian owned and operated family business, specialising in the delivery of rail infrastructure projects. Martinus provides civil, track, overhead wiring and signalling capability supported by a range of specialised plant and equipment. Description of Works involved It is noted this description may vary and is subject to final planning consents. Albury to Illabo (approximately 185km) · Five rail bridge modifications · Five road bridge modifications including two replacement bridges · Four footbridge modifications · One footbridge removal · Two gantry modifications · 15 track modifications including slews, reconstruction and lowering · One conversion to single track. Stockinbingal to Parkes (approximately 170km) · Two water tower modifications · Five track slews · One rail bridge and platform awning modification · One track lowering and bridge modification · One crossing loop. Signalling Design and Installation · Scope yet to be finalised. === WORK PACKAGES Wiradjuri Artist - 14 Nov 2025 Any opportunities not listed elsewhere - 31 Dec 2027 TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Inspection of towers using unmanned aerial vehicle
opportunities.description: RFT0493 - All Centres - Inspection of Towers Using Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) for a Period of 36 Months The Power and water Corporation (PWC) manages a large portfolio of transmission structures across the Northern Territory. These include 66 kV and 132 kV lattice towers, guyed towers, poles made up of concrete and steel, A frame and I beam structures that operate in harsh environmental condition s and require inspection to maintain safety, reliability and regulatory compliance. PWC is seeking a qualified tenderer to provide drone inspection services for transmission structures. The tenderer shall be responsible for capturing high-resolution RGB and thermal aerial imagery, identifying defects and anomalies, and delivering comprehensive inspection reports with mapped results in ArcGIS or an approved alternative platform. The tenderer shall undertake all inspection activities in accordance with the reference guideline document. The tenderer may propose additional inspection activities where these enhance the overall condition assess ment. Referenced documents which are part of the scope shall be reviewed to understand the full requirements. Any proposed deviations from the specified scope or the guideline must be clearly identified and supported with justification. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: Expression of Interest - audit Committee Independent Members Expressions of Interest are invited from individuals interested in joining Somerset Regional Council’s audit Committee. The Committee The audit Committee has four members in total – two Councillors and two independent members, with one independent member attending each alternate Committee meeting. The independent member in attendance will act as the Chairperson of the Committee. As an Independent Member of the Committee, you will contribute your specialist skills and knowledge to matters which assist Council in the oversight of internal and external audit functions, financial reporting, and oversight of internal control systems. Meetings Meetings are held at least four times per year, with a dedicated meeting for consideration of the financial statements. Meetings are generally held at the Council Administration Office at 2 Redbank Street, Esk. Each independent member will need to attend at least two (2) meetings each year. Total remuneration for this position $1,000 per meeting inclusive of preparation, travel time and costs, meeting attendance and superannuation (as applicable). TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Grant - Prosperity Path Program
opportunities.description: Prosperity Path Program The Principal are supporting businesses looking to set up in the council area, and existing local business looking to expand or create new employment opportunities, through Prosperity Path Program. Grants of up to $2,000 are currently available this financial year. Any proposal that boosts the local economy will be considered under this grant funding. There are 3 different grants available. Get Going stream - $1,000 per application Tailored for busy small business owners who need a little extra support to take their business to the next level. The application process is designed with minimal paperwork required. This could be used for new equipment, training or conference attendance, marketing or events. New Business stream - $1,500 per application Designed to accelerate the establishment of a new business, alleviate business hardship in its infant stage (up to 12 months of the business launch). Funding is functioned as support only. Start-up businesses expected to be planning to generate the majority of their income from other sources (i.e. not from this grant). This could be used for equipment, training or conference attendance, marketing, Council rate relief or rent assistance. Growth stream - $2,000 per application Aimed to assist the growth or expansion of an established business, either into new markets, property , products or services or escalating the current property . These expansions expected to grow business revenue as well as bring more local jobs. This could be used for equipment, new infrastructure, training or conference attendance, or marketing. Generally, to be eligible an applicant must be a registered Australian business and be located, or committed to relocate, within thecouncil area. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Environment Group Grants
opportunities.description: Environment Group Grants Council is proud to support the Environment Grant Program in recognition of the significant contributions our community environment groups make. The program supports groups with capacity building, weed control and biolink enhancement to benefit the community and natural environment of the Macedon Ranges Shire. Council has endorsed updated guidelines for Group Support and Biolink Boost grants, strengthening alignment with the Community Grants Policy 2025 and simplifying the program for applicants. The Group Support grant has increased to up to $1,000 per group, providing flexible support for group administration, training, governance, insurance and small-scale environmental works. The Strategic Weed Grant has been discontinued. Weed management is now supported through the Biolink Boost Grant, where it forms part of broader restoration projects. The Biolink Boost Grant will move to a biennial model, with the next round opening in early 2027 to align with other Council funding streams. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: For further information, call the Environment Unit on (03) 5422 0333 or email environment@mrsc.vic.gov.au

opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior finance process P2P functional analyst
opportunities.description: LH-06230 - 1 Senior Finance Process P2P Functional Analyst Job details The Bureau’s Finance Department is responsible for delivering accurate, timely, and meaningful financial information to internal decision‑makers. Over time, several core processes have become less efficient, inconsistent, or overly manual due to system constraints, legacy practices, and resourcing pressures. The Bureau currently operates on an older version of SAP, with a full upgrade scheduled to commence in FY 26/27. To ensure the upgrade is successful and does not migrate existing issues into a new system, it is essential that Finance processes are documented, stabilised, and improved ahead of the transition. The Bureau is seeking an experienced and specialised Finance Process Architect to assist the Finance Team in assessing, prioritising, and redesigning key financial processes. This engagement will provide additional capacity to stabilise foundational processes, improve efficiency, and prepare the organisation for the upcoming SAP upgrade. The successful candidate will work collaboratively with Finance staff to document, analyse, and improve end‑to‑end processes, with early focus on the procure to pay cycle including purchase orders/requisitions, accounts payable, accruals, internal control s, workflows, monthly reporting, inventory management and SAP workflow dependencies. Objectives and Outcomes • Strengthen and stabilise key financial processes to ensure accuracy, timeliness, and cost‑effectiveness. • A prioritised improvement roadmap across all key finance processes. • Develop clear, documented, repeatable processes that support consistent financial operations. • Build internal capability through co‑design, knowledge transfer, and clear documentation. • Documented end‑to‑end processes for priority finance functions. • Clear process maps, RACIs, and standard operating procedures. Key duties and responsibilities Full Requirements – Responsibilities The responsibilities of the role include but are not limited to: Process review: • Assess current processes across key finance functions. • Identify inefficiencies, risks, control gaps, and manual workarounds. • Review SAP transaction flows and dependencies. • Engage with Finance staff to understand pain points and constraints. • Develop a design of future state processes, including a prioritised implementation roadmap. Process redesign: Develop improved process maps and process flows for priority areas (AP, accruals, SAP workflows). • Define roles, responsibilities, and handoffs. • Support development of standard operating procedures and supporting documentation. • Ensure alignment with current SAP capabilities and future upgrade requirements. • Implementation Support: • Support early adoption of redesigned processes. • Provide coaching and knowledge transfer to Finance staff. • Assist with testing and validation • Assist with training and development of training documents • Comply with all Bureau work, health and safety policies and procedures, and take reasonable care for your own health and safety and that of employees, contractors and visitors who may be affected by your conduct. • Commitment to APS Values, Employment Principles, Code of Conduct and the Bureau's Social Justice Strategy. Knowledge and Skills Required Essential • Relevant qualification and proven hands-on experience in accounting principles and practices • Deep understanding of end‑to‑end finance processes (P2P, O2C, R2R, Treasury, asset Accounting). • Strong knowledge of financial control s, compliance, and audit requirements. • Experience designing global templates and standardised finance processes, including use of Signavio or related process management tools. • Strong working knowledge of ERP systems (SAP preferred), including finance modules and cross‑module integration. • Strong background in communication and change management • Demonstrated experience in training development and delivery • Ability to translate business requirements into system‑enabled processes. • Understanding of data structures, master data governance, and reporting frameworks. • Experience in process mapping, process modelling, and operating model design. • Familiarity with process improvement methodologies (Lean, Six Sigma, BPMN). • Strong facilit ation, negotiation, and influencing skills. • Ability to work with senior finance leaders and cross‑functional teams. • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts. • Ability to diagnose root causes across people, process, data, and technology. • Strong analytical mindset with experience using process mining or analytics tools. • Ability to balance strategic thinking with practical, actionable solutions. Desirable • Experience with SAP S/4HANA Finance or other modern ERP platforms. • Experience leading finance transformation or ERP implementation programs. • Exposure to automation technologies (RPA, workflow, AI). • Experience in shared services or global business services environments. • Knowledge of project management methodologies. • Experience in highly regulated industries (e.g., government, financial services ). opportunities.title: Catering services
opportunities.description: Expression of Interest: Catering services Required Gascoyne Art Awards 2026 | Opening & Award Night The Shire of Carnarvon Art Gallery Team invites Expressions of Interest (EOI) from local catering businesses and food providers to supply catering services for the Gascoyne Art Awards 2026 Opening and Award Night. We are seeking a caterer who shares our passion for the Gascoyne region and can deliver a memorable culinary experience that celebrates the best of local produce. 1. Event Overview Event - Gascoyne Art Awards 2026- Opening & Award Night Date - 3 July 2026 |5.30pm-8pm Venue - Library & Art Gallery Expected Guest - 80 Event Duration - 2,5 hours Service Style - Tapas-style finger food & table grazing 2. What We Are Looking For We are seeking a caterer to provide a festive, sophisticated, and inclusive food experience across two service styles: A. Roaming Canapés - Waiter-Served Sophisticated Finger Food Elegant, bite-sized canapés circulated among guests by wait staff throughout the evening A minimum of 5–6 varieties rotating throughout service A balanced selection across seafood, meat, and vegan options B. Table Nibbles - Grazing Boards & Platters Beautifully presented grazing-style boards and platters placed on tables for guests to enjoy at leisure To include a mix of fresh fruit, vegetables, dips, breads, cheeses, charcuterie, and sweet bites Festive and visually appealing presentation 3. Key Requirements The successful caterer must address the following: Local Produce Focus Showcase the Gascoyne region - we want our guests to taste where they are Strong preference for locally sourced ingredients: Carnarvon tropical fruits & vegetables, Gascoyne pastoral meats, Shark Bay/Exmouth seafood, local honey, native ingredients, etc. Dietary Inclusivity Menu must cater for both vegetarians and meat lovers equally - not as an afterthought Style & Presentation • Tapas-style - fresh, vibrant, shareable • Festive - this is a celebration! The food should feel special and generous • Fresh - emphasis on quality, seasonal, and locally grown fruit and vegetables • Presentation should complement the artistic nature of the event 4. services to Be Included Please indicate in your response whether you can provide the following: • Full canapé and grazing menu (food preparation and supply) • Wait staff for roaming canapé service • Setup and styling of all table platters and grazing boards • All necessary servingware, platters, napkins, and utensils • Full pack-down and clean-up after the event • Food safety and hygiene compliance. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: For enquiries or further information, please contact: E: robertson.k@carnarvon.wa.gov.au What to Include in Your EOI Your Expression of Interest should include: • Business name and contact details • Brief overview of your business and relevant experience (particularly event/function catering) • Proposed sample menu addressing both service styles (roaming canapés and table nibbles) - including vegetarian, meat, and seafood options • Estimated cost per head (or total cost based on 80 guests) - broken down if possible • Staffing plan - number of staff provided (if applicable) • Any photos or references from previous events (if available) • Availability to cater on the event date How to submit: Please submit your Expression of Interest to: Email: artgallery@carnarvon.wa.gov.au In person: 3 Francis Street Attn. Keely Robertson

opportunities.description: EOI-01974 - Fire Gaseous Suppression - All Iron Ore and Dampier Salt Sites Scope of Work Rio Tinto Iron Ore (RTIO) is seeking Expressions of Interest (EOI) from qualified contractors for the provision of Fire Gaseous Suppression systems at brownfield sites, across the Pilbara mines and ports operations. This EOI intends to understand the capacity and capability of the Fire Gaseous Suppression systems market players, to support RTIO’s ongoing projects at brownfield sites, with a particular focus on the supply, installation, construction and/or commissioning. Geographically RTIO mine sites and ports are located across diverse regions: • East Pilbara • Central Pilbara • West Pilbara • Coastal Operations The EOI seeks to understand the capability of contractors who can perform the below works: To undertake IG541 & pre-engineered foam suppression system upgrades for 115 asset s across 11 RTIO sites including DSL (Dampier Salts Limited) Phase 1 installation works to be completed by Q4 2028. IFC package to be provided to successful contractor to supply and install. The current Gaseous suppression systems requiring upgrade to IG541 will mainly be FM200 systems but will include a small number of aerosol & C02 systems & a small number of HPU’s with FM200 systems will require upgrading to Pre-Engineered Foam Suppression systems. Phase 2 works to follow in FY28 for execution completion in 2030 and will be approximately 138 asset s across 11 RTIO sites including DSL (Dampier Salts Limited). Upgrades will include asset types of Substations, Switch rooms, Communications rooms, Server rooms & HPUs (Hydraulic Power Units) on fixed and balance machines. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: PCS-03843 - Data and Engagement services (Important) The Buyer is seeking a Seller to deliver data and engagement services to support its stakeholder engagement and digital service delivery functions as part of its website ecosystem. The Seller must: • Provide access to a platform that enables the Buyer to plan, deliver and manage community of interest processes under the Energy Industry Jobs Plan, as well as broader stakeholder engagement activities, including consultations surveys, media releases and stakeholder relationship management . • Provide implementation, configuration and onboarding services . • Ensure minimal disruption to existing services and continuity of data access . • Provide ongoing technical support, maintenance and system updates. The platform provided by the Seller must: • Provide tools to design, publish and manage consultations, surveys and engagement activities. • Enable segmentation and management of stakeholder group and Community of Interest cohorts. • Host all data onshore within Australia to ensure data sovereignty. • Comply with applicable Government security requirements and standards. • Integrate with the Buyer's existing website and digital systems. The platform should provide the Buyer with a consolidated, secure and efficient solution to manage stakeholder engagement activities and improve data driven decision making. Essential criteria 1. Capability of data to support compliance with applicable Government security requirements and information management obligations. 3. Whole of life costs. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Solution architect
opportunities.description: LH-06228 - 1 Solution Architect Job details This opportunity has been sent to selected suppliers only. Should DHA require other/further/new submissions DHA will make direct contact. Unsolicited emails will not be answered. Defence Housing Australia (DHA) provides quality housing and related services to Defence members and their families. In doing this, DHA supports the operational, recruitment and retention goals of the Department of Defence. DHA is a small to medium-sized government business enterprise (GBE) with approximately 600 staff across 19 offices around Australia, including three contact centres, located in three states. The Business Solutions and Technology (BS&T) team operates from DHA’s head office in Canberra. BS&T are responsible for all software related business analysis, business solutions, in-house software development, telephony, IT services and infrastructure. We deliver business as usual activities and innovation driven initiatives that provide efficiencies to the broader organisation. DHA is undertaking a significant forward technology program with a focus on ensuring fit for purpose technology across all business areas. As part of the large program of work, DHA requires a Solution Architect to lead in works relating to Salesforce CRM implementation and support transition and decommission of legacy applications to Cloud and SaaS products. DHA’s architects are responsible for the creation, design and management of all system architecture documentation, the design and consideration of future architecture states, and the Enterprise Architecture Tool. Key duties and responsibilities The respondent will undertake the following tasks under this engagement: 1. Working with Technology Business partners, the Architecture Forum and Multi-Disciplinary Teams (MDT) providing advice on current and new technology development and implementation considering future architecture requirements. 2. Apply technical knowledge of Salesforce, TechOne, .Net and Java (as currently used in bespoke enterprise systems) and supporting uplifting products and capabilities to the Cloud. Provide technical advice and solution design, ensuring fit for purpose and integration considerations" 3. The role will create and modify architecture artefacts as required, including high level and detailed designs, architectural diagrams, feasibility studies, mapping process/data flows, and delivery of functional designs for the following: • Deliver and present architectural solutions to support emerging business requirements, as well as review documents produced by DHA’s implementation partners. • Participate and guide in existing (legacy system) capability transfer to Salesforce and new systems. • Architect Cloud native solutions on Hyperscaler clouds such as Microsoft Azure and AWS. • Familiarity with Enterprise Service Bus (ESB) Patterns and MuleSoft. • Deliver and present architectural solutions to support emerging business requirements. • Contribute to design to support permanent fixes and changes relating to incident management and tactical operations projects. 4. Lead documenting current state architecture and the design and development of future state architecture and providing advice via the architecture forum. 5. Respondent will provide Architectural leadership for the Modernisation and Improvements program of work, including Salesforce implementation, FinTech and Data programs with a focus on simplicity of system design and seamless integration. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Multiple junior tester
opportunities.description: LH-06223 - Multiple Junior Tester Role Title: Manual Test Analyst SFIA Level: 2 - 4 A SFIA 2 IT Test Analyst tests the function and interfaces of the systems which make up the Department’s core business processing systems, sourcing and analysing design documents, and assists in the preparation, peer-review and execution of test scripts. A SFIA 2 IT Test Analyst works in an agile/iterative environment and utilises test management tools to prepare and execute test scripts, document results, analyse data, prepare test artefacts and escalate issues, defects and incidents in line with assigned work scope from the Scrum Master or Team Lead. They may be expected to lead sections of work within a release and be required to undertake specialist or technical research and analysis activities. They communicate across a range of media types, using various styles to suit different audiences and understand and embrace the agency’s various ways of working and are agile in their approach to collaboration and delivery of services . SFIA 4 IT Test Analyst A SFIA 4 IT Test Analyst leads a team to test the function and interfaces of the systems which make up the Department’s core business processing systems. They prepare, estimate, manage and review testing documentation, ensuring relevant strategies, plans follow best practice and management of risks are considered. They provide training on technical testing processes and work closely with other test analysts to achieve testing outcomes. A SFIA 4 IT Test Analyst works in an agile/iterative environment and undertakes specialist or technical research and analysis activities. They identify opportunities for automation within software processes, using scripts that test functionality through analysis, test preparation, test execution, defect management , and technical troubleshooting activities. They communicate across a range of media types, using various styles to suit different audiences and understand and embrace the agency’s various ways of working and are agile in their approach to collaboration and delivery of services . Key duties and responsibilities • Plan, design, execute and review testing activities in line with departmental test strategies, standards and quality frameworks. • Develop and maintain test plans, test cases, scripts, test data, environments and supporting documentation across water fall and Agile projects. • Manage defects through investigation, documentation, escalation and resolution in accordance with defect management processes. • Collaborate with business, technical and stakeholder groups to clarify requirements, validate system behaviour and coordinate UAT. • Provide testing estimates, progress reporting, metrics and quality assessments to support project planning and delivery. • Support production releases through test completion, sign‑off and assurance of test deliverables. • Contribute to continuous improvement of testing practices, tools and processes, and assist enterprise testing and QA activities. • Provide guidance, mentoring and knowledge transfer, ensuring documentation is stored in accordance with recordkeeping requirements. • Work with various testing methodologies including water fall and Agile • Adhere to the Defect management strategy to manage defects, gathering information for defect entries and identifying points of failure and organising resources in the investigation of issues • Contribute to and review testing artefacts, test specifications • Assist the Enterprise Testing and Quality Assurance section and other teams with testing activities. Required skills and experience: SFIA Level 2: Demonstrated experience (3+ years) in manual testing, including: • Desktop/Application-Seibel, .Net, (including D365), interagency, system integration, mobile device & browser stack testing. • Analysing documented specifications, user stories and requirements to support the creation of test cases • Write, review, and execute test cases. • Demonstrated ability to work under limited direction with competing priorities and be accountable for completion of work; • and Demonstrated ability to adapt and work in a constantly changing environment. • Report and track the resolution of defects/issues as they are found in testing. • Work with the ability to be proactive, flexible, and agile, whilst managing changing goals and scope in a multidisciplinary team. • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, and skilled at prioritisation and multi-tasking. • Ideally exposure to or previous experience in .Net, Seibel, Sales force applications • access ibility testing experience is desirable SFIA Level 4: Demonstrated experience (6+ years) as Senior Test Analyst, including: • Desktop/Application-Seibel, .Net, (including D365), interagency, system integration, mobile device & browser stack testing. • Analysing documented specifications, user stories and requirements to support the creation of test cases • Write, review, and execute test cases. • Demonstrated ability to work under limited direction with competing priorities and be accountable for completion of work • Demonstrated ability to adapt and work in a constantly changing environment. • Report and track the resolution of defects/issues as they are found in testing. • Work with the ability to be proactive, flexible, and agile, whilst managing changing goals and scope in a multidisciplinary team. • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, and skilled at prioritisation and multi-tasking. • Ideally exposure to or previous experience in .Net, Seibel, Sales force applications • access ibility testing experience is desirable. Qualifications ISTQB Foundation Certificate or equivalent certificate, or relevant practical experience TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Solution Design describes the function of designing and delivering new functional solutions and providing ongoing operational support for ICT solutions. Senior Solution Design Managers are responsible for driving competitive advantage for the agency, helping to define and articulate the strategy for a designated industry or solution area. They are responsible for the delivery of all ICT application services necessary to achieve strategic objectives and lead the deployment of future functionality and enhancements to existing ICT solutions. Senior Solution Design Managers operate with a significant degree of independence and work under the broad guidance of a National Manager to deliver quality outcomes. opportunities.title: Enhancing planthopper management in lychee
opportunities.description: Horticulture Innovation Australia (Hort Innovation) is a not-for-profit, grower-owned Research and Development Corporation (RDC) for Australia’s $17 billion horticulture industry. Hort Innovation invests around $170 million in research and development (R&D) and marketing programs annually to provide benefit to industry and the wider community. Hort Innovation is seeking a suitably qualified, experienced and capable delivery partner for the project: Enhancing planthopper management in lychee (LY25002). The objectives of the services being sought are to: • Enhance the management of flatid planthopper within lychee • Understand current pest management practices of lychee growers and relevant farm management practices • Describe the economic impact and extent of flatid planthopper as an issue for Australian lychee production • Identify key trends, knowledge gaps and insights associated with the management of flatid planthopper • Deliver key information to lychee growers to enhance the management of flatid planthopper • Respond to key research/extension opportunities to support the lychee industry in responding to flatid planthopper Hort Innovation has prepared a Request for Proposal (RFP) document that provides background information, outlines the scope of services , the procurement process and the information required from interested parties. The RFP may be downloaded from www.tenders.net (you will need to be a member of tenders.net to access this information). The closing date for responses is 13/05/2026 at 3:00 PM (AEST). opportunities.title: Managing contractor for Economic and Social Infrastructure Program (ESIP)
opportunities.description: Papua New Guinea --- DFAT 1039 (DFAT-1039) - Economic and Social Infrastructure Program 2 (ESIP 2) Papua New Guinea is a longstanding partner and close neighbour to Australia, as well as a key actor in regional stability and economic connectivity. Yet, Papua New Guinea faces acute infrastructure gaps that constrain economic growth, equitable human development and climate resilience. Accelerating access to high-quality, fit for purpose, and sustainable infrastructure is critical to PNG’s prosperity and to meeting its development goals. It also supports resilience to security challenges in areas such as climate change and cyber security . Infrastructure delivered through the Australian aid program can help create jobs, enhance skills development and generate additional economic activity for local businesses and supply chains. It can also underpin stronger local capacity for infrastructure prioritisation, planning and delivery working in partnership with targeted ministries and State Owned Enterprises (SOEs). This approach provides tangible and lasting benefits to Papua New Guinea, supports economic growth and service delivery and delivers on a priority area for the PNG Government in support of the overall bilateral relationship. DFAT has a requirement for a Managing Contractor for the PNG Economic and Social Infrastructure Program 2 (ESIP 2) - an 8-year program worth AUD 800 million. It is expected ESIP 2 could deliver the following End of Programs Outcomes: 1. Targeted Ministries and SOEs demonstrate strengthened institutional capacity for quality infrastructure policy, planning, procurement, financing, management , maintenance , and coordination with relevant private sector entities. 2. Women, people with disabilities, and marginalised groups experience more equitable, inclusive, and safe access to, participation in, and benefit from, ESIP2-funded economic and social infrastructure. 3. Social and economic infrastructure delivered through ESIP2 demonstrates high standards of climate change and disaster resilience, supporting GoPNG to more sustainably plan, deliver, and maintain infrastructure. In addition to a focus on the power and telecommunications sectors, ESIP2 is expected to support social infrastructure and capacity building for infrastructure delivery, sectoral planning and management . It will also deliver infrastructure, and maintenance where necessary, to contribute to the goals and objectives of other Australian sectoral and place-based programs. Timeframe for Delivery: Four years plus option/s period of up to four years Estimated Value (AUD):From to $800,000,000.00. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Human centred design exploration
opportunities.description: PCS-03818 - Human Centred Design Exploration: Investors in Australian Critical Minerals Opportunities Requirements Geoscience Australia is seeking a Human Centred Design (HCD) team to work with our Minerals, Energy and Ground water Division and with our Digital Experience team to create an actionable understanding of current and potential financial investors in the Australian critical mineral sector, including their motivations and information needs. This body of work will contribute to defining digital products related to critical minerals and inform Geoscience Australia’s HCD digital service experience strategy. The results of this work will be a typology of financial investors, and their information needs that will provide insight on: WHAT: Current state and size of the investment ecosystem and stakeholders in financial investment in the Australian critical mineral sector including relevant geo-political, macro-economic, and technological factors that determine and shape investment. This ecosystem should include the tripartite approach of raising funds by mining-government investment mechanisms and equipment manufacturers. HOW: Common investment processes, decision points, risk considerations, and key milestones that shape financial involvement in Australian critical mineral opportunities as they move from early, speculative exploration through to mature, commercially viable resource projects. WHO: Types and proportions of investors in this ecosystem, and when, how, and with what criteria and information different types of investors choose to invest (or divest) in opportunities across the development pipeline. This ecosystem involves banks, equity firms, private capital, major mining companies, stockbrokers, and government finance agencies. WHY: Understanding the drivers of investor behaviour and the criteria and information used by specific investors at each stage of development GAP: What data or information could improve and promote confident decision-making in Australian critical minerals projects, but are currently unavailable, difficult to source, or otherwise challenging to incorporate and/or communicate? What role do investors see for Geoscience Australia in providing such data or information? Activities: • 1 Kick off session with project sponsors • Conduct user experience research including qualitative and quantitative methods suitable to addressing the project goals, such as through interviews, qualitative surveys, workshops and desktop research. • Creation and delivery of research data documents such as transcripts, workshop reports, bibliographies from desktop research • Data analysis and insights generation • Create typology artefacts (see deliverables below) • 2 sessions in which draft analysis and artifacts are shared with project sponsors • 2 sessions presenting final analysis and materials to project sponsors Artifacts and deliverables: • Qualitative and Quantitative Research data documents including transcripts, session notes, workshop reports, bibliographies and the like. • Insights report that pulls together and provides overarching narrative and context for typology artifacts and identifies challenges in data availability, sourcing, formatting, etc. • that if addressed could provide more certainty related to investments. • Current state ecosystem and stakeholder maps of financial investment in the Australian critical mineral sector • Australian critical mineral project development journey maps for archetypal mineral resources, characterising common financial investment activities and decisions from pre-competitive geoscience data creation and delivery, through to mature commercially viable projects • Investor Archetypes including when, how, and with what criteria and information they choose to invest (or divest) in opportunities across the development pipeline • Investor Archetypes journey maps that detail investment decision milestones, the criteria and information that is used, that is available (but not used), that is not available (and desired), across the development pipeline. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: LH-06233 - 1 Program Manager Job details The Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (DAFF) is seeking an experienced Program Manager or senior Project Manager to lead the delivery of ICT Networks projects within the ICT services Division (ISD). ISD is responsible for managing and delivering Information and Communications Technology (ICT) functions across Australia and internationally, ensuring a secure, modern ICT environment that enables the department to operate 24/7 and achieve its strategic objectives. As part of the Capital security Technology and asset Refresh (CapSTAR) Program, ISD requires a Program Manager to oversee the Network Edge sub-stream and its associated projects. This role will focus on refreshing ageing network infrastructure by implementing supportable devices and modernised network capabilities. Key objectives include enhancing network management and monitoring, as well as expanding Wi-Fi coverage across DAFF sites to improve connectivity for staff. Key duties and responsibilities The successful candidate will be responsible for: Governance & assurance – Ensure delivery aligns with ICT governance, policies, regulatory requirements, and stage gate approvals across network and edge programs. Stakeholder alignment – Engage business, product, security , and technical stakeholders to define and prioritise requirements aligned to network strategy and cyber security objectives. Executive reporting – Produce concise, high-quality reporting and governance artefacts with clear visibility of progress, risks, dependencies, and decisions. Technical coordination – Lead cross-functional coordination across engineering, architecture, infrastructure, security , and vendors to align design, integration, and delivery. Financial management – Own budget end-to-end, including forecasting, cost control , and variance management ; ensure delivery within approved funding and drive cost optimisation and commercial discipline. Integrated delivery control – Maintain oversight of schedule, budget, risks, issues, and dependencies, ensuring alignment to baselines across multi-site deployments. Change & risk management – Govern change and proactively manage risks and issues, ensuring impacts to design, security , budget, and delivery are assessed and control led. Delivery planning – Drive integrated plans across network, security , and infrastructure, actively managing dependencies such as carriage, site readiness, and vendor inputs. Vendor management – Manage vendors and partners to deliver against scope, service levels, quality, timeframes, and commercial obligations. Operational readiness – Ensure effective transition to operations, including service acceptance, documentation, and support readiness. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Engineering services - prototype biosecurity boot-hygiene stations
opportunities.description: Householder – Request for quotation — Engineering services : Design and construction of prototype Bio security boot-hygiene stations The Lord Howe Island Board (LHIB) are seeking quotations from skilled local contractors to modify two existing boot-hygiene stations. These modified stations will serve as prototypes for a proposed upgrade to all the stations on the island. Boot-hygiene stations are a key part of the Board’s bio security program. They help control the spread of potentially harmful pathogens across the island. They must be effective, easy to use and durable to ensure a high level of compliance by users. Scope of Works The required services include, but are not limited to: • Designing modifications to one large station and one small station (see Figures 1-4 via the link provided). • Consultation with LHIB during the design stage to ensure the proposed modifications meet functional and operational bio security requirements. • Fabrication primarily with marine grade aluminium/ stainless steel and other materials if required. • Installation of the stations for a trial period. • Further modification of the prototype if required. Project Timeline LHIB anticipates that the prototype stations will be designed, fabricated and installed in 2026. The exact timing will be agreed with the successful contractor following evaluation of submissions. Evaluation of Submissions The Board will evaluate submissions against the following measurable criteria, which must be addressed in the submission: • Design quality and suitability Demonstrated alignment of the proposed design approach with functional, operational, and bio security requirements, for one large and one small boot hygiene station. • Engineering and fabrication experience Evidence of relevant engineering and aluminium fabrication experience. • Value for money Demonstrated cost effectiveness of the quoted price for the full scope of works (design and construct) compared with other conforming submissions. • Licences and insurance Provision of current and valid evidence of a relevant business licence, public liability and workers compensation insurances. • Delivery timeframe Likelihood of delivery within the agreed timeframe, assessed by a detailed project schedule with key milestones to meet the required completion date - end 2026. Work Health and Safety: The contractor must comply with all applicable work health and safety legislation and industry standards. The contractor must comply with LHIB site-specific safety requirements and relevant LHIB Job Safety Analyses where applicable to the works. Approval of Works On completion of the works, the contractor must notify LHIB and provide evidence that the work is complete. Payment will follow confirmation that the works have been completed in accordance with the agreed scope. Any design concepts, drawings or prototype modifications developed as part of this engagement will become the intellectual property of the Lord Howe Island Board. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Darryl Birch Team Leader Biosecurity E: darryl.birch@lhib.nsw.gov.au Krissy Ward Senior Environment Manager E: kristine.ward@lhib.nsw.gov.au P: 02 6563 2066 Darryl Birch, Team Leader Biosecurity, is available to discuss the proposed design parameters and arrange a field visit to the stations in situ. This will occur on receipt of the submissions. Email your submission to: Administration Office E: administration@lhib.nsw.gov.au Use the subject line: RFQ – Engineering Services: Design and construction of prototype Biosecurity boot-hygiene stations. Attention: Darryl Birch The Lord Howe Island Board is not bound to accept the lowest-priced or any submission. LHIB reserves the right to discontinue the process or seek further information from respondents at any time.

opportunities.title: Systems frameworks (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Sydney Metro West - Systems Frameworks Package Honeywell has been awarded the system framework contract for design, supply, installation and maintenance of below systems for Sydney Metro West project: • Building management control Systems to supervise operation of the Sydney Metro West stations; and • Electronic access control Systems to manage staff security access to operational areas of the stations • Closed Circuit Television Systems (CCTV System) to ensure safe, fast and reliable services , • Fire Detection Systems to ensure safety across the alignment. • Public Address Systems to keep customers informed at all times. • Passenger Information display Systems providing display boards with up-to-date trip information. Project location and context The Sydney Metro West project is a new 24-kilometre underground metro railway which will double rail capacity between Parramatta and the Sydney CBD, transforming Sydney for generations to come. This once-in-a-century infrastructure investment will provide fast, reliable turn-up-and-go metro services with fully access ible stations, link new communities to rail services and support employment growth and housing supply Stations have been confirmed at Westmead, Parramatta, Sydney Olympic Park, North Strathfield, Burwood North, Five Dock, The Bays, Pyrmont and Hunter Street in the Sydney CBD. Sydney Metro West will target an opening date of 2032. === WORK PACKAGES Work Package - EOI Closes Building management control System (BMCS) - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026) Closed-circuit television (CCTV) system - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026) Electronic access control System (EACS) - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026) Fire Detection - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026) Passenger Information display System (PIDS) - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026) Public Address and Voice Alarm (PAVA) system - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026) Warehouse - 01 Jul 2026 (EOI opens 20 Apr 2026). TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: MAAS-RFQ 26/007 - Powerhouse Exhibition fit out - Task Eternal - Exhibition Showcase Description The Museum of Applied Arts and Sciences (Powerhouse) seeks Requests for Quote (RFQ) for the delivery of exhibition fit out services for the Powerhouse Parramatta opening exhibitions. Project goals Powerhouse is producing Task Eternal, the largest exhibition for the opening of Powerhouse Parramatta in late 2026. Powerhouse seeks to engage a suitably qualified supplier or suppliers to undertake design development, supply, manufacture/fabricate fit-out elements to house exhibition collection objects and commissions. The service requirement includes: • Pre-production and briefing with Powerhouse’s Technical Production team and Design and Collections team. • Prototyping/testing. • Samples, prototype, finishes sign off by Powerhouse at specified hold points. • Design development. • Shop Drawings & 2 x Powerhouse review cycles • Construction, fabrication and/or supply of furniture, fixtures and equipment. • Supply and installation of fabricated elements. • Documentation to enable the Powerhouse to deconstruct or remove the elements at the end of the exhibition. • Submission of final designs for sign off by Powerhouse teams and/or stakeholder(s). Scope The scope of works includes the following item: Exhibition Showcase The supply and installation of a showcase for an exhibition scene for Task Eternal as per the drawing package at Attachment A, (to be issued following execution of a signed NDA) including: • supply and installation of an exhibition showcase for an exhibition scene for Task Eternal • any associated engineering or structural certification • Shop drawing production & 2 x Powerhouse review cycles. • In person review by Powerhouse teams for design & technical. • delivery and installation at Powerhouse Parramatta • provision of install and deinstall documentation • adherence to conserv ation requirements as detailed on the plan All elements must be delivered by September 2026. opportunities.title: Weir removal
opportunities.description: P_2620822 - Buckenbowra Weir Removal Description Demolish & appropriately dispose of a failing weir structure from within Buckenbowra River, near Batemans Bay. Works include localised regrading of river sands to tie into surrounding river bed levels. Project goals Weir removal will free movement of water and sediments, along with improved fish passage within the Buckenbowra River. Removing the weir will return this section of the Buckenbowra River back to its former condition . Scope The weir is approximately 1 metre high and comprises 2 rows of sheet metal piling core, loose rock filling, gabion basket scour protection and a concrete capping. A concrete and aluminium fishway is present on the western side of the structure adjacent to drop boards that can be used to lower water levels in the weir pool prior to initiation of works. An engineering assessment (obtained by the Principal) will determine if weir abutments will need to be removed or can remain and be made safe as part of these works. Unless abutments are retained, all weir components should be removed and disposed of appropriately. The structure has deteriorated, with scouring on the downstream side of the weir exposing the sheet metal piling and causing loss and deterioration of gabion rock baskets. Following removal of weir components, sediment present upstream of the weir will need to be regraded to meet downstream river bed levels on a low gradient to prevent erosion of the river bed. The site is access ible via Quart Pot Road on the eastern side of Buckenbowra River. The western side is not access ible. An intended process would be to set up erosion and sediment control s, slowly lower weir pool levels by sequential removal of drop boards. Working from the western bank back to the eastern bank to remove weir components, then regrading river bed across the weir location. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Wind / solar farm & battery project (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Pottinger Energy Park - Wind Farm, Solar Farm, and Battery (BESS) Project Pottinger Renewables Pty Ltd is a partnership between Someva Renewables and AGL Energy. Someva Renewables is a NSW family-owned, Australian based company, who has spent the past two years measuring the wind and solar asset s in the Hay and Edward River Local Government Areas, designing the development and consulting with community. AGL operates Australia's largest private electricity generation portfolio, and as the second-oldest listed company in Australia, has a 185-year history of operation. The Pottinger Energy Park is a GW-scale wind farm, solar farm and battery energy storage system project. It is located in the outer regional areas of NSW, approximately 60km south of Hay and 110km north of Deniliquin in the rural locality of Booroorban. The project is located entirely within the South West Renewable Energy Zone. The associated infrastructure includes: - Wind turbines and their hardstands - Solar panels, single-axis trackers and medium voltage power stations - Battery energy storage systems - Internal access tracks - Public road upgrades - 330 kV/ 33 kV substations - 330 kV switchyard - 330 kV overhead transmission line - 33 kV underground cables - Permanent Operations and maintenance building - Temporary construction compounds, concrete batching plants, accommodation The project is estimated to provide Full Time Equivalent (FTE) employment for up to 900 personnel during construction and 80 personnel during operations. Construction of the project may commence in late 2026 pending the project's success in gaining South West Renewable Energy Zone access Rights through a tender process with EnergyCo. One of the key merit criteria is likely to be centred on Regional Development and Local Supply Chain. As such, we particularly seek the following details within your expressions of interest: - The extent (percentage) of steel products and components produced or sourced using locally (Australian) milled steel. - Number of 'learning workers'* you have and/or will likely have by late 2026, including a description of any strategies you have in place here as a company. - Number of 'underrepresented groups'** you have and/or will likely have by late 2026, including a description of any strategies you have in place here as a company. No main contractors have been engaged at this stage. It is expected that most of these work packages will be available through the main contractors. View Project --- Work Package - Full Scope Closes Grouting - Wind Turbine Towers - 31 Jul 2026 Construction Material Testing - 31 Jul 2026 Temporary Accommodation - 31 Jul 2026 All capabilities not specified elsewhere - 31 Jul 2026 Crane and Lifting Equipment Hire and Operators - 31 Jul 2026 Environment and Sediment control - 31 Jul 2026 High Voltage Commisioning and testing - 31 Jul 2026 Mechanical and electrical Installation - 31 Jul 2026 Temporary Site Compound - services - 31 Jul 2026 Logistics - 31 Jul 2026 Piling - 31 Jul 2026 Fencing - 31 Jul 2026 Site Investigations and Surveying - 31 Jul 2026 Civil and Earthworks Subcontractors - 31 Jul 2026 Shuttle Bus - 31 Jul 2026 Overhead Power Lines - 31 Jul 2026 Low Voltage electrical and Safety Testing - 31 Jul 2026 electrical Wholesalers - 31 Jul 2026 Diesel Generators and Supply - 31 Jul 2026 Concrete - 31 Jul 2026 Permanent Operations and maintenance Building - 31 Jul 2026 Quarry Products - 31 Jul 2026 Steel Supply and Formwork and other fabricated steel products - 31 Jul 2026 Underground Cabling - 31 Jul 2026 water Cart - 31 Jul 2026 TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Renewable energy infrastructure (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Central-West Orana Renewable Energy Zone (REZ) The Central-West Orana REZ will be serviced by new transmission network infrastructure, including high-capacity transmission lines and energy hubs, which will transfer power generated by solar and wind farms to electricity consumers. This infrastructure will be critical for the successful operation of the REZ. EnergyCo is leading the development of the REZ transmission network infrastructure in its capacity as the Infrastructure Planner for the REZs in NSW, and is responsible for developing and overseeing the planning and approval processes for the REZ network infrastructure. EnergyCo has selected ACE Energy as the first ranked proponent as Network Operator for the Central-West Orana REZ. ACE Energy is sponsored by Acciona, Cobra and Endeavour Energy. As the network operator, ACE Energy will be responsible for designing, financing, building, operating and maintaining the network infrastructure for the clean energy zone. The infrastructure will include energy hubs and new high-capacity transmission lines. Centred around the town of Dubbo, the Central-West Orana REZ was originally expected to deliver 3 GW of new solar, wind and storage capacity but that appears certain to be revised. === WORK PACKAGES Work Package - Full Scope Closes CWO-T-EHB.143 - Substations Test Equipment - 07 May 2026 CWO-T-EHB.155 - Transformer Oil Separation Tank - 07 May 2026 CWO-T-EHB.159 - Switching Station - Hydraulic services - Septic and Storm water services - 07 May 2026 CWO-T-EHB.157 - Firefighting System - 07 May 2026 CWO-T-EHB.135.4 - Preassembly and Erection of Gantry - Elong Elong and Switching Stations - 07 May 2026. opportunities.title: Tunnel works (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Western Harbour Tunnel Stage 2 Overview The Western Harbour Tunnel Project (WHT) includes 6.5 kilometres of dual three-lane tunnels, stretching from the Warringah Freeway in Cammeray, across Sydney Harbour, to the WestConnex interchange at Rozelle, alleviating congestion on the Anzac Bridge, Sydney Harbour Bridge and Sydney Harbour Tunnel. The Tunnel will be delivered in two packages: - Stage 1 (delivered by John Holland CPB Contractors) includes excavation and partial civil fit out of the southern section of the tunnel between Rozelle and Birchgrove. - Stage 2 (this project) includes the construction of approximately 4.8 kilometres of tunnels from under Birchgrove, through to the Warringah Freeway near North Sydney and tunnelling under Sydney Harbour. Stage 2 also includes the remaining civil fit out of the Stage 1 section, surface works at the North Sydney end of the tunnel, and mechanical and electrical fit out for the entire 6.5km length of the tunnel. ACCIONA Construction Australia Pty Ltd has been appointed by Transport for NSW to deliver Stage 2 of the WHT Project. ACCIONA is proposing that roadheaders will be used to excavate the tunnels on the northern side of Sydney Harbour between North Sydney and Balls Head. Two tunnel boring machines (TBMs) will be used to excavate the remainder of the tunnels from Birchgrove on the southern side of the Harbour through to a cavern at Balls Head where they will meet the northern roadheader tunnels. The Stage 2 tunnelling works are outline in Figure 1. The orange lines indicate the TBM tunnels and the caverns where the TBMs will be launched and retrieved. While the light blue lines indicate the roadheader tunnels. The dark blue lines indicate the extent of Stage 1 works. Figure 2 demonstrates the tunnel vertical alignment from south at Birchgrove to north at Warrigah Freeway. Further information on the project is available at nswroads.work/whtportal with an interactive portal here to provide area specific information to help you navigate the project. === WORK PACKAGES Contract - Full Scope Closes Civil fitout RW0560 Cammeray Completions Civil Work - 30 Apr 2026 RW0086 Signage - 30 Apr 2026 RW0684 Paving and Curb Install - 30 Apr 2026 RW0689 Wayfinding Playground Furniture Installation - 30 Apr 2026 RW0446d Drainage and services - 30 Apr 2026 RW0683 Form, reo and pour (FRP) - 30 Apr 2026 RW0688 linemark ing and Signage - 30 Apr 2026 RW0266 CCTV Jet Cleaning - 30 Apr 2026 RW0074 Ernest Street Bridge Hard landscap ing - 30 Apr 2026 RW0838 CCTV Cameras in Tunnel - 30 Apr 2026 Project Wide RW0686 landscap ing and Planting - 30 Apr 2026. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Haemodialysis services, equipment & consumables
opportunities.description: NTG26-0001 - All of NT - Supply and Delivery of Haemodialysis services , Equipment and Consumables for a Period of 60 Months The Department of Health is seeking a single entity to provide Haemodialysis services across the Northern Territory. The requirement is for patient treatment in urban dialysis centres, remote communities in renal facilit ies and patient homes. The capability of providing Plasma Exchange (PE) services in the Darwin and Alice Springs region is also included in this tender. Offers must include as a minimum but not limited to the following: Supply, delivery, installation and commissioning of: · haemodialysis machines (HD) with haemodiafiltration capability. · Home Haemodialysis machines. · Able to monitor effective dialysis dose, access flow and recirculation. · water treatment plants (reverse osmosis (RO) units and all components of pre-filtration required for dialysis). · Individual portable reverse osmosis filtration units. · water chillers for ROs and portable ROs. · Dialysis appropriate chairs. · Bedside trolleys. · Device integration with NT Health electronic patient record – Acacia. Ongoing service provision including but not limited to: · Equipment maintenance . · Onsite Technical Support · Technical backup. · Spare parts. · water testing to demonstrate safe water for dialysis. · Dialysis technology. · Supply of all consumables. Katherine Dialysis Clinic, 122 Casuarina Street Katherine East NT 0850, and Alice Springs Dialysis Clinic , 33 Gap Road, Alice Springs NT 0870 are excluded from this tender. These services are currently fully outsourced to a private service provider Price Range Estimated at $70M - $90M. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Technology services
opportunities.description: RFT0577 - All Centres - Provision of Technology services for a Period of 60 Months Power and water ’s Technology landscap e comprises of Information and Communications Technology (ICT) and Operational Technology (OT) environments (applications, systems, infrastructure, network, hardware). Power and water operates critical infrastructure and requires access to a broad range of specialist and generalist technology capabilities to support business operations, regulatory obligations, cyber security requirements, and long-term digital transformation objectives. Power and water is seeking to engage suitably qualified and experienced technology service providers for strategic partnerships for multiple categories within Technology. Power and water aims to modify its approach to sourcing technology services from bespoke contracts for small bundles of work to enterprise-level strategic arrangements across ICT and OT environments. This Request for Tender (RFT) is an open-market engagement, offering an opportunity to small-to-large technology service providers to participate and provide best-in-class technology and commercial solutions to Power and water . This Request for Tender (RFT) covers five categories across ICT and OT services . The anticipated spend across the categories depends on the supplier capabilities and the final scope awarded to the suppliers. The tentative breakdown of the anticipated spend is as follows: Category A Application maintenance and Support (AMS) $5 – 15m Suppliers’ capabilities will determine the applications, projects and licenses Category B Core Capability Program (CCP) $10 – 20m (for 2 years) Suppliers’ capabilities and delivery model for Oracle, Maximo and future projects will determine the actuals Category C Infrastructure and Database services (IDS) $1 – 5m Support for BAU work and project support will determine the actuals Category D Cyber security (CS) $10 – 20m (for 2 years) Contingent on final requirements for SP-2 capability uplift Category E OT & SCADA services and Equipment Supply (OT & SCADA) $7 – 10m Contingent on future projects pipeline The numbers above are indicative based on the current spend, future projects pipeline and changing technology landscap e at Power and water .
