opportunities.title: Structural improvements to posts and slab
opportunities.description: TC26011 - Lot 181 Kubin Road, St Pauls Structural Improvements to Posts and Slab REPLACE HANDRAILS TO STAIRS AND TOP LANDING WITH NEW HOT DIPPED GALVANISED HANDRAILS: Handrails to consist of top and bottom rails and R12 vertical BARS @ 125 mm Centres. Handrails shall be fully welded and hot dipped galvanised. Site bolting to connect larger sections. On site welding of galvanised sections is not permitted. Avoid paint finishes over galvanising. Handrails shall comply with NCC Volume Two. Handrails shall be securely fixed with appropriate galvanised coach screws and bolts. Install hardwood trimmer/blocking joist as required ensuring adequate fixings. Patch and paint all bare and disturbed surfaces affected by the handrails. REPLACE TOP LANDING AND SUBFRAME WITH NEW: (1.2m x 1.2m) Remove and dispose of existing top landing and subframe. Fix bearers to stumps, and appropriately fix joists to bearers at 450mm MAX. spacings. Lay 90 x 19mm hardwood decking to joists using 50mm galvanised fixings. Deck must comply with all current and relevant Australian Standards and Building Codes. REPLACE BOTTOM CONCRETE LANDING WITH NEW: Demolish and remove existing bottom concrete landing. Concrete landing to be 1300mm wide x 1300mm long broomed concrete and to include: Clearing of grass as necessary to complete the work. Excavation of footings/thickenings and top soil (remove a minimum depth of 100mm of soil).Supply and placement of formwork, compacted fill, water proof membrane, footing and slab reinforcing (F72) Mesh, bar chairs, steel dowels to join to existing slabs (600mm centres), and perimeter bar (N12).Concrete and any other items necessary to complete the work. Concrete is to be steel trowelled and broom finished. Finished concrete to ensure surface water drainage from the building. Ensuring a smooth transition from existing concrete. Concrete shall be 25MPa. (U.N.O)All trade related waste to be removed upon completion of works, all disturbed surfaces to be levelled and raked to natural contours of the land. opportunities.description: EOI - UMI Arts Big Talk One Fire Cultural Festival 2026 Celebrating decades of First Nations culture Celebrating almost two decades of First Nations culture, UMI Arts Big Talk, One Fire Festival will return to Munro Martin Parklands on October 24. The free event is expected to bring together more than 3000 people for a family friendly celebration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander arts, culture, music and storytelling. UMI Arts artistic director Lisa Michl Komanggén said Big Talk One Fire was shaped by the people who take part, creating a space where culture is both celebrated and experienced. Expressions of interest are open for First Nations contributors across the program, including stage performers, market stallholders, workshop facilit ators and food vendors. Guided by the festival’s philosophy, Big Talk: real talking, real listening; One Fire: coming together with the spirit of community, the event continues to provide a colourful, welcoming and inclusive platform where culture is shared, stories are told, and connections are made across generations. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Gated parking Proof of Concept
opportunities.description: TL26-00294. Gated Parking proof of Concept (PoC) for public transport park and ride sites opportunity. This proof of Concept (PoC) has been initiated to evaluate proposed technical solutions and the associated costs for deploying a gated parking system at Park and Ride facilit ies. These facilit ies currently operate with open access and serve both public transport users and general parking customers. However, this unrestricted model has led to a recurring issue; public transport users often struggle to find available parking spaces, especially at high-demand locations such as those located near hospitals and shopping centres. To address this, the proposed solution will explore gated access that enables free parking for users who have recently tapped a valid go card or credit card on a public transport trip. This approach ensures that genuine transit users are supported in their journey while maintaining access for non-public transport customers through the possibility of introducing a nominal parking fee. The goal is to improve parking availability for commuters, encourage public transport usage, and better manage overall demand at key Park and Ride locations. This engagement is for a proof of concept as a service. The Customer does not intend to purchase any hardware or software and the proof of concept should be fully packaged and delivered by the Supplier. The key desired outcome from the PoC is lessons learned and recommendations report developed in collaboration with the Customer based on the outcomes of the proof of concept exercise. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Regional Arts Development Fund
opportunities.description: Regional Arts Development Fund The Regional Arts Development Fund (RADF) is a collaborative initiative between the Queensland Government, through Arts Queensland, and Gladstone Regional Council. RADF is dedicated to promoting the vital role of arts, culture, and heritage in fostering diverse, inclusive communities and strengthening regions. The RADF objectives are to support arts and cultural activities that increase access to experiences in regional Queensland, grow employment and capacity-building opportunities for artists, and deliver on local priorities while promoting the value of arts, culture, and heritage. Funding Priorities Gladstone Regional Council’s priorities for RADF are as follows: Creative Life - An active, vibrant arts and cultural sector with opportunities for all community members to participate according to their needs, interests and abilities Unified, Connected Community - A cohesive community that values and celebrates its way of life, civic occasions and achievements Animated Places and Spaces - Engaging and appealing public spaces and access to quality arts and cultural facilit ies Valued History and Heritage - A strong sense of history through the preservation, promotion and visibility of the region’s rich natural and cultural heritage Partnership and Collaboration - Productive partnerships and alliances within and external to the Region Cultural Citizenship - Sound community-based leadership of a well-coordinated arts and cultural sector Cultural and Creative Enterprise - An arts and cultural sector that contributes to a dynamic, resilient local economy Funding Rounds The timeframes for each round is based on the financial year. There are two rounds per financial year. Significant for each application, is to adhere to the due dates as no amendments or late submissions will be accepted past the due date. It may take up to 8 weeks from the due date for the application to be approved. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Gas project (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Arrow Energy Overview Arrow Energy operates four gas fields in Queensland's Surat basin. Surat Basin Arrow Energy has operated in the Surat Basin for more than 15 years. It is now the site of the new Surat Gas Project (SGP). The SGP is underpinned by a December 2017 deal with the Shell QGC-operated joint venture. This deal has allowed Arrow to take advantage of existing QGC-operated infrastructure to reduce our impacts on landholders and communities. Following our Shareholders' investment announcement, Arrow sanctioned the first phase of the SGP in April 2020. Phase one includes more than 600 wells to be drilled from 2020, focusing on an expansion between Arrow's current operational areas at Daandine and Tipton. This initial phase included construction of Inlet Processing facilit ies (IPFs) at Shell-QGC operated David and Harry Field Compression Stations. IPFs enable Arrow to supply gas from its operational fields under the gas sales agreement. Arrow will continue to use its existing gas compression facilit ies at Daandine and Tipton, with upgrades to the Tipton Central Gas Processing facilit y. Phase one also include upgrades and connection to water treatment facilit ies. In addition to using Arrow's existing water treatment facilit ies which will be improved through upgrades to Arrow's Tipton and Daandine water Treatment Plants, Arrow will send water to the Shell-QGC operated Kenya water Treatment facilit y. Two new brine storage ponds were constructed at the facilit y to support water treatment for beneficial use. Phase one is now in the final stages of completion with Phase two scheduled to begin in 2024 pending final shareholder investment decision. Phase two development will expand into new gas fields in the northern section of Arrow's tenure north-east of Miles. Construction is scheduled to commence from late 2024, and will bring an additional 130TJ/day of gas to market over 27 years, and will include but is not limited to: • Approximately 250 new gas wells around the Miles region including gathering network. • Construction of a new Field Compression Station (FCS) and a Steel Export Pipeline. • Construction of a Metering & Flare Package at the existing Shell-QGC operated McNulty FCS. • Construction of a new Hybrid Power Station under an Independent Power Producer Agreement. Subsequent phases Arrow's future plans include: • Additional wells and associated gathering to expand across Arrow's Surat tenure areas • construction of another new Field Compression Station at Lynwood, south of Arrow's Tipton development • construction of another Inlet Processing facilit y (IPFs) at the Shell-QGC operated Jammat FCS. More information is available from www.arrowenergy.com.au === WORK PACKAGES Work Package - Close Date Design and Construct - Arrow Concentrated Brine Dam 2 - 01 May 2026 TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: Meet the Supplier - Sustainability and Innovation Overview ICN SA is planning a 'Meet the Supplier' event for suppliers to pitch / showcase their innovative products or services to buyers. The "Meet the Supplier - Innovation and Sustainability" event is designed for suppliers whose products, services or technologies can deliver sustainability benefits to South Australia's major projects. Suppliers are invited to express interest if they offer innovative solutions in any of the following areas: Electrification Solutions that contribute to emissions reduction and support the transition to low-carbon operations, for example by providing equipment that uses electricity rather than petroleum-based fuels. Examples could include earthmoving equipment, forklifts and back-up energy generation. Alternative Fuels Technologies that replace traditional petroleum-based fuels with low-carbon alternatives such as biofuels (e.g. biodiesel, bioethanol), hydrogen or battery storage systems. These solutions can include mobility applications that use low-carbon fuels. Other Innovative Sustainable Goods and services Suppliers with technologies or services that offer sustainability benefits for infrastructure, mining, or energy projects not included in the categories above. Event Format Selected suppliers will be invited to deliver a 3-minute pitch showcasing how their offering contributes to sustainability in major projects. Proposed event date: Tuesday 25 November 2025, between 9:15 to 11:30 Major project buyers will receive targeted invitations. If additional spaces are available, invitations will be sent to ICN SA's general mailing list. === WORK PACKAGES Contract - Full Scope Closes Tradeshows Sustainability Sustainability Solutions - Meet the Supplier (Round 2) - 01 May 2026 Innovation Innovative Solutions - Meet the Supplier (Round 2) - 01 May 2026. opportunities.title: Art gallery exhibit
opportunities.description: Exhibition EOI: Berninneit Art Gallery Bass Coast Shire Council is accepting Expressions of Interest for the exciting opportunity to exhibit at Berninneit Art Gallery between 10 April to 29 May 2026. Berninneit Art Gallery is a purpose-built art gallery in the architecturally designed Berninneit cultural centre in Cowes, Phillip Island. Applications are welcomed from artists, curators, collective and organisations operating in both Bass Coast Shire and beyond for a 6-week exhibition in 2027. Gallery Vision The Gallery is a professional visual arts space that brings the best in contemporary art to our region, and which supports and fosters local artists. Attracting residents and visitors alike, it is a curated gallery that is Bass Coast’s first purpose-built exhibition space. The gallery will present curated and touring contemporary exhibitions relevant to our community, and showcase works from Bass Coast artists, Council’s collections and the wider arts sector. We strive to present well-executed and coherent exhibitions that are of the highest quality from emerging and established artists from Australia and beyond. The exhibition programme is designed to present works that support and develop the Arts in the region which in turn fosters a strong sense of local identity and encourages meaningful dialogue and participation from community. The gallery will present a program of high-quality contemporary, regional and First Nations produced exhibitions. The gallery offers a welcoming space for community engagement, exploration and enjoyment of contemporary art and cultural events, where a multi-disciplinary approach to programming allows for new ideas, perspectives and forms in art and culture to cross-pollinate. Please read the Exhibitors Information Pack: Expression of Interest for 2027, available via the link provided, to find out more about applying for an exhibition. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Energy terminal
opportunities.description: Vopak Victoria Energy Terminal Vopak Victoria Energy Terminal Pty Ltd (Vopak) is proposing to develop the Victoria Energy Terminal (VET) which is a low-impact solution to ensure Victorians have gas for their homes and businesses for the foreseeable future. VET incorporates a floating Liquified Natural Gas (LNG) marine terminal located in Port Phillip Bay, 19 kilometres offshore from Avalon, as well as onshore facilit ies. The Project involves several scope components including: 1. Marine terminal where the FSRU will be continuously moored and where LNG carriers will be temporarily moored alongside to transfer LNG. 2. Floating Storage and Regasification Unit (FSRU) vessel will receive LNG from the LNG import carriers delivered by ship-to-ship transfer. A regasification unit on the FSRU will convert the liquefied natural gas into vaporised natural gas. 3. Subsea High Voltage (HV) power cable connection which will provide the FSRU with power supply. 4. Buried subsea and onshore gas pipeline that allows natural gas to be transported to the onshore Gas Receiving Station (GRS). An underground shoreline crossing will be installed by Horizontal Directional Drilling (HDD) methodology. 5. Gas Receiving Station (GRS) which will receive the gas from the FSRU, provide custody transfer metering and treat the imported gas with additives such that the gas meets the requirements of the VTS for supply. 6. Onshore gas pipeline from the GRS to the connection point at the VTS 7. Onshore electrical power supply - HV transmission cables, terminal station, substation and any other grid connection requirements to provide electrical power to the FRSU and GRS. Note: The FSRU and Onshore electrical Power Supply to the HDD entry point will be Build Own Operate (BOO) packages. The project is planning for a Financial Investment Decision (FID) in Q4 2026. Vopak is proposing to undertake a formal Expression of Interest (EOI) process to identify suitably qualified and experienced organisations to execute the Engineering, Procurement and Construction / Installation (EPCI) for the following potential Contract / package options: 1. Overall EPCI Contract (includes Offshore and Onshore). 2. Offshore only EPCI Contract. 3. Onshore only EPCI Contract. 4. Individual packages for Onshore or Offshore. === WORK PACKAGES Work Package - EOI Closes EPCI Contract(s) EOI for EPCI Contract(s) - 29 Apr 2026 Individual Packages EOI for Individual Packages - 29 Apr 2026 TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Offshore wind project (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Blue Mackerel Offshore Wind Project Overview Backed by two of the world's leading energy companies, Blue Mackerel is strongly positioned to be one of Australia's first offshore wind projects. Located approximately 10km off the Gippsland coast between Seaspray and Woodside Beach, the project is targeting delivery by 2032. At up to 1GW, it could generate enough electricity to power up to 750,000 Victorian homes. By delivering large-scale renewable energy, the project is set to generate meaningful employment, investment, and supply chain opportunities across the region. Project Overview Blue Mackerel is being developed by JERA Nex bp - a 50:50 joint venture between JERA Co. and bp. With more than 15 years' global experience in offshore wind, JERA Nex bp is one of the world's largest offshore wind developers, owners and operators. The company's combined portfolio includes approximately 13GW of net potential generating capacity: around 1GW of installed capacity, 7.5GW in development, and 4.5GW from secured leases. JERA Nex bp draws on the strength of two world-leading companies- JERA Nex's track record in delivering and operating offshore wind projects with bp's extensive development portfolio and offshore energy expertise. The project is committed to partnering with the Gunaikurnai Land and water s Aboriginal Corporation, Traditional Owners, First Nations businesses, local communities, and Gippsland-based suppliers to ensure local participation and long-term benefits. Blue Mackerel acknowledges the Gunaikurnai as the Traditional Custodians of Gippsland and pays respect to Elders past and present. Timing: Blue Mackerel is currently in the early planning and feasibility phase, following the award of a feasibility licence (FL001) in April 2024 for a 163 km area within the Gippsland Declared Offshore Wind Zone. A 7-year feasibility period is now underway, during which Blue Mackerel will conduct environmental studies, planning assessments, community engagement and regulatory approvals before construction can begin. Subject to approvals and final investment, construction would commence in 2029 with first power targeted for 2032. Progress to Date Already, Blue Mackerel has made significant progress: First management Plan for offshore wind activities to be approved by the new Offshore Infrastructure Regulator Geophysical and Geotechnical Marine Surveys (completed March 2025): These works gathered deep and shallow seabed data to inform foundation and cable design. Wind and Wave Monitoring (deployed March 2025): A FLiDAR buoy and wave buoy are collecting long-term wind and metocean data. Environmental Monitoring Campaign (commenced July 2025): Includes aerial marine mammal surveys and deployment of Passive Acoustic Monitoring (PAM) equipment to gather two years of environmental baseline data. Opportunities for Suppliers Our vision is to grow a strong Australian offshore wind supply chain. That means creating opportunities for Gippsland-based, Victorian and Australian businesses across various stages of the project. The above studies will support early engineering studies, which will inform early design concepts, construction methods, risk management strategies, and detailed work package planning. The process of concept and preliminary design is designated as Pre-FEED (Pre- Front End Engineering Design). Pre-FEED works will support: a. Key design decisions, b. Risk assessment and mitigation, c. Project scope break-down to work packages/lots , d. Approach to the construction execution and operations phase and e. Precise cost estimates. The ongoing Pre-FEED works are assisting the identification of work packages-lots ( ref. (c) above). The following main work packages have been identified to date: 1. Foundations fabrication 2. Foundations Transport and Installation (T&I) 3. Onshore Substation supply and construction 4. Onshore underground cables supply and installation 5. Landfall-Shore crossing (HDD-Horizontal directional drilling) for cables 6. Offshore Substation fabrication 7. Offshore Substation Transport and Installation (T&I) 8. Offshore Export cables supply 9. Offshore Inter-Array cables supply 10. Offshore Export and Inter Array Cables T&I 11. Wind Turbine Generators (WTGs) supply , T&I and maintenance 12. Operations and maintenance (O&M) Engineering works to further break down (WBS -Work Breakdown Structure) the Blue Mackerel project scope and identify supply categories and work elements is still in progress. === WORK PACKAGES Work Package - Full Scope Closes Heavy Lifting and Transport equipment and services at Port in Victoria - 22 Apr 2026 Pre-assembly and inter-connection works of Wind turbine Towers at Port in Victoria - 22 Apr 2026. opportunities.title: Lithium-tantalum project (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Kathleen Valley Lithium-Tantalum Project Liontown is an ASX100 company whose purpose is to find, develop and supply battery minerals to the electric vehicle and energy storage industries. Liontown are currently developing the Kathleen Valley Lithium-Tantalum Project (Kathleen Valley) which will include the construction and operation of a fully integrated, low-carbon mine in Western Australia. Kathleen Valley is located in the north-eastern Goldfields region, approximately 680km northeast of Perth and 60km north of the mining town, Leinster. Liontown is committed to establishing a strong environmental, social and governance (ESG) foundation. ESG is central to our strategy, and we are determined to demonstrate sustainability leadership in the resources sector from the outset of planned operations. We believe that the success of our business is deeply intertwined with the well-being of our planet and society. We strive to create a positive impact through our operations, and we actively seek suppliers who share our commitment to our ESG principles. The primary focus of Kathleen Valley will be the extraction of lithium with a secondary focus on tantalum. The Project operating phase has an expected mine life of over 20 years. Kathleen Valley remains on target to commence first production of concentrate by mid-2024. Liontown remains committed to working with, and ensuring that, Australian industry, particularly the local communities is afforded full, fair and reasonable opportunity to participate in the Project. === WORK PACKAGES Work Package - Full Scope Closes Underground Project Crew - 27 Apr 2026. opportunities.title: Subsea tieback program (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: North West Shelf Subsea Tieback Program The North West Shelf (NWS) subsea tieback (SSTB) program is focused on using existing infrastructure to develop reserves from existing and undeveloped NWS Project fields. The NWS SSTB program will use the same types of equipment, methodology and approach to contracting to develop these fields that will connect into the NWS Project's existing offshore infrastructure. The principal NWS Project oil and gas fields are located about 125 kilometres north-west of Karratha, Western Australia in water depths ranging between 80 and 131 metres, covering a total area of 3,134 square kilometres. Current appraisal and development activities are focused on opportunities close to existing infrastructure. Offshore, the North Rankin Complex, the Goodwyn A platform (GWA) and the Angel platform dehydrate the raw hydrocarbons for export via a dual subsea pipeline system to the Karratha Gas Plant (KGP). The NWS Project's onshore KGP consists of five LNG trains and a domestic gas plant and is located on the Burrup Peninsula of North Western Australia, near the town of Karratha. The KGP processes raw dehydrated gas and exports LNG, condensate and LPG via ship together with condition ed gas via the domestic gas pipeline. === WORK PACKAGES Contract - Full Scope Closes TechnipFMC - GWF Provision of Heavy Lift and Transportation services - 30 Apr 2026. opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior lead developer developer
opportunities.description: LH-06235 - 1 Senior Lead Developer Developer Job details The role will require that you work across both Bio security systems and Export systems to help drive a program of work to integrate platforms to a single unified portal starting with SSO and account, roles and privileges, building to a full legacy migration. It will require working with multiple different technology stacks and navigating codebases ranging from Delphi and .VB to modern C# and Dynamics 365. Working with a small team that includes a Lead Architect/ Developer, Developers, Testers and Designers you will be working with product teams to help understand their systems and dependencies to help scope and drive system changes for relying parties, scope SSO effort and changes to attribute models that support delivery and update architectural documents to run through governance, cyber and oversight forums. You will take your steer from the product owner and lead developer to drive internal teams updating through dev-ops to scope work and ensure timelines are met. Key duties and responsibilities Key Duties and Responsibilities Hands-on Engineering: Act as the primary contributor for complex coding tasks across C#, ASPX, SQL, and JScript, while providing maintenance and logic-extraction for legacy Delphi, VB, and ASP systems. System Architecture: Define the technical blueprint for how our custom applications, databases, and Dynamics 365 ecosystem communicate, ensuring long-term stability and performance. Technical Project management : Bridge the gap between the "what" and the "how." You will oversee project timelines, manage technical risks, and ensure that architectural goals align with delivery schedules. Mentorship & Best Practices: You will set the standard for code quality, documentation (XML/HTML), and database schema design, mentoring the team through complex migrations and integrations. Required Skills and Experience • Demonstrable evidence of progressive, documented IT experience (circa 20 years), illustrating an experience trajectory from legacy client-server systems that evolved alongside the industry into modern cloud-based ERP and CRM environments. • Demonstrated evidence showing a rich understanding of hands-on knowledge across a broad range of programming languages across modern tiers, legacy tiers and web & data. • Demonstrated experience working on complex enterprise projects that required documenting architectural vision and applying whole of government standards, cyber best practices and legacy migration planning. • Demonstrated experience in project management leveraging agile practices, benefits realisation, risk management , planning and documentation to support. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: APS6 HR payroll / systems analyst
opportunities.description: LH-06225 - Minimum of 1 APS6 HR Payroll / Systems Analyst Job details Systems Analysts translate business requirements into technical requirements that align with relevant architectures. This role is a Senior HR Payroll Administration Officer (DSS) The role is responsible for the maintenance and support of the Department of Social services (DSS) ERP system including the HR Payroll Administration function. The role operates within the Payroll Administration and HR Support team to ensure the correct operation of the Human Resources aspects of SAP, under limited direction, through the provision of system support, implementation of system configuration changes, performance and system testing as necessary, and updating of system documentation; and other tasks as directed. The role is a specialist in, and responsible for, multiple components of the SAP Human Resources and Payroll modules and will provide specialist support for these components including the implementation of approved system changes Key duties and responsibilities Duties may include some or all of the following; • Manage the function of Payroll Administration for the Department of Social services (DSS) and other client agencies • Manage the fortnightly payroll process including quality assurance and associated mandatory reporting • Provide specialist, third-tier support for SAP HR and Payroll, including first level system configuration • Perform system testing and documentation • Monitor and action queries on the SAP Help Desk including incident investigation and resolution • Contribute to the day-to-day operations of the team including setting priorities, allocation of internal resources, and assisting with the development of work plans in relation to Payroll Administration. In addition the following Knowledge / Qualifications are desirable • Comprehensive understanding of the SAP HR Payroll Administration processes • Comprehensive understanding of Superannuation processes and Interfaces utilised for Payroll reporting and submission • Comprehensive understanding of Single Touch Payroll including reconciliation processes • Ability to undertake baseline SAP configuration i.e. Superannuation Funds, Salary Sacrifice, Leave, Payroll Wage types • Ability to interpret legislation and condition s of service within the Public Sector as it relates to the maintenance of employee data on SAP • Ability to work as an effective team member providing support to the team leader establishing clear work plans and timeframes in relation to payroll activities, effectively contribute and also assist other team members to achieve quality outcomes. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: LH-06239 - 1 Release Train Engineer Job details Managing the release of new and updated services into production, ensuring alignment with business objectives and compliance standards. opportunities.title: Invite only: Microsoft Fabric / Power BI Architecture review & optimisation
opportunities.description: PCS-03803 - Provision of Microsoft Fabric / Power BI Architecture Review and Optimisation Requirements Purpose: (Full Statement of Requirement (SoR) attached via link provided) The Tax Practitioners Board (TPB) seeks quotations for a targeted independent assessment of TPB’s Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse/OneLake) and Power BI environment to rapidly identify root causes of current performance, refresh reliability and access /permissions issues, validate the end‑to‑end architecture (from Lakehouse through semantic models to reports) against Microsoft best practice, and deliver practical, prioritised recommendations (including governance guardrails) and next‑phase actions. The Statement of Requirement is intended to be delivered as twelve (12) business days of total effort, completed within an elapsed period of 10 business days from commencement (with flexibility), with any redesign/implementation, deeper investigation, detailed target‑state documentation and formal knowledge transfer handled as a follow‑on engagement. Scope of services (Full scope attached via link provided) This assessment will confirm objectives and priority focus areas, then review the current reporting and data platform to understand how it is performing and where key issues may be occurring. The work will be time-boxed to the most material areas and provide a concise summary of findings and a prioritised set of practical recommendations and next steps aligned to good practice. Deliverables (Full list of deliverables attached in the SoR) Deliverables will include an executive-ready consolidated assessment report (PowerPoint) focused on Power BI optimisation—covering current-state observations, key performance/refresh drivers, relevant capacity considerations, and a prioritised recommendations roadmap—with only high-level Fabric architecture, security and governance observations where directly relevant. Indicative timeframe Refer to the statement of requirement for this detail. Suppliers should propose a realistic schedule based on their approach. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Data specialist services
opportunities.description: PCS-03731 - Procurement of Data Specialist services for access and Home Support Division Requirements About the Project The Department of Health, Disability and Ageing (the department) is seeking data specialist services to deliver on current Support at Home (SaH), Single Assessment System (SAS) and the Commonwealth Home Support Program (CHSP) reporting objectives. Duties may include, but are not limited to, the maintenance and evolution of an existing reporting suite, extraction of information from the Ageing and Aged Care Data Warehouse, remediation of any extraction issues, and developing bespoke and ongoing reporting for both internal and external stakeholders. Professional advice on reporting best practices is also highly desirable. Purpose The SaH, SAS and CHSP Data teams within the access and Home Support Division (AHSD) require data specialist resources to provide ongoing support with the maintenance of the current performance tracking and reporting operations, for the SaH, CHSP and assessment, including contributing to the new Aged Care Act 2024 wait times report. Furthermore, the implementation of SaH on 1 November 2025 has required the ongoing development of new reporting tools using new data sets. Context In view of the ongoing need for timely, high‐quality data on the SaH program, SAS, CHSP, ongoing support from data specialists is essential to ensure that outcomes are being met to a high standard. Key Dates ACTIVITY - DATE Anticipated start date - 1 July 2026 Contract term - The term is until 30 June 2027 Contract extension option - 2 x 12 month extension options TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Community Grants Program
opportunities.description: water loo Wind Farm Community Grants Program Community Grants Program is part of its commitment to make a positive contribution to the local Mid North community, water loo Wind Farm provides community funding to support locally-based initiatives that deliver positive and sustainable differences to the local community. The program provides up to $30,000 in funding each year for local initiatives that aim to benefit the community in the areas of diversity, sustainability and environment, health and wellbeing and community participation and pride. Funding priorities: To be considered for funding, projects should address at least one or more of these areas: • Community Diversity: Support for projects that encourage communities to connect and be inclusive of gender, cultures, age groups, nationalities and abilities. This includes events that celebrate culture and traditions, projects that aim to improve multicultural infrastructure, provide support for disadvantaged groups, achieve gender equality, or initiatives that seek to address social isolation and improve access and opportunity for everyone. • Sustainability and Environment: Support for projects that seek to build or enhance community sustainability or the local environment, in particular through the use of renewables, the reduction of waste or via protection of the environment. This includes solar panel installation, recycling at events, school education programs, community gardens and environmental preservation programs. • Health and Wellbeing: Support for projects that seek to improve the health and wellbeing of the local community. This may include physical or mental health programs, initiatives that aim to improve sports participation and access , sporting facilit y upgrades and education programs. • Community Participation and Pride: Support for projects that encourage greater community participation and/or seek to recognise and celebrate the unique attributes of the region. This includes community volunteering initiatives, playground / public space enhancements, community festivals and events and local heritage preservation. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Email enquiries to community@waterloowindfarm.com.au

opportunities.title: Porepunkah Local Area Traffic Management Plan
opportunities.description: The purpose of this project is to develop a Local Area Traffic management Plan (LATM) for Porepunkah that identifies, prioritises, and supports the delivery of safety and connectivity improvements across the township’s transport network. The LATM will provide a coordinated, evidence-based framework to address identified safety risks and infrastructure gaps, with a particular focus on improving outcomes for vulnerable road users, including pedestrians and cyclists. It will build on existing strategic work and translate these findings into implementable, place-based solutions. The LATM must be prepared in accordance with the Austroads Guide to Traffic management – Part 8: Local Street management and align with the Victorian Government’s Movement and Place Framework. Scope of works include: 1. Project inception 2. Literature review & data collection 3. Site visit 4. Community engagement (pre-draft) 5. Risk assessment 6. Concept Development & Options Analysis 7. Draft LATM Report 8. Community Engagement (Draft Plan) 9. Final LATM TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Alex Smith - Senior Strategic Planner 03 5755 0555 alexs@alpineshire.vic.gov.au

opportunities.title: Nuclear medicine manufacturing program - Project management services
opportunities.description: PSC002756. Nuclear Medicine Manufacturing Program - Project management services . ANSTO is seeking an experienced PMS provider to support the delivery of the Nuclear Medicine Manufacturing Program (NMMP) at its Lucas Heights campus, NSW. The PMS provider will deliver embedded project and program leadership and operate as an integral member of ANSTO’s NMMP Delivery Team. The role spans the full lifecycle of the Program, including planning, design, procurement, construction, commissioning, and transition to operations. The PMS provider will be responsible for integrated program co-ordination and management , providing proactive, evidence-based advice to ANSTO, and supporting informed decision-making across scope, schedule, cost, risk, and quality. The role requires active engagement with internal ANSTO stakeholders, regulators, delivery partners, consultants, and subject matter experts to manage interfaces and ensure alignment across all workstreams. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Healthy School Lunch Program
opportunities.description: DECYP 2386. Healthy School Lunch Program. The Healthy School Lunch Program is a significant Government initiative aimed at addressing food in security and supporting student wellbeing. Moving from delivering around 3,000 meals per week to 15 schools in 2022, it has now expanded to deliver over 21,000 meals per week to 60 schools from 2026. Ongoing delivery of the program beyond 2026 will aim to support families with the increasing cost-of-living pressures. In addition to this, it will enable children from low socio-economic backgrounds to be well-nourished and ready to learn and continue to contributing to improved attendance and engagement outcomes. Period of Contract (Estimated): From 01/02/2027 to 31/01/2030. Period of Option to Extend: From 01/02/2030 to 31/01/2032. Estimated date contract will be retendered: 02/08/2031. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Catering services (panel)
opportunities.description: 260402 (GEN/260402) - Catering services Panel Request for Proposal (RFP). Edith Cowan University (ECU) is seeking responses to this Request for Proposal (RFP) from suitable, experience and professional Catering services . The objective of this Request for Proposal is to establish a multi-supplier Catering services panel that provides the University with access to capable, compliant, and value for money catering services . The panel will support events of varying size, complexity, and profile, including (but not limited to) high value and high profile University functions. Edith Cowan University (ECU) hosts a diverse range of events throughout the year, including academic functions, corporate engagements, student activities, cultural events, and high-profile stakeholder occasions. Catering services play a critical role in supporting these events and in upholding the University’s reputation for quality, professionalism, and hospitality. To meet ongoing and future catering requirements, the University is seeking to establish a panel of suitably qualified and experienced Catering Service Providers. This panel will enable the University to efficiently engage reliable suppliers who can deliver consistent, high-quality catering services across a variety of event types, sizes, and formats. This Request for Proposal (RFP) invites submissions from providers who demonstrate the capability, capacity, and commitment to meet the University’s standards, compliance requirements, and operational needs. The vendors will be required to: - Support events of varying size, complexity, and profile, including high value and high-profile University functions. - Enable access to a range of catering styles and service models, from simple refreshments to full-service catering. - Ensure compliance with relevant University policies, food safety regulations, workplace health and safety requirements, and sustainability expectations. Requirements: - Provision of light refreshments, including tea, coffee, and morning and afternoon tea - Corporate and business lunch catering, including boxed lunches and buffet style offerings - Catering for formal functions and special events - Accommodation of dietary, allergen, and cultural requirements (including, but not limited to, vegetarian, vegan, halal, and gluten-free options) - Compliance with food safety standards and the delivery of consistent quality and reliable service - Delivery, setup, and pack down of catering services as required - Provision of sustainable packaging solutions and food waste minimisation initiatives (desirable). TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior test analyst
opportunities.description: LH-06237 - 1 Senior Test Analyst Job details The Test analyst will be principally responsible for designing, reviewing and executing manual and automated test cases as required for multiple projects across different systems/service lines. The role will be responsible for developing test planning and reporting on deliverables, review test cases to ensure accuracy, completeness, traceability and coverage of business requirements and risks Key duties and responsibilities The principal accountabilities and responsibilities include: • Design, review and execute manual and automated tests across multiple projects, often concurrently as required. • Develop test planning and reporting deliverables. • Participate in the review of requirements and specifications documentation. • Conduct detailed analysis of system and business requirements, using these products to design test cases that will satisfy relevant objectives. • Identify and support capability development and process improvement opportunities for the Testing services function in both manual and automation space. • Contribute to knowledge transfer among team members. • Develop and maintain productive working relationships with internal and external stakeholders. • management of defects including reporting using tools such as JIRA • Review test cases to ensure accuracy, completeness, traceability and coverage of business requirements and risks. • Certification in ISTQB is highly desirable. • Certification in Tricentis TOSCA desirable. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: ERP program manager
opportunities.description: LH-06245 - 1 ERP Program Manager Job details The Enterprise Resourcing Planning Branch (ERPB) is seeking to engage Senior Program Manager to support the commencement of a large-scale enterprise transformation program implementing a new ERP platform across the organisation. This role will play a critical part in driving and maintaining an integrated delivery schedule and ensuring alignment with program objectives, timelines and governance requirements. The ideal candidate will have demonstrated experience in leading and managing large scale ERP implementation projects, leading cross functional teams, identifying and resolving impediments and be fully competent in delivering projects throughout the entire project lifecycle. Key duties and responsibilities The Program Manager will: • lead and coordinate program delivery activities to support the achievement of the department’s desired outcomes. • develop, maintain, monitor and report with program management artefacts such as a roadmap, integrated program management plan, status of the deliverables, milestones, dependencies, resources, budget, risks and issues. • oversee day-to-day project activities, ensuring progress aligns with the project plan. • engage and collaborate with business stakeholders and suppliers as required to deliver a well-defined and understood solution. • Build strong relationships and partner with the Senior Responsible Officer (SRO), system integrator, change manager, technology, and senior executives within the Program Board. • Report to and will support the SRO in delivering the ERP implementation project. • be accountable for ensuring that the contract with the system integrator is executed in accordance with the terms and condition s agreed by the department, including scope, deliverables, performance obligations, and governance requirements. • undertake detailed risk analysis and actively manage the program’s key risks and issues, including establishing mitigation strategies, contingency plans, ownership and due dates, conducting regular risk reviews, and escalating material risks to the ERP Program Board through established governance processes. • manage the development of the required artefacts for the project in line with department’s governance and assurance frameworks. • engage and collaborate with other project streams to manage interdependencies, cross share capability and capacity, and ensure delivery of value-add, integrated solution. • take responsibility for project progress and use of resources and initiate corrective action where necessary, foreshadowing upcoming resource needs and actioning engagement well ahead of start dates. • provide reports and attend ERP Program Board and Committee meetings (once formal governance structure is formed) with leadership. • be the primary liaison to the SI, monitoring progress against contracted deliverables, supporting acceptance processes and escalating issues of performance or scope in accordance with the program’s governance and contracted framework. • develop project closure artefacts including lessons learned and operational transition activities. The specified personnel may be directed to assist with other work in relation to other streams within the ERP Branch. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: FinOps engineer
opportunities.description: LH-05624 - 1 FinOps Engineer Job details The Australian Electoral Commission (AEC) is seeking to engage an experienced FinOps Engineer whose primary focus is on improving cloud resource allocation and supporting business teams in optimising their cloud utilisation. The role will also provide technical direction and advice for tasks, processes, and procedures to support the implementation of FinOps best practices. Key duties and responsibilities • Cloud Cost Optimisation: specialising in managing cloud costs through monitoring for anomalies and responding to alerts of increases in costs. This includes undertaking research and working closely with cross-functional teams, providing recommendations to optimize cloud usage, reduce waste, and improve cost efficiencies. • Cloud Usage Analysis: Analysing cloud usage patterns and identify cost optimization opportunities. This involves understanding resource consumption, identifying underutilized resources, and suggesting adjustments. • Cost Allocation and Chargeback: the set up of cost allocation mechanisms to attribute cloud expenses to specific teams or projects. They also establish chargeback processes to allocate costs accurately. • Collaboration: Collaborate closely with engineering, finance, and business teams. By fostering communication and collaboration, they ensure that cloud spending aligns with business goals. • Data-Driven Decision Making: the setup of practices that enable timely and informed decisions by providing financial accountability through data-driven insights. They develop cloud spend and usage reports to help organizations make informed choices about cloud. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Project managers
opportunities.description: LH-06249 - 4 Manager Project Managers The Treasury is seeking experienced Project Managers to play a critical role in delivering ICT projects on time and within budget. Successful candidates will demonstrate strong experience in managing IT projects and driving business and digital process improvement initiatives, with the ability to apply industry-standard methodologies, tools, and frameworks. The candidate will have the ability to quickly adapt to the Treasury environment by engaging with key business users and stakeholders from various levels. They will be comfortable working both collaboratively within a team and autonomously to achieve successful outcomes. The role requires strong project management capability in complex delivery environments, including initiatives with multiple stakeholders, dependencies and external partners. Projects may span planning, design, delivery, transition and review phases, and require coordination across organisational boundaries. We are seeking candidates with at least 5 years documented and proven experience to lead and deliver projects of this scale and scope in an outcomes focused environment for the Commonwealth or equivalent to the scale and scope of Department of Treasury. The intent of this process is to hire multiple Project Managers immediately and/or include interviews and negotiations regarding start/end dates and hourly rates. Following this, Treasury may utilise the outcome of this approach to market for up to three months from the closing date to engage other project managers from various suppliers involved in the process, leaving the evaluation process open to allow this to be achieved. Due to the panel currently requiring all contracts to be signed prior to notifying unsuccessful suppliers, those invited should note until all contracts are signed the RFQ will remain in "In Negotiation" phase. Additionally, no further suppliers will be invited to the RFQ due to line area evaluation resources in the peak pre-budget timeframe. Key duties and responsibilities • Participate and assisting in high-level planning for program and project implementation • Define and maintain project scope, ensuring alignment with organisational goals and objectives, and effectively manage scope changes as required. • Develop and manage project plans, schedules, budgets, and resource allocations to ensure delivery within agreed timeframes and constraints. • Lead and coordinate multi-functional project teams, fostering collaboration and accountability to achieve delivery outcomes. • Build and maintain effective working relationships with technical resources and stakeholders. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: DevOps engineers
opportunities.description: LH-06250 - 2 DevOps Engineers The Australian Criminal Intelligence Commission (ACIC) is seeking to engage a suitably qualified and experienced DevOps Engineer to: • Assist the Infrastructure team of the NCIS Program. • Design, implement and maintain the security of Azure cloud platform and services . • Establish and maintain strong working relationships with internal and external parties, including senior stakeholders. Share technical knowledge across the team, and provide support to other parts of the NCIS Program. Key duties and responsibilities Implementing technical solutions in cloud to facilit ate migration of applications. • Designing infrastructure solutions to host applications currently on premise. • Developing automated solutions to deploy infrastructure and applications to cloud. • Working within DevOps practices of the wider program team. • Inputting features and design of cloud foundations. • Developing new standard features and automation for standard customer cloud environments. • Contributing to Product management practices to develop new features. • Contributing to agile/scrum ceremonies to develop, plan, and prioritise new feature delivery. • Supporting Java applications running in a Linux environment. • Undertaking performance tuning. • Working in a diverse team across a wide range of different technologies and systems. • Automating processes and building self-service capabilities. • Delivering and building services and technologies including but not limited to Microsoft Azure. • Leading and coordinating cloud development activities between relevant stakeholders and technical resources - both internal and external. • Periodically collaborating to develop and implement operational improvements. • Leveraging experience with cloud native tools to review metrics and monitor the health/utilisation of agency’s cloud services . • Designing, implementing and maintaining the security of Azure cloud platform and services . • Implementing new security measures on the Azure cloud. • Collaborating with developers and the NCIS Sustainment team to create strategies for improving security . • Scripting and automating security processes using Microsoft Azure DevOps. • Setting direction for use of Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC), Microsoft Defender for Cloud, Administrator protection strategy and Azure policy to govern Azure resources. • Managing vulnerability scanning tools to identify security risk in software code or platform. • Responding to security incidents if required. • Working with architects, administrators, and developers to plan and implement solutions that meet security and compliance requirements. • Ensuring that Azure deployments are secure and compliant with industry and regulatory standard, the primary work focus will be infrastructure work that improves NCIS security posture. • Specified Personnel will be expected to transfer skills and knowledge to departmental staff and ensure appropriate documentation is stored in accordance with the Department’s recordkeeping policy and practices. • All services and deliverables performed by specified personnel must: 1. be performed or delivered promptly and without delay in accordance with the timeframes notified by the buyer from time to time; and 2. meet the assessment and performance criteria set out in any monitoring and performance assessment processes notified to the seller from time to time. The ACIC requires specified personnel to: Obtain the ACIC’s Organisational Suitability Assessment (OSA) prior to commencement. • Have an Australian Government security clearance (AGSVA) to the minimum of Negative Vetting Level 1 (NV1) or be willing to undergo and obtain and AGSVA NV1 clearance. • As directed by the ACIC, complete a Deed of Confidentiality or Non-Disclosure Agreement prior to commencement or comply with processes required to engage the Specified Personnel under section 48 of the Australian Crime Commission Act (2002). Note: Seller may be required to meet the cost of any security clearances TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: ERP - organisational change management services
opportunities.description: PCS-03858 - ERP - Organisational Change management services The department is looking for an experienced organisational change management (OCM) capability to support the commencement of a large-scale enterprise transformation program implementing a new ERP platform across the organisation. This OCM capability will play a critical part in the ERP Program driving business readiness, stakeholder engagement, and adoption across a complex, multi-vendor environment. The OCM capability should demonstrate proven ERP expertise to provide advice and direction on the current ERP Program change strategy. The OCM capability will: • review existing and / or develop change management artefacts (i.e. strategy, plan, change impact assessments), to address diverse stakeholder groups and alignment with the new ERP platform • develop and support the development of new change management resources, tools and templates • support the Buyer’s leadership team in driving change messaging and reinforcing behavioural shifts • oversee stakeholder engagement, targeted communications and adoption planning tailored to ERP Program’s needs • be supported by in-house change management practitioner, ERP Program support officers and a communications team fostering a collaborative approach • progress the ERP change management model and key change priorities, and • work collaboratively with suppliers, vendors, business units and delivery teams. The specified personnel may be directed to assist with other work in relation to other streams within the ERP Branch. If the department elects to extend the contract past the initial term, the ERP Program will facilit ate significant system and process change and will require strong OCM experience within ERP environments. During the optional term(s) of the Contract the OCM capability will: • lead the associated change activities during the implementation of a new ERP Platform across the organisation (i.e. engagement strategies) • work closely with ERP Program leadership, business SMEs, technology teams and the ERP platform implementation partner to ensure change impacts are understood, managed and embedded effectively • lead stakeholder engagement, business readiness assessments / metrics and adoption activities • partner with training teams to ensure effective learning and capability uplift • develop and track a benefits realisation plan and report these to the PB, identifying if any benefit is at risk, so that scope or change management interventions can be made. • monitor change risks, resistance and adoption metrics across the ERP Program, and • integrate change management practices throughout the entire program lifecycle to drive both people and process transformation in parallel with technology implementation. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Agentic AI strategy analysis
opportunities.description: PCS-03855 - MST26-011 Agentic AI Strategy Analysis This procurement is intended to address a capability and capacity gap in the agency’s ability to independently assess and design for agentic AI. The agency requires a Seller to provide expert, vendor-agnostic analysis of available approaches and to test feasibility and implications for the agency’s environment. The Seller will also support the agency to uplift internal capability through practical guidance, training and reusable resources, and ensure recommendations align with agency and whole-of-government frameworks (including for system design, risk assessment, assurance and implementation planning). The ideal end state is that the agency has a clear, evidence based and defensible position on where agentic AI could be used, an agreed readiness and gap assessment, and a roadmap and capability model that enables the agency to make informed investment and governance decisions and progress priority use cases in a control led, responsible and repeatable way. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: ICT professional services
opportunities.description: PCS-03854 - ICT Professional services to support ICT Delivery and Enablement Projects 2026 The purpose of this procurement is to establish an ICT Professional services Panel to provide the Department of Education (the department) with flexible, ondemand access to Professional and Consulting services to support the delivery of a range of ICT and digital initiatives and projects of varying size, complexity and duration across the department. The department is seeking to establish a panel by engaging sellers (up to 5) for 24 months (with an up to 1 x 12-month extension if required) to provide ICT Professional and Consultancy services across various disciplines, on an as needed basis. Successful sellers must be flexible and able to work closely with business areas to respond to changing requirements. This includes being highly responsive to requests, providing timely advice, and delivering a positive customer experience for business stakeholders. Sellers are expected to operate collaboratively and maintain clear, professional communication throughout engagements. Sellers are expected to demonstrate a strong commitment to the department and to delivering high quality, collaborative engagement experiences that support timely, effective, and value for money outcomes. Sellers must have the capability and experience to maintain, source, and mobilise suitably qualified and experienced personnel at short notice when requested by business areas. This includes the ability to support multiple concurrent engagements across different business areas, where required, and to ensure continuity of service across Work Orders. This approach will enable the department to onboard ICT professional and consultancy resources efficiently and deliver outcomes in a timely manner. Sellers must ensure that any specialists or experts proposed to the department hold current police checks and minimum baseline security clearances, to support efficient onboarding and minimise delays to commencement. Successful suppliers may be required to deliver one or more of the following deliverables, as specified in individual Work Orders. The nature, scope, and timing of deliverables will vary depending on business requirements, the needs of business owners, the CIO function, and the stage and progress of each project. Deliverables and capability may include, but are not limited to: • ICT advisory services , including the provision of expert strategic, technical, operational, architectural, assurance, governance, security , compliance, or service management advice and recommendations • Business and technical analysis, including requirements definition, documentation, and stakeholder engagement • Development of plans, reports, strategies, design artefacts, and other agreed documentation • Delivery of agreed outcomes, milestones, or work packages • ICT project and program support, including planning, coordination, reporting, risk and issue management , and stakeholder engagement • ICT solution design, implementation support, testing, deployment, transition, and handover activities • Operational and postdeployment support activities, where required • Knowledge transfer to departmental staff, including handover documentation, where appropriate, and • Support for the continuous improvement of ICT practices, processes, or systems. All deliverables must be fit for purpose, meet agreed quality and performance standards, and be delivered in accordance with the relevant Work Order. Deliverables will be subject to review and formal acceptance by the Department. The contract put in place with the successful seller/s will be of a value anticipated to be the maximum value estimated for ICT Professional services at the time the procurement is finalised. The Department is unable to make any guarantees on the number of ICT Professional services sellers eventually engaged and or periods of engagement. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Totara LMS managed services
opportunities.description: PCS-03860 - Totara LMS Managed services The Australian Government, through the Office of the eSafety Commissioner (eSafety), is seeking to procure a suitably qualified supplier via the Digital Marketplace to deliver ongoing managed services for the Be Connected (https://beconnected.esafety.gov.au/) and Frontline Worker’s (https://frontlineworkers.esafety.gov.au/) websites. Be Connected is a national digital inclusion initiative designed to improve the confidence, digital skills and online safety of older Australians, supported through online learning resources and a network of community organisations. The Frontline Workers website provides online training for frontline workers, delivering in-depth training to enable them to better help their clients experiencing technology- facilit ated abuse. Both websites are built on the Totara Learn LMS platform and collectively provide access to hundreds of learning modules, courses, and resources for their respective cohorts. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only. E: ICTProcurements@esafety.gov.au Deadline for asking questions Monday, 27 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time.

opportunities.title: Invite only: Security services
opportunities.description: PCS-03859 - security services The eSafety Commissioner’s (eSafety) purpose is to help safeguard Australians at risk from online harms and to promote safer, more positive online experiences. eSafety leads and coordinates the online safety efforts across Australian Government departments, authorities and agencies, and engages with key online safety stakeholders. It has a broad remit which includes administering complaint schemes and the development of audience-specific content and programs to help educate all Australians about online safety risks and how to deal with harms including cyberbullying, adult cyber abuse, image-based abuse and illegal and restricted content. This RFQ seeks to engage a qualified security services provider to deliver penetration testing and PSPF/ISM-aligned security assessments of IT Systems. Due to variability in system size, complexity, and scope, services will be procured on a fee-for-service basis, with work commissioned via individual work orders. The contract will include a Do Not Exceed (DNE) maximum value, with services delivered as required over the contract term, and agreed upon before commencement. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Service desk analyst
opportunities.description: LH-06238 - 1 Service Desk Analyst AUSTRAC is seeking a full-time onsite Service Desk Analyst to support end users of IT systems and services within the Information and Technology Solutions (ITS) Branch. The Service Desk Analyst will act as a key frontline support resource in the Sydney office, providing high-quality onsite and remote assistance, managing incidents and service requests, provisioning and supporting end-user equipment, and contributing to the effective operation of AUSTRAC’s IT support environment. The successful candidate will possess skills and experience aligned to a Service Desk Analyst role at the APS5 equivalent level. They will otherwise demonstrate the skills and experience under "Key duties and responsibilities" below. Key duties and responsibilities The Service Desk Analyst is a key member of the IT Service Desk within the Information & Technology Solutions (ITS) Branch. The IT Service Desk works collaboratively to provide clear, effective and responsive IT support, ensuring the staff of AUSTRAC can use agency technology with confidence. The Service Desk Analyst in each state office is responsible for day-to-day operational support. They must have the ability to work under limited supervision in resolving a wide range of technical issues while also providing high-quality user experience. The key duties and requirements for the role are as provided below. Key Duties The Service Desk Analyst is responsible for: • Providing high-quality onsite and remote IT support, ensuring timely resolution of tickets for all staff, including support for Senior Executives. • Managing and actioning service requests and incidents in alignment with established ITIL processes, maintaining clear communication with stakeholders and delivering excellent customer service. • Administering core systems, user accounts, permissions, and network resources in accordance with organisational policies and security requirements. • Working collaboratively with team members, platform teams, and external vendors to support the delivery of IT services . • Managing end-user IT equipment and peripherals, including troubleshooting and resolving hardware/software incidents, assisting with deployment of new or replacement devices, and maintaining accurate asset records. • Sharing technical expertise and contributing to a knowledge-sharing team culture. • Participating in the testing and implementation of new services , tools, and processes that enhance IT operations. • Creating and maintaining clear technical documentation and user guides to support IT processes and assist staff. • Ensuring compliance with corporate security policies and proactively identify and report risks in the IT environment. • Undertaking other duties as directed, aligned with the priorities of the ITS branch. Requirements for the Role The Service Desk Analyst will possess the Core capabilities and Professional capabilities outlined below. Core capabilities Deliver Client Focussed Results Takes personal responsibility for service provided to clients. Ensures a high level of responsiveness and access ibility to clients. Works to strengthen and maintain relations with clients from culturally and linguistically diverse groups. Monitors and manages client service delivery and ensures that it is accurate, timely, consistent and appropriate. Seeks feedback from clients on satisfaction and areas for potential improvement. Apply Expertise Understands the technical and professional skills and expertise in the work area. Ensures effective use of own and team’s expertise in supporting work area objectives. Consults appropriately and includes others to ensure comprehensive professional input is used. Provides appropriate advice in area of professional or technical specialisation. Resolves problems effectively using established practices and methodologies relevant to area of specialisation. Ensures knowledge, skills and expertise are current and appropriate to complete work objectives. Communicate Effectively Communicates clearly, concisely and unambiguously. Acts as an effective representative of work area. Engages in active listening and seeks clarification of own understanding. Tailors communication to audience and circumstances and ensures common understanding of issues. Encourages discussion, welcomes and responds to feedback and adapts approach to ensure audience understanding. Adapt and Respond to Change Participates in the implementation of change initiatives. Accommodates changing priorities and operating environments and responds with initiative and flexibility to periods of uncertainty and ambiguity. Modifies own work practices to support changing circumstances and organisational focus. Actively helps and supports others in dealing with uncertainty and change. People Focussed Works collaboratively with team members and with a range of internal and external clients. Expresses own views in a constructive and diplomatic way. Recognises contributions made by others and rewards achievements. Reflects on how own emotions impact on others. Values Based Demonstrates high levels of professionalism and independence. Provides a high level of honest and impartial advice. Provides leadership in ethical behaviour. Promotes and upholds relevant codes of ethics and practice and aligns business processes accordingly. Accepts responsibility for and acknowledges mistakes and is willing to seek advice from others. display s the courage and commitment to maintain a difficult position. Is prepared to address difficult issues. Recognises individual differences and working styles and manages diversity in the workplace. Professional capabilities Technical Proficiency Demonstrated technical expertise solving problems in a modern Microsoft end-user environment, some examples include: • Windows 11 • Microsoft Intune • Active Directory and Azure Active Directory (Entra ID) • Hardware including Microsoft Surface Pro, Apple iPhones, Cisco Videoconferencing and MFDs • Microsoft 365, Office Apps and Exchange online. • Basic Network Troubleshooting (VPN, LAN) • ITSM tools (e.g. Jira, ServiceNow) Operates in alignment with service management principles and best‑practice frameworks (e.g., ITIL v4), applying these to ensure consistent IT service delivery. Location of services : Sydney, NSW Clearance required: Ability to obtain and maintain Negative Vetting 1 (NV1), with an existing NV1 clearance being desirable Suitability assessment: Labour Hire Workers must pass an Agency Suitability Assessment before being able to commence with AUSTRAC TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: WS5622271244 - Existing advertising billboards TfNSW is looking to partner with out-of-home (OOH) supplier/s to operate, manage and maintain 62 static and 34 digital OOH advertising roadside asset s located in metro and regional NSW. Project goals Proposals from Respondents to undertake the development, management and operation of Existing Advertising asset s (static and digital billboard asset s) including the upgrade of static asset s to digital and the decommissioning of static asset s within this RFP. The proposed contract terms are: • Digital billboards – 5 years, plus a single, 2-year option • Static billboards – 3 years, plus a single, 2-year option • Static billboards upgraded to digital – 7 years, plus a single, 2-year option Scope The Supplier must work collaboratively with TfNSW to achieve the following key objectives: • meet and exceed the outcomes, obligations and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in the Proposed Contract; • efficiently plan and obtain DA consents from the Department of Planning and Environment (DPE) to upgrade static asset s to digital; • build state-of-the-art digital Advertising asset s in a timely, efficient, and safe manner; • position the in-scope Advertising asset s as a premium asset within the OOH marketplace; • provide a strong commercial revenue stream to TfNSW that exceeds annual sales targets and grows year on year; • minimise exposure to various risks by adhering to relevant standards, policies and legislation (safety, development protocols, planning, advertising content standards etc) • innovate to ensure the increasing sustainability of advertising revenue generated; • report performance metrics to TfNSW; and • propose new advertising strategies to TfNSW that are consistent with the requirements of TfNSW. opportunities.title: Grant - Community Building Partnership
opportunities.description: Community Building Partnership 2026 The Community Building Partnership (CBP) program provides grant funding to local community organisations and councils to strengthen the wellbeing of NSW communities through small local infrastructure projects. Grant amount: From $10,000 to $100,000 The Community Building Partnership (CBP) program provides grant funding to local community organisations and councils to strengthen the wellbeing of NSW communities through small local infrastructure projects. • Total funding available for the CBP round in 2026 in $41.85 million. • There is a maximum of $450,000 grant funding available in each electoral district. Who can apply To be eligible to apply for funding, applicants must be one of the following organisation types: • incorporated association registered with NSW Fair Trading • Australian public company limited by guarantee • Local Aboriginal Land Council • Indigenous corporation registered with the Office of the Registrar of Indigenous Corporations • NSW local council • council committee under section 355 of the Local Government Act 1993 • trust registered with the Australian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission (ACNC) • P&C Association registered by NSW Fair Trading • P&C Association registered by the Federation of Parents and Citizens Associations of NSW • not-for-profit entity incorporated under an Act of Parliament • non-distributing co-operative registered with NSW Fair Trading Eligible applicants must also hold a valid and current Certificate of Currency for Public Liability Insurance with a minimum cover of $5 million. Types of projects funded under this grant CBP will consider applications between $10,000 and $100,000 for 3 categories: 1. Capital works Capital works are defined as ‘improvements to the land or building, or freestanding equipment that will be fixed or installed to the land or building’. This includes: • construction of new community infrastructure. • upgrade (refurbishment, repair and maintenance ) to existing community infrastructure. • any project component that requires fixed installation and would require permission from the owner of the building/land to commence works. This applies even if your organisation is the owner and/or other project components are freestanding equipment. 2. Equipment Equipment is defined as ‘freestanding equipment that does not require fixed installation and/or registration’. This includes: • vehicles that do not require registration, e.g. tractors, utility task vehicles (UTVs), all-terrain vehicles (ATVs),dragon boats, golf buggies, dinghies, powerboats or sailing boats less than 5.5 m long or with an engineless than 4.0 kW. • Automated External Defibrillators (AEDs). Applications are limited to one package up to $3,000 per application, per project location, and will be deemed ineligible if it is for more than one AED package at one project location. 3. Vehicles Vehicles are defined as ‘vehicles that require registration’. This includes: • purchase or modification of vehicles that require registration. • on-road vehicles such as cars, buses, trucks, and trailers. • off-road or on- water vehicles such as motorised rescue vehicles and boats. All projects must be delivered in NSW within the Electoral District for which funding is sought from. When the project can start and end The project should be started by 8 May 2026 and the project must be completed by 24 January 2028. What co-contributions are required Local councils and council committees under section 355 of the Local Government Act 1993 are required to provide a matched cash contribution equal to the grant amount requested. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: CBP Team Department of Communities and Justice P: (02) 8753 8144 E: CBP2026@dcj.nsw.gov.au

opportunities.description: TSC 03/2026 - Supply and Installation of Solar PV and Battery Storage Systems Supply and Installation of Solar PV and Battery Storage Systems - Temora, NSW 2666 Temora Shire Council is a Local Government Organisation in the Riverina region of New South Wales (2666) and is situated within the Southwest Slopes area of Wiradjuri Council approximately 80km from Wagga Wagga, 150km from Canberra and 450km from Sydney. Temora Shire Council (the Principal) is seeking to install solar photovoltaic (PV) and Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) at two of its operational facilit ies.
