Site 1 is Temora Recreation Center with a 49.5kW Solar and 80kWh battery. Site 2 is Temora Sewage Treatment Plant with a 39.9kW solar and 78kWh battery. A ley requirement of this project is the operation of both battery and solar systems as part of a Virtual Power Plant (VPP). opportunities.title: Climate-related financial disclosures
opportunities.description: RFP Climate-Related Financial Disclosures Central Queensland University (CQU) is seeking Proposals from suitably qualified and experienced consultants to design and propose a modular, scalable approach to preparing for emerging climate-related financial disclosure requirements, noting these are not yet mandated for statutory bodies in Queensland. This invitation deliberately does not prescribe a fixed scope of works. Suppliers are asked to apply their expertise to: a) articulate what services are required to support compliance with, and readiness for, climate-related financial disclosure frameworks such as Australian Accounting Standards Board AASB S2, International Sustainability Standards, the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures framework and broader Australian public sector and higher education regulatory expectations including NSW Treasury’s TPG 24-33, b) distinguish between core foundational services , optional or advanced services , and future-phase services as disclosure maturity evolves, and c) propose a delivery model that supports both institution-specific requirements and shared, multi-institution solutions that would be applicable across regional universities. In addition to CQU’s own needs, this Invitation expressly seeks proposals that identify opportunities for collaboration across Reginal Universities Network (RUN) institutions, including: • services and deliverables that can be standardised or reused across multiple universities, • shared tools, frameworks, template or guidance materials, • joint training or delivery models, • pricing structures that demonstrate economies of scale where multiple institutions participate. While CQU will contract in its own right, suppliers are asked to proactively identify, design and clearly articulate where shared RUN-level solutions are appropriate and how these could be practically implemented. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Central Queensland University Procurement Advice Team E: pat-requests@cqu.edu.au

opportunities.title: Construction of water treatment plant upgrade
opportunities.description: DLGWV2025-0153 - Construction of the Cherbourg water Treatment Plant Upgrade The Department of Local Government, water and Volunteers is seeking to engage a contractor to undertake the Construction of the Cherbourg water Treatment Plant Upgrade to address long-standing challenges with drinking water quality in the community. These challenges include persistent Boil water Advisories (BWAs), low chlorine residuals, E.coli detections, and elevated disinfection by-products, primarily caused by ageing infrastructure, limited instrumentation, and inadequate treatment processes at the existing facilit y. The project aims to implement critical improvements to eliminate these risks and ensure the community has access to a safe, reliable, and sustainable drinking water supply. Cherbourg is an Aboriginal community of the Wakka Wakka people, located 267 km northwest of Brisbane within the South Burnett Region. The community has an estimated population of 1,190 residents according to the 2021 census, though Cherbourg Aboriginal Shire Council (CASC) suggests the actual population is closer to 1,800. With project population growth to 4,000 by 2070, the demand for essential services including safe and reliable drinking water is expected to increase significantly. Cherbourg’s water supply is sourced from “The Rocks” in Barambah Creek. The Bjelke-Petersen Dam, managed by Sun water , can augment the Barambah Creek supply through releases, though this is rare. water is pumped from the creek to a conventional water treatment plant that consists of a clarifier followed by sand and carbon filters, before disinfection with chlorine gas. water is then stored in an arrangement (based on need and circumstance) across 3 service reservoirs prior to distribution to the supply network. The WTP was established in 1970 and has undergone several upgrades over time, with several upgrades taking place between 2016 and 2022, including the installation of the 2x2ML reservoirs, addition of granulated activated carbon (GAC) filtration, and installing a new raw water rising main. Despite these upgrades, the WTP is significantly challenged in producing water that is compliant with desired water quality criteria. Data and information are also scarce and difficult to source, with immature asset management and no structured data/information historian, which limits the understanding and visibility of what is occurring. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Transmission lines & infrastructure (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: The Gladstone Project Overview Powerlink is delivering the Gladstone Project which will strengthen and increase capacity of the electricity transmission network in the region. This critical program of transmission upgrades will ensure the safety and reliability of the power network as the region prepares for the eventual retirement of the Gladstone Power Station, and heavy industry moves toward using renewable energy sources. The Gladstone Project will see several key projects planned, assessed and delivered in incremental stages over the next decade. This will include new transmission lines and new transmission infrastructure such as substations and associated equipment. Stage 1 This first stage involves construction of the Calvale to Calliope River Transmission Line Reinforcement Project. Early works (planning and assessment) funding has been approved and is underway, and construction is expected to start by mid-2026. Completion is scheduled for late 2028. Stage 1 will also include works at the existing Calvale Substation, and an expansion of the existing Calliope River Substation. For further information on the planning and engagement activities completed to date, as well as the status of approvals, please refer to Powerlink's website: Calvale to Calliope River Transmission Line Reinforcement Project | Powerlink. Future stages Investment and funding decisions for future stages of the Gladstone Project will be assessed and delivered as part of the Queensland Government's Priority Transmission Investment (PTI) process. For further information on this investment process, please refer to Powerlink's website: Priority Transmission Investments | Powerlink. The Gladstone Project is expected to be implemented in three stages, subject to several factors, including: the PTI assessment, Ministerial direction, responsiveness to the load growth in the region, and the need to be flexible and maximise delivery efficiency. === WORK PACKAGES Contract - opportunities.title: Art submissions
opportunities.description: Watching Over Estuaries: Call for Art Submissions In 2026, EstuaryWatch celebrates 20 years of monitoring, learning, caring, and building connections with Victorian estuaries, thanks to the passion of volunteers, coordinators, partners, and communities across the state. To mark this special milestone, we are planning a 20‑year celebration event in late winter / early spring (date to be confirmed), and we invite you to be part of it by contributing to a community art exhibition (and also coming along to celebrate). For 20 years, EstuaryWatch has been more than a program, it’s been a community of people who care deeply about estuaries and each other. This exhibition is a way to honour that shared journey, reflect on what we’ve learned, and celebrate the many ways people connect to water . We warmly invite volunteers (past and present), staff, partners, and supporters to submit artworks inspired by EstuaryWatch and 20 years of estuary stories. These works will be exhibited as part of the EstuaryWatch 20-year celebration event (details still to be confirmed). Artists will be fully acknowledged, and further details will be shared soon. Theme: 20 Years of EstuaryWatch Submissions might explore: • Estuaries you’ve monitored, loved, or learned from • Life by the water — people, plants, animals, tides, seasons • Citizen science in action • Change over time (or what stays the same) • Connection, care, and community • Reflections on 20 years of EstuaryWatch What you can submit Works can be new or existing, provided they align with the theme. You don’t need to be a “professional” artist — this exhibition is about stories, perspectives, and connection, not perfection. We welcome a wide range of creative responses, including: • Photography • Painting or drawing • Printmaking or mixed media • Textile or fibre art • Sculpture (small–medium scale) • Other creative interpretations TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Send submissions to: justine.holmes@ccma.vic.gov.au To express interest or submit artwork, please provide: Your name and connection to EstuaryWatch Title of the work Medium and approximate dimensions A short description or story behind the piece (optional but encouraged) Photos of the artwork (for 2D works or sculptures)

opportunities.title: Grant
opportunities.description: GO8383 - Australia-Japan Foundation 2026-27 Grant Round The Australia-Japan Foundation (AJF) advances Australia's engagement with Japan by supporting people-to-people links through programs and partnerships aligned with the Australian Government's priorities. The AJF 2026-27 Grant Round invites grant applications for projects in the following priority areas: • activities that promote cooperation on bilateral, economic security and broader geostrategic issues • activities that expand Japanese literacy in Australia and/or an understanding of Australia in Japan • arts, culture, sport, and youth engagement • activities that advance cooperation in key emerging sectors, including energy transition, critical minerals, resilient supply chains, agriculture, artificial intelligence, robotics, cyber security and advanced technologies. The intended outcomes of the grant round are: • delivery of impactful and well-promoted activities that catalyse new, and deepen existing, bilateral, institutional and community partnerships and collaborations based on shared strategic goals. • increased public engagement and understanding and awareness of shared interests between Australia and Japan. • increased recognition in Japan of Australian excellence and expertise. • greater involvement of emerging leaders and youth in shaping the future of bilateral cooperation. • visible inclusion of diverse voices and communities. • creation of digital asset s that can be used to promote the activity and contribute to AJF’s broader communications efforts. Total Amount Available (AUD): $850,000.00 Estimated Grant Value (AUD): From $50,000.00 to $150,000.00 TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: UGV to support robotic autonomous system
opportunities.description: LCD-26-0009 - UGV to support Robotic Autonomous System Trial / Test & Evaluation Requirements RAS Breaching 1.Purpose This procurement is for a trial to evaluate Robotic and Autonomous Systems (RAS) solutions capable of augmenting under armour breaching to reduce risk, accelerate tempo, and enhance persistence in contested environments. Participation does not constitute a commitment to purchase. 2.Background Breaching under fire remains one of the most hazardous land combat tasks, exposed to direct and indirect fires, explosive hazards, obstacles, and potential electronic warfare interference. RAS technologies have the potential to reduce human exposure, accelerate breach timelines, and enable human machine teaming. Experimentation is required to validate concepts and inform requirements for future acquisition and Army force design. 3.Trial Objectives Assess whether RAS solutions can augment under armour breaching, including obstacle reduction, wire breaching, and lane proof ing. Validate RAS performance in contested environments including GPS/EW degradation and UAS dense airspace. Evaluate integration with existing C2 systems. Determine COTS/MOTS maturity, safety, interoperability, and feasibility of service introduction within 24 months. 4.Scope of Trial Location: Conducted within Army training areas. Window: Delivery in Q2 2026 for trial execution in Q3 2026. Activities: o Breach under fire evolutions (wire/obstacle reduction, lane marking, proof ing). o Integration with combat engineer force elements . 5. Minimum Capability Requirements - Provide a RAS platform/subsystem that augments under armour breaching including obstacle and wire reduction. Mandatory - Be COTS/MOTS with feasible service introduction within 24 months, including safety documentation and compliance. Mandatory 6. Trial Scenarios UGV enabled breach force as part of a deliberate breach scenario. Breaching of dummy minefields Breaching of wire obstacles Breaching of obstacles in the urban environment proof ing of lanes through obstacles Marking of lanes through obstacles 7. Evaluation Methodology Structured mission threads: breach tasks, manoeuvre support, sustainment. Measured performance: time to breach, obstacle reduction effectiveness, lane proof accuracy, comms resilience, and safe state behaviours. After action review supported by system logs, ISR footage, and user debriefs. 9. Data Collection Requirements Usage logs, telemetry, sensor feeds. Communication status, EW impact, disruptions. Failures/abort events, maintenance actions, setup/pack down times. Survey/interview user feedback and ISR/UGV/UAS video records. 10. Supplier Support Requirements Operator and maintainer training materials. Provision of spares and tools. The Contractor shall provide technical support services sufficient to meet training requirements and to enable effective operational use of the Uncrewed Ground Vehicle (UGV). Ability for Defence apply additive manufacturing methods to spares to assure supply chains. Fault response times consistent with activity tempo. Safety case, documentation, configuration control . Break-fix support to maintain system availability and ensure UGVs remain operational throughout test, trial, and evaluation activities in September 2026 for approx. 5 days. 11. Deliverables RAS breaching systems and required access ories. Safety documentation, operator/maintainer manuals, SDS, configuration lists. Training packages, job aids, reference materials. 12. Risk & Compliance Compliance with Defence safety, standards, and autonomous system policies. Safe state behaviours during loss of comms or EW overload. Supplier liability and insurances appropriate for destructive trials. 13. Assumptions Army provides range access and safety governance. Suppliers provide on site reps and spares to sustain trial tempo. 14. Future Procurement Statement. Participation in this trial does not guarantee nor provide advantage in any future procurement. Other Instructions: condition s for Participation: Suppliers must be able to deliver the proposed system no later than 29 June 2026 to ensure participation in the scheduled Army trial. Delivery after this date will render the supplier unable to meet trial requirements. Timeframe for Delivery: NLT 29 June 2026 TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Notice: Pitch Day 2026 - decoy, deception, camouflage & concealment
opportunities.description: ASCA-PITCHDAY-26-AN2 - Advance Notice - ASCA Pitch Day 2026 - Decoy, Deception, Camouflage and Concealment The Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator’s (ASCA) mission is to accelerate the development and transition of asymmetric capabilities to the Australian Defence Force through innovation, in order to meet Defence priority needs. ASCA's Pitch Days provide industry and academia the opportunity to present their ideas to Defence, ensuring Defence remains aware of new capabilities and innovations. This advance notice is for the second of two Pitch Days in 2026, and will be an RFI on new capabilities and innovations that enable agile signature management through decoy, deception, camouflage and/or concealment. Responses to the RFI describing such capabilities and innovations - dubbed 'proposed solutions' - must address the Challenge Statement that fall under this theme of 'Decoy, Deception, Camouflage and Concealment'. Respondents to the RFI that propose promising solutions (as assessed by a panel of Defence subject matter experts) will be invited to pitch to an audience of senior Defence officials, other valued guests and members of the broader defence ecosystem at the Pitch Day event, that will coincide with the Land Forces International Land Defence Exposition 2026 in Perth, in October. Further information on this upcoming RFI, including the Challenge Statement, assessment criteria and Pitch Day event, will be made available from May 2026. Visit the ASCA website: www.asca.gov.au/opportunities This is an advance notice for an upcoming Request For Information (RFI). The 'Close Date & Time' applies to the notice and does not apply to the RFI. The closing date and time of the RFI will be disclosed once the RFI has been released. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: NOTE: This information is subject to change and should be verified through the AusTender Website. ASCA Innovation Challenges E: asca.innovationchallenges@defence.gov.au

opportunities.title: Research - pasture seeds program
opportunities.description: Open Call: Pasture Seeds Program 2026-27 AgriFutures Australia investments are driven by RD&E Plans that address the needs of industries and stakeholders, and ensure our priorities are industry and government driven. Our aim is to deliver research and innovation that aims to give our levy payers real returns, as well as prepare them to thrive into the future. The goal of the AgriFutures Pasture Seed Program is to support a thriving and collaborative Australian certified temperate pasture seeds industry. To ensure the Program goal is achieved, AgriFutures is seeking research proposals to address the investment priorities within the AgriFutures Pasture Seed Program RD&E Plan 2023-2028. Background AgriFutures Australia welcomes Preliminary Research Proposals, that address one or more of the strategies within the R&D objectives for the industry and outlined in the AgriFutures Pasture Seed Program RD&E Plan 2023-2028. The RD&E Plan is structured around three key priority areas: 1. Supporting production systems and agronomic advancements 2. Developing people 3. Connecting the value chain As a vital component of all animal production systems, the Pasture Seeds Program operates at a strategic level to ensure investments demonstrate robust value for money while achieving timely and appropriate outcomes for the pasture seeds industry. We also support and encourage proposals that demonstrate collaboration with animal production industries and projects or short-term support for existing and relevant research relevant to the pasture seeds industry objectives. Proposals must address strategies, consider the justification, be linked to an indicative activity, and deliver on a Key Performance Indicator (KPI) listed within each priority of the RD&E Plan. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Amy La Manager, Levied Industries E: pastureseeds@agrifutures.com.au End of period for questions or requests for information: 20 May 2026, 12PM (AEST) Open Call – applications close: 25 May 2026, 12PM (AEST) Open Call – Full Research Proposal (FRP) applications open: 22 June 2026, 12PM (AEST) Open Call – FRP applications close: 27 July 2026, 12PM (AEST)

opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior software engineer
opportunities.description: LH-05757 - Minimum of 3 Senior Software Engineer The Archer IRM SMEs support the existing Archer platform and associated solutions, while undertaking development activities to further enhance or build upon capabilities. This may include: 1. Design and development of new solutions 2. Enhancements to existing solutions 3. Patching, maintenance and defect remediation 4. Testing Key duties and responsibilities Duties may include some or all of the following: • Work within an existing project team, following an agile delivery model using the JIRA platform for backlog management . • Elicit, refine and document user requirements into user stories to support development. • Prepare functional solution design documentation to support the delivery of each development sprint. Development sprints are planned by the project team, and this resource will be expected to contribute to that planning. • Configure the Archer IRM solution to meet business requirements, including completing system integrations between Archer and other existing platforms. • Contribute to test planning and undertake unit testing prior to promoting changes to Test. • Showcase sprint outcomes to the business owner and Senior Responsible officer. • Support production release activities outside of core business hours. opportunities.title: Erosion & carbon benefits of buffel grass
opportunities.description: Erosion and Carbon Benefits of Buffel Grass Buffel grass pastures are of significant importance to red meat production in both northern and central Australia. Studies have also shown that cattle perform well on buffel-based pastures. As a persistent groundcover, buffel can rapidly re establish after rain or fire, stabilise soils and reduce dust/erosion; these attributes are valued by producers and land management organisations when rehabilitating degraded rangelands. However, recent nomination of buffel grass as a potential Weed of National Significance (WONS) has raised significant concerns among the many producers reliant on buffel. MLA is seeking applications from organisations or consortiums to conduct a project that defines the role buffel grass plays in reducing soil erosion, and increasing carbon sequestration, in Australian red meat production zones. This will complement an economic benefit assessment being undertaken in parallel. Applications should meet the requirements specified in the tender document, and include a completed preliminary application form available on the Project application guidelines and forms website. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: If you have questions about this tender, please contact: Alister Hawksford Program Manager – Sustainability Credentials E: ahawksford@mla.com.au M: 0423 659 140

opportunities.title: Invite only: Secure internet gateway penetration testing services
opportunities.description: PCS-03837 - Secure Internet Gateway (SIG) Penetration Testing services Requirements The successful supplier will deliver a comprehensive security assessment of CASA’s newly built and self-hosted Secure Internet Gateway (SIG). The objective is to identify vulnerabilities, evaluate the effectiveness of security control s, and provide actionable recommendations to strengthen the overall security posture of the gateway environment. The engagement will include: • Offensive security testing from both external (unauthenticated) and internal perspectives, including simulated attack scenarios such as lateral movement under an assumed compromise. • Secure configuration review of gateway technologies and key supporting systems. • Secure code review of all in-scope Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) relevant to the gateway services and secure administration. The successful supplier must provide: • Detailed test plans, including the proposed methodology, detailed scope, timelines, resourcing, and identification of key stakeholders. • Comprehensive test reports that clearly document findings, vulnerability ratings (using OWASP, CVSS, or another recognised standard), potential business/operational impact, and prioritised remediation recommendations. • Experience in testing Microsoft Azure cloud-native environments and virtual appliance-based firewall integrations based on Microsoft’s recommended landing zone hub and spoke architecture. • Experience with Zero Trust architecture assurance or related security transformation programs. • Demonstrated recent experience in delivering penetration testing services for government or similarly regulated organisations, including offensive security testing, secure configuration reviews, and Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) assessments for modern cloud native and hybrid scenarios. • Provide examples for each. • Ability to deliver all required artefacts, including a detailed test plan, comprehensive test reports with vulnerability ratings (using OWASP, CVSS or equivalent). • Team members that must hold relevant industry certifications (e.g., OSCP, OSCE, CREST or equivalent) and possess demonstrable experience conducting penetration testing and security assessments across cloud-native and hybrid networks in a federal government environment. Demonstrated sound understanding of Australian government protective security requirements. The supplier is to provide its proposed resourcing for the engagement, inclusive of CVs, and references from Australian federal government customers. The supplier will be required to adhere to strict confidentiality and security protocols throughout the engagement. Prior work in aviation, critical infrastructure, or other highly control led regulatory environments is desirable. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Development finance entity package for renewable energy zone
opportunities.description: ROI-2014632 - New England Renewable Energy Zone - Development Finance Entity Package - Call for Registrations of Interest Call for Registrations of Interest for the Development Finance Entity Package for the New England Renewable Energy Zone. Project goals EnergyCo is leading the planning and coordination of Renewable Energy Zones (REZs) across NSW to meet the NSW Government's vision set out in the Electricity Infrastructure Roadmap. The New England REZ is a critical energy project for NSW that will provide clean and reliable electricity to consumers for generations to come. The New England REZ network infrastructure project is the backbone of the New England REZ, and includes new transmission infrastructure, energy hubs and enabling infrastructure. The project is planned to be delivered in two stages to provide a transfer capacity of 6GW, with stage 1 to deliver 2.4GW by 2032 and stage 2 to deliver an additional 3.6GW by 2034. To lower costs for NSW electricity consumers, EnergyCo will procure a ‘Development Finance Entity’ to provide lower-risk financing for land, biodiversity, contingency and other development costs, which are typically funded by Government on major infrastructure projects. Additional information can be obtained from the EnergyCo website at: https://www.energyco.nsw.gov.au/ne. Scope The Development Finance Entity will be responsible for procuring and maintaining lower-risk financing for land, biodiversity, contingency and other development costs. The Development Finance Entity will not be exposed to construction and operational risks, providing an opportunity to efficiently raise finance. This will also boost investment certainty, lower overall project costs, provide opportunities for alternate financing and lower costs for NSW electricity consumers. The successful entity (or consortium) will be appointed under a regulated concession model. Recognising the long-term nature of the New England REZ, the Development Finance Entity Package presents a significant opportunity for an entity or a consortium to invest in a whole-of-life approach to the financing of the development and other related costs associated with New England REZ. EnergyCo is seeking registrations from interested parties with bona fide capability and experience to deliver the Development Finance Entity Package, either as a single entity, as a fully formed consortium or through the demonstration of the ability to procure a fully formed consortium. In their registration, interested parties must provide evidence that entities have a proven track record and appropriate capability and experience financing projects of similar scale. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Kathryn Coutts E: nerez.dfe@energyco.nsw.gov.au Additional details/instructions To receive the Call for Registrations of Interest, an entity must complete and duly execute a Confidentiality and Disclaimer Deed Poll and Individual Deed Poll available with this BuyNSW notice, and submit by email to EnergyCo: nerez.dfe@energyco.nsw.gov.au. Following receipt of completed and fully executed Confidentiality and Disclaimer Deed Poll(s) and Individual Deed Poll(s) from a bona fide entity, EnergyCo will provide access to the Call for Registrations of Interest to the entity via the Development Finance Entity Data Room. To submit a registration, an interested party must return all returnable schedules available in the Call for Registrations of Interest by email to nerez.dfe@energyco.nsw.gov.au before the close date.

opportunities.title: Design consultancy services for electrification of leisure centre (various)
opportunities.description: T016/2026 - Design Consultancy services for the Electrification of Mount Annan Leisure Centre Camden Council invites tenders for the Design Consultancy services for the Electrification of Mount Annan Leisure Centre. A detailed description of the requirements can be found in Council’s Request for Tender document. SCOPE OF WORKS Activity 1: Upgrade of Pool Hall and Spa Gas fired Air Handling Units (AHU) Activity 2: Electrification of Domestic Hot water Units Activity 3: Behind the Meter Renewable Energy Generation Activity 4: Electrification of Cooking Appliances SCOPE OF services The Consultant shall provide comprehensive, multidisciplinary design services required to deliver the electrification upgrade at Mount Annan Leisure Centre. The services shall include investigation, design development, documentation, coordination of sub-consultants, and support during construction. The Consultant will be responsible for leading and coordinating all design disciplines and ensuring that all outputs are fully integrated and aligned with project objectives. This includes reviewing existing documentation and feasibility studies, validating design assumptions, and developing practical and cost-effective design solutions. A key requirement of the scope is the integrated design of the facilit y’s thermal systems, including the coordinated operation of heat pump systems with pool water heating and pool hall air handling ( HVAC ) systems. The Consultant shall ensure that all heating, ventilation, and associated energy systems are designed as a unified and optimized thermal system to deliver stable environmental condition s, improved efficiency, and reliable operation within a live aquatic facilit y. All designs must comply with relevant Australian Standards, the National Construction Code, and applicable authority requirements. The Consultant shall ensure that all deliverables are suitable for tendering and construction purposes. Documentation shall be sufficient for a construct only Tender to be advertised and successfully delivered. The Consultant is also responsible for identifying and mitigating all design, investigation, and integration risks associated with delivering the project within an existing operational facilit y and shall incorporate appropriate design responses to ensure a coordinated, compliant, and buildable outcome. 1 Review & Validation • Review all existing documentation, including: o Electrification Feasibility Study o Type 2 Energy audit (AS/NZS 3598) o Existing drawings, asset data, and reports • Validate feasibility assumptions and confirm: o System capacities o Spatial requirements o Integration constraints • Identify gaps, risks, and opportunities 2 Site Investigations & Existing condition s • Undertake detailed site inspections and investigations, including: o Existing plantrooms and services o electrical infrastructure (switchboards, loads, distribution) o Mechanical systems (boilers, AHUs, ductwork) o Hydraulic systems (hot water , pipework) o Roof condition and structural capacity (for solar PV) o Existing BMS system • Confirm: o Available space for new plant (internal/external) o access , constructability, and staging constraints • Coordinate and manage: o Structural assessments o Hazardous materials (if required) o Service locating and utility investigations 3 Concept & Options Development • Develop design options for: o Heat pump systems (ASHP, CO₂ systems) o Plantroom layouts (internal vs external) o Domestic Hot water (DHW) system configurations o Solar PV layouts • Assess options against: o Cost o Energy performance o Constructability o Operational impacts • Present recommended option to Council • Recommended Option Report including justification 4 Detailed Design (All Disciplines) Mechanical services • Design of: o Air Source Heat Pump systems (~1,100kW) o Hydronic air handling systems o Heat recovery systems o Ventilation systems (100% outside air) • Full integration of new heat pump systems with existing pool water heating and pool hall HVAC systems, ensuring coordinated thermal control , energy sharing where applicable, and stable temperature and humidity condition s within the aquatic environment • Plant sizing, redundancy, staging strategy and acoustic performance review. • Recommended maintenance schedules and required equipment clearance for ease of access for routine inspections and maintenance . • Durability in varying environmental condition s electrical services • Assessment and upgrade (or augmentation) of the Main Switchboard (MSB) • Load analysis and electrical capacity assessment • New power distribution for: o Heat pumps o DHW systems o Kitchen equipment • Solar PV system design (~180kW) • Integration with Council systems Hydraulic services • Design of: o CO2 heat pump hot water systems o Storage tanks and reticulation • Pipework modifications and integration • Drainage and overflow provisions Structural Engineering • Assessment of: o Roof capacity for solar PV o Plant loads and supports • Design of: o Plant platforms and supports o Structural modifications (if required) BMS & control s • Design of upgraded Building management System (BMS) • Integration of: o Mechanical systems o Energy monitoring systems • Optimisation for: o Energy efficiency o Operational control The primary objective of this upgrade is to integrate all systems into a single Building management System (BMS), enabling centralised control , enhanced automation, and compatibility across all systems, along with remote monitoring capabilities for efficient operation and performance management . Ability to integrate with existing renewable energy systems. 5 Coordination & Integration • Lead full multidisciplinary coordination • Ensure: o All systems are integrated and functional o No clashes between disciplines • Coordinate with: o Council stakeholders o facilit y operator (e.g., BlueFit) o Utility providers (e.g., Endeavour Energy) 6 Approvals & Compliance 7 Cost Planning & Value Engineering 8 Tender & Construction Documentation 9 Construction Phase services 10 Commissioning & Handover Support TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Diane Ross P: 132263 E: quotes@camden.nsw.gov.au

opportunities.title: Occupancy opportunity
opportunities.description: BDA001283 - Call for Occupant Expressions of Interest This is a Call for Occupant Expressions of Interest (EOI). It invites organisations interested in locating within Bradfield City’s Enterprise Precinct, as either an owner occupier or tenant, to register their interest ahead of a future market process to appoint a master developer for the site. Expressions of Interest are invited from businesses and other organisations interested in occupancy within the Enterprise Precinct that demonstrate: • Commitment to contributing to the vision of an innovation-driven Enterprise Precinct. • Industrial or operational activities that contribute to advanced technologies, research, collaboration or knowledge sharing in the Precinct. In particular, activities in advanced industries such as aerospace, clean technology, construction & advanced materials, defence, education & research, freight & logistics technologies, advanced manufacturing, semiconductor technology or circular economy, or other activities that support the Precinct’s growth and economic development goals. • An interest or willingness to be part of a thriving sustainable Enterprise Precinct by collaborating with other entities that locate here and seek to leverage proximity to the NSW Government’s $300M investment in the Advanced Manufacturing Readiness facilit y (AMRF). TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Interested parties may submit an Expression of Interest by completing the Returnable Schedule at Part E and lodging an EOI Response electronically via the Lodgement Portal (reference: BDA001283).

opportunities.title: Recyclables receival & processing services
opportunities.description: CPA/6743 - Recyclables Receival and Processing services Central Coast Council invites submissions from suitably qualified and experienced contractors for the provision of Recyclables Receival and Processing services . This Request for Tender (RFT) forms part of Council’s broader program to modernise waste and resource recovery service delivery and to establish a new long-term processing arrangement to replace existing contractual arrangements. The RFT seeks to appoint a contractor capable of delivering reliable, transparent and high-performing recyclables processing services that support Council’s environmental objectives, community expectations and regulatory obligations. The proposed contract term is: • Initial Term: 10 years (commencing Monday, 1 March 2027) • Extension Options: 3 years + 2 years (at Council’s discretion) • Maximum Term: 15 years. Currently Council has an existing contract to process dry recyclables from the yellow bin services which ends January 2027. Council has a statutory obligation to provide Domestic Waste Collection services under the Local Government Act 1993 and obligations to divert waste from landfill in accordance with the waste minimisation hierarchy under the Waste Avoidance and Resource Recovery Act 2001. The ‘Yellow Bin’ recycling service is well established internationally and across Australia and is recognised as a core service as part of the Domestic Waste Collections provided to communities. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Kellie Neville Procurement Officer P: 43047098 E: kellie.nevilIe@centralcoast.nsw.gov.au

opportunities.title: Audit, Risk & Improvement Committee - independent member
opportunities.description: EOI - Independent Member to ARIC Uralla Shire Council is seeking Expressions of Interest (EOI) from people interested in joining the Uralla Shire (USC) and Walcha Councils' (WC) audit , Risk and Improvement Committee (ARIC) as an External Independent Member, with both councils seeking a common external member. Both USC and WC seek to leverage shared experiences in similar matters of governance for issues that are often common across each Council, both in size and scope. Each ARIC meeting will be scheduled for the same day with travel between each Council of approximately 45KM. ARIC agenda business paper formats are sent electronically to each member. The Councils are inviting expressions of Interest from suitably qualified individuals for appointment as an external independent member to the USC and WC ARICs. The audit , Risk and Improvement Committee is a key component of Councils’ governance frameworks. The Committees will be responsible for assisting Councils to fulfil their governance and oversight responsibilities. The audit , Risk and Improvement Committee is comprised of three independent external members. Each Council has nominated a councillor to the committee (non-voting). Both Councils will be expecting the new member to commence duties at the audit , Risk and Improvement Committee meeting to be held 1 June 2026. An induction will be scheduled in May. The Committee meets at least four times a year. Currently we have scheduled meetings for 1 June 2026, 7 September 2026 and 7 December 2026, The Committee is a formally appointed Committee of each Council. The appointment will be for a four-year term. The current meeting fees for an independent member is $750 per meeting per Council. In addition, Council will cover reasonable travel and accommodation costs. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: For further information, contact: Steven Williams P: (02) 6778 6300 E: council@uralla.nsw.gov.au Complete the nomination form on the link provided and submit to: E: council@uralla.nsw.gov.au

opportunities.title: Funding - Short-term property spaces (creative & cultural use)
opportunities.description: Bring Your Cultural and Creative Ideas to Life in Redfern Apply for one of our short-term property spaces at 107 Redfern Street. Use of 107 Redfern Street At its meeting on 17 March 2025, Council resolved to prioritise local Aboriginal creative and cultural use of 107 Redfern Street. Redfern is recognised as a significant place of Aboriginal life, resistance and self-determination, with an enduring history of community-led movements, cultural pride and leadership. About this opportunity We have 6 spaces available at 107 Redfern Street, Redfern for cultural and creative use through our short-term empty properties program. This 2-storey industrial-style facilit y is in the heart of Redfern. • Tenancy length: 12 months • Cost: $100 a week per space plus GST. • Consideration may be given to individual circumstances and rates may vary. For detailed information about the spaces, read the applicant information pack. Cultural and creative leaders, artists, knowledge holders and creative workers are invited to apply for a space, a combination of spaces, the full ground floor or first floor. All spaces will be unfurnished. We strongly encourage Aboriginal-led and community- control led organisations and cultural practitioners to apply. This opportunity is designed to activate 107 Redfern Street while we consider its future as a centre for Aboriginal creativity and culture. Eligible applicants can apply for a cash grant from the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander collaboration fund to support the activation, establishment or programming of the spaces. About the short-term empty properties program We own several commercial and community properties, including retail and office space. These properties may be empty for short periods between tenancies. The short-term empty properties program provides immediate and temporary occupancy of under-used properties within our portfolio. The properties are offered for at least one month. Our program supports: • creative practitioners and cultural organisations • community organisations and social enterprises • businesses and startups that support the local economy • organisations with a focus on sustainability and the environment. Preference is given to applicants in our area or to projects that can demonstrate significant benefit to local communities. Funding priorities • Increasing opportunities for local Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander organisations • Activities that reflect our cultural, social, economic and environmental strategies and action plans • Boosting connection with the local community, including artists and creative organisations, small business and visitors • Stimulating local economies by encouraging a diverse business mix with innovative or capacity building enterprises • Furthering opportunities and exposure for emerging and established enterprises including artists. Eligibility You must show how your proposal benefits our residents, workers and visitors. Applicants must demonstrate that payment of standard rates would cause financial hardship. These individuals and organisations can apply: • Eligible not-for-profit organisation • Incorporated association • Unincorporated community group auspiced by an eligible not-for-profit organisation • Corporation • Co-operative • Partnership • Sole trader • Trustee of a trust. This program is open to for-profit entities. For the definition of each eligible applicant above. See the glossary/definitions section in the grants and sponsorship guidelines. For more information about eligibility, see the grants and sponsorship guidelines. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Grants team P: 02 9265 9333 E: grants@cityofsydney.nsw.gov.au Attend an open day or information session Visit 107 Redfern Street to walk through the spaces on offer and hear how to apply. opportunities.title: Commercial advisory services for transmission project
opportunities.description: SR01038 - Commercial Advisory services for Hunter Transmission Project EnergyCo seeks one qualified provider with an experienced commercial team to manage commercial and regulatory functions for the Hunter Transmission Project during delivery. Project goals The NSW Government has developed the NSW Electricity Infrastructure Roadmap (the Roadmap) to transition NSW to a cleaner future and ensure this new supply of electricity can be reliably distributed to consumers. To maintain the energy supply to the Hunter, Sydney and Illawarra regions, 500 kilovolt (kV) transmission connections are required between Eraring Power Station and Bays water Power Station in Liddell in the Upper Hunter Valley. The HTP is both a critical State significant infrastructure (CSSI) project and a Priority Network Infrastructure Project (PNIP). As the remaining coal-fired power stations close, the HTP is critical for energy security in New South Wales (NSW). Scope EnergyCo seeks to engage a single qualified Service Provider to deliver an experienced Commercial team that will support the Hunter Transmission Project during the delivery phase. The Commercial team will provide specialist commercial, regulatory, financial and contract management capability to ensure strong oversight of the project’s commercial and regulatory functions, including management of interfaces with the Network Operator and Third Parties. The Service Provider will supply a team of six professional roles forming a core component of EnergyCo’s delivery‑phase Commercial function. These resources will maintain oversight of commercial, legal and regulatory activities, contribute to project governance, and help ensure effective delivery of key milestones. They will operate as part of an integrated project team, working closely with EnergyCo staff and stakeholders to support timely decision‑making, manage risks and maintain continuity across commercial activities. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Electric vehicle fleet charging infrastructure
opportunities.description: SCM13381 - NSW Government Electric Vehicle Fleet Charging Infrastructure Prequalification Scheme Description Deliver the full scope of electric vehicle supply equipment goods, installation works and maintenance services . Scope The Electric Vehicle Fleet Charging Infrastructure Scheme is used by NSW Government Agencies and Non-Agency Eligible customers such as State owned corporations, councils and not-for-profit (“Buyers”). ​ ​The approved supplier must provide quotations as requested by an eligible buyer to be considered for work, and must not charge over the maximum rates submitted under the scheme. The supplier may submit lower rates and prices than the rates and prices under the scheme rates card when tendering for a project with an eligible buyer.​ ​The scope of work, fees and other requirements for a project agreement will be formed using the standard form agreement (see supporting documents). Approved suppliers under the scheme either directly, or via the engagement of its selected sub-contractors remain responsible for the delivery of the EVSE goods, works and services under the project agreement formed.​ ​At the completion of each project the approved suppliers must submit a detailed project report to the Department. --- The NSW Government committed in 2021 to transitioning the government fleet of passenger vehicles to electric by 2030 as part of the NSW Electric Vehicle Strategy. https://www.energy.nsw.gov.au/sites/default/files/2022-09/nsw-electric-vehicle-strategy-210225.pdf Specifically, the Government set out goals to have 50% of newly purchased/leased passenger vehicles to be electric by 2026 and 100% of newly purchased/leased passenger vehicles to be electric by 2030. Achieving these targets requires Electric Vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure to be set up at vehicle base locations such as NSW Government sites and depots. The NSW Government Electric Vehicle Fleet Charging Infrastructure Prequalification Scheme SCM13381 (“Scheme”) is open to applications from companies seeking to be prequalified to deliver electrical Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE) Goods, Works and services under Capability A. Capability A - electrical Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE) Goods, Works and services (as defined under the Scheme condition s). Applicants must accept the Scheme condition s and satisfy all requirements as a condition of prequalification under the Scheme. Prequalification under the Scheme is not a guarantee of work, continuity of the inclusion on the Scheme, receipt of opportunities to make submissions; or that engagements or work of any kind or quantity will be offered. The Scheme will be open for an initial term of three (3) years. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Surface-based civil infrastructure works
opportunities.description: RFT-BUR-1009A - Surface Civils Expression of Interest Queensland Hydro is seeking Expressions of Interest from suitably qualified and experienced construction contractors to deliver surface-based civil infrastructure works in support of the Borumba Pumped Hydro Energy Storage (PHES) Project Exploratory Works. The scope comprises enabling surface civil works required to provide safe access , working areas, and temporary water supply to support exploratory tunnelling, geotechnical drilling, and associated investigation activities across the project site. The successful respondent will be appointed as Principal Contractor and will be responsible for overall site management , including interface coordination within a multi-contractor and environmentally constrained operating environment. The works are located in steep, undeveloped terrain and require a contractor with demonstrated capability in managing complex interfaces, traffic and access management , and engagement and coordination of specialist subcontractors. The Principal Contractor will also be responsible for sourcing, managing, and coordinating geotechnical drilling subcontractors in accordance with Queensland Hydro requirements. The Exploratory Works are investigative in nature and are distinct from the Borumba PHES Main Works. They will provide critical geotechnical and site information to inform design development, future project procurement, and preparation of the Project Environmental Impact Statement (EIS). This Expression of Interest forms the first stage of a two-stage procurement process: Expression of Interest (EOI): April – May 2026 Request for Tender (RFT): June 2026 (shortlisted respondents only) Anticipated Contract Award: September 2026. opportunities.title: Drone services
opportunities.description: RFT000028 - Provision of Drone services The objective of the drone service is to provide a safe, efficient, and high-quality aerial capability to support operational, safety, environmental, and asset management activities across the Port of Townsville precinct. The service is intended to enhance situational awareness, improve the timeliness and accuracy of data collection, reduce reliance on manual or high-risk inspection methods, and support informed decision-making. Drone operations are expected to be conducted in compliance with all applicable CASA aviation, maritime, safety, security , and privacy requirements while minimising disruption to Port of Townsville operations. The Port of Townsville operates within a complex, high-risk environment characterised by critical infrastructure, vessel movements, heavy machinery, and constrained access areas. Traditional inspection and monitoring methods can be resource intensive, pose safety risks, and may not provide the level of coverage or frequency required for effective port management . Drones now provide a reliable means of capturing high-resolution imagery and data over land and water , enabling more proactive maintenance , enhanced monitoring, and improved incident response. As a result, the Port of Townsville seeks to engage a qualified service provider to deliver professional drone services aligned with its operational and regulatory context. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Design and construction of new dwellings
opportunities.description: YASC-2026-004 - RFT - Ambrym Street Design and Construction of 10 X New Dwellings The Ambrym Street Housing Project comprises the design and construction of ten (10) new residential dwellings within a proposed subdivision at Ambrym Street. The development will deliver a mix of dwelling types in accordance with the issued architectural drawings and site plan. The Contractor is responsible for the full design development, coordination and construction of the Works, including all associated civil works, services infrastructure and external works required to deliver a complete and operational housing development. The Works shall be delivered in accordance with Remote Housing Standards and all applicable codes, standards and authority requirements, and shall result in dwellings that are fit for purpose and ready for occupation. The Scope is described in the document(s) attached to or provided with this Request for Tender and identified as: Scope of Works (Design & Construct) The Contractor shall undertake the complete design, coordination, certification and construction of ten (10) new residential dwellings at Ambrym Street Stage 1 as follows; • House Type 1 x four (4) • House Type 2 x three (3) • House Type 3 x one (1) • House Type 4 x two (2) The Works include, but are not limited to, all labour, materials, plant, equipment, design, approvals and activities necessary to deliver a fully operational, compliant and complete housing development fit for purpose and ready for handover and occupation. Reference Documents • Architectural Drawings: o 2601 Yarrie Homes – House Type 1 (2 Bed Duplex) o 2601 Yarrie Homes – House Type 2 (3 Bed) o 2601 Yarrie Homes – House Type 3 (4 Bed) o 2601 Yarrie Homes – House Type 4 (4 Bed) • Site Plan: o 2331-A005 (16) Ambrym St Proposed Subdivision – Lots + Houses These documents define the design intent only and do not limit the Contractor’s obligations under this Contract. Key Requirements • The Contractor is responsible for the complete design development, coordination and certification of the Works. • The Contractor shall ensure the Works comply with all applicable legislation, codes, standards and authority requirements. • The Works shall be delivered as a complete and functional development, notwithstanding any omissions or discrepancies in the reference documentation. • A mandatory Indigenous Employment and Business Participation (IEBP) Plan shall be submitted with the Tender. Failure to submit an IEBP may result in the Tender being deemed non-conforming. Finished Floor Levels (FFL) • Finished Floor Levels (FFL) has been provided and detailed on the below; o ARO0319-C13 (SEWER) - C13 Scope of Works Without limiting the Contractor’s obligations, the Works include but are not limited to: Design & Approvals • Detailed building designs of all dwellings and associated infrastructure • Design coordination across all disciplines • Preparation of all documentation required for construction, approvals and certification o The Contractor must submit SD stage drawings to Dept. of Housing for feedback and approval prior to progressing to CD. • Obtaining all approvals, permits and authority clearances Site Establishment & Civil Works • Site establishment, access , temporary services and control s • Clearing, grubbing, demolition if required and site preparation (demolition of existing dwellings is excluded from this tender) • Bulk earthworks, cut and fill, trimming and compaction incl. testing if applicable • Set-out of all works in accordance with approved design and issued FFLs • Storm water drainage, overland flow management and lawful discharge Residential Construction • Construction of ten dwellings in accordance with nominated house types • Masonry block construction to all dwellings • All works to be in accordance with Remote Housing Standards (RHS) or as otherwise nominated • Complete construction of each dwelling including all structural, architectural and building elements necessary to achieve a fully functional and compliant dwelling • Supply and installation of all fixtures, fittings and appliances • Kitchens, bathrooms and laundries including joinery services & Utilities • All hydraulic services including water , sewer and drainage • electrical reticulation, metering, switchboards and internal installations • Communications and data provisions as typically required • Coordination and connection to mains External & Ancillary Works • Driveways, pathways, hardstand areas and access ways • Fencing, gates and boundary treatments • landscap ing, reinstatement and making good of all disturbed areas • Installation of external fixtures including T-Bar clotheslines, external sink and bins Testing, Commissioning & Handover • Testing and commissioning of all services and systems • Rectification of defects and completion of outstanding works • Provision of as-constructed documentation, manuals and warranties • Final certification and approvals for occupancy • Handover of a complete, operational and compliant development Contractor Obligations • The Contractor shall verify all site condition s, dimensions, levels and service locations prior to commencing Works. • The Contractor shall be deemed to have allowed for all condition s that may reasonably be anticipated from available information and site inspection. • No claim will be accepted for matters arising from failure to adequately investigate site condition s or coordinate the Works. • The Contractor is responsible for all coordination, interfaces and integration of the Works, whether expressly stated or reasonably inferred. Project Specification • Design and Construction Standards for Remote Housing – August 2025 • National Construction Code (NCC) The Contractor shall comply with the current edition of the National Construction Code (NCC), all applicable Australian Standards (AS/NZS), and all relevant statutory and regulatory requirements. It shall be the Contractor’s responsibility to identify and comply with all standards applicable to the Works, whether they are specifically listed in this document. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior Microsoft 365 application developer
opportunities.description: LH-06264 - 1 Senior Microsoft 365 Application developer The Bureau seeks a contractor to design, implement, migrate, and operationalise a data management solution for the Flood Warning Infrastructure Network (FWIN) program. The solution will be implemented on Microsoft Power Apps + Microsoft Dataverse, with automation via Power Automate and integration hooks to GIS and Power BI, to improve data integrity, governance, user experience, audit ability, and integration. Background to Requirement FWIN currently manages asset acquisition and lifecycle data using multiple SharePoint Lists. This approach has reached its limits, resulting in recurring issues with data quality, relational constraints, audit ability, security , workflow orchestration, and integrations (notably breaks in Power BI reports and manual GIS export steps). A future-state evaluation compared: Option 1: Power Apps + Dataverse Option 2: Single consolidated SharePoint List The analysis found Power Apps + Dataverse best meets FWIN’s needs for relational data structures, robust governance and security , workflow automation, scalability, audit ability, and reliable analytics/GIS integrations. The contractor will deliver this future state and transition FWIN from SharePoint Lists to Dataverse as source of truth. This role will be located in the Bureau's Melbourne office. Objectives and Outcomes • Establish a Dataverse data model, configured security , and workflow automation to support FWIN lifecycle management . • Improve data integrity with validated forms, enforced business rules and quality checks. • Enable consistent, reliable integrations with GIS and Power BI. • Provide an administered environment with audit history, role-based access , and clear governance. • Deliver documentation, training and handover to internal staff. Outcomes • A single source of truth in Dataverse with robust relational integrity and field-level control s. • control led, guided user experience (Power App) that reduces errors and supports lifecycle workflows. • Analytics through Power BI and an automated GIS export pattern (where feasible). • Improved data governance: audit ability, approvals, change control , and stewardship processes. • Operational readiness: runbook, support model, and transferred knowledge. Key duties and responsibilities Full Requirements – Responsibilities 1. Comply with all Bureau work, health and safety policies and procedures, and take reasonable care for your own health and safety and that of employees, contractors and visitors who may be affected by your conduct. 2. Commitment to APS Values, Employment Principles, Code of Conduct and the Bureau's Social Justice Strategy. 3. The contractor will operate within the Bureau's M365/Power Platform environment and comply with all Bureau ICT security policies for OFFICIAL information. The contractor is required to hold a Baseline security clearance or ability to obtain one. Knowledge and Skills Required Functional Requirements 1. Data Model & Forms - Implement a relational data model in Dataverse covering core FWIN entities (e.g., sites, asset s, lifecycle states, metadata, reference data). - Provide validated, guided forms with business rules, condition al logic, and field-level validation. - Enforce mandatory fields by lifecycle stage; prevent invalid state transitions. 2. Governance, security & audit - Implement role-based access control (RBAC) and field-level security (team/role based). - Enable audit ing (create/update/delete, security /role changes, configuration changes) and maintain version history for key records. - Provide a change control approach for configuration. 3. Workflow & Automation - Configure business process flows for lifecycle management . - Implement scheduled data quality checks using Power Automate where appropriate. - Configure notifications/approvals for key events (e.g., state changes, validation failures). 4. Integrations - Establish reliable Power BI dataflows/datasets for analytics. - Design and, where feasible in this engagement, implement an automated GIS export pattern to eliminate manual steps (or propose a staged approach). 5. Migration - Plan and execute migration from current SharePoint Lists (FReD) to Dataverse, including data profiling, cleansing rules, mapping, and reconciliation. 6. Documentation & Handover - Deliver solution design, data dictionary, security model, workflow specs, integration design, operational runbook (backup/restore, incident, access ), and admin/user guides. - Provide training (admin + user) and knowledge transfer sessions with recordings and quick reference guides. Non-Functional Requirements • security & Privacy: Align to Bureau ICT security policies for OFFICIAL data. • Compliance: Support records/archives and applicable Australian Government policies and standards. Key Performance Indicators • Data Quality Pass Rate:100% • Migration Reconciliation: 100% • security & audit : 100% of privileged changes logged; 0 unauthorised access incidents attributable to solution design. • Documentation & Handover: All artefacts delivered, reviewed, and accepted; = 85% training satisfaction (post-session survey). • Delivery Timeliness: Milestones achieved within ±10% of agreed baseline schedule. Standards The Contractor must ensure the solution aligns with the following, as applicable to OFFICIAL information handled within the Bureau’s O365 environment. Documentation Contractor must produce and maintain (at minimum): • Solution Design (current/future-state architecture, entity definitions, relationships, rules) • security Model (roles, field-level security ) • Workflow & Automation Specs • Integration Design (Power BI, GIS export pattern, interfaces) • Migration Plan & Report (profiling, cleansing, mapping, test results, reconciliation outcomes) • Operational Runbook (monitoring, backup/restore, incident & change procedures, access requests) • User & Admin Guides; Training materials and recordings • Test Plans and Evidence (functional, integration, performance, security ) TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior product software engineer
opportunities.description: LH-06260 - 1 Senior Product Software Engineer (Developer) We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Senior Product Developer & SME to support and build upon a product generation framework based on Quadient's Inspire Flex product. The successful candidate will be responsible for supporting projects with the analysis required to feed into the design and build of templates, creating standard templates and patterns for future projects, providing technical expertise on Inspire Flex to projects and support teams, uplifting the BAU support team's skills to provide ongoing capability, deploying the software to production, and supporting projects in development and test. Additionally, the candidate should have technical and thought leadership skills to coach others and help develop their capability with this specialist software. Key duties and responsibilities The successful candidate will have responsibility to ensure the Automatic Weather Station Network meets the Bureau's current and future needs. To deliver this, the position holder will be broadly responsible to undertake the following duties: 1. Demonstrate comprehensive knowledge of data acquisition (DAQ), remote terminal (RTU) or other monitoring equipment (operation, maintenance , and integration with other systems), particularly in relation to data ingestion and external control mechanisms. 2. Review, problem find and maintain software and scripts in Python and C++, in alignment with established development processes. 3. Support the planning and execution of equipment testing activities to ensure reliability and performance. 4. Interpret strategic objectives and outcomes, identify potential challenges and uncertainties, and incorporate these insights into planning, decision-making, and implementation processes. 5. Collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders, fostering clear communication and positively influencing team outcomes. 6. Assess delivered equipment and systems to ensure compliance with technical requirements, legislation, policies, and regulatory standards. 7. Review activities to identify success factors and actively contribute to continuous improvement and innovation initiatives. Knowledge and Skills Required Mandatory Extensive technical and hands-on experience in the development of complex monitoring systems, incorporating: Embedded micro- control ler data acquisition and processing systems, focusing on software development in high level programming/scripting languages, Python – mandatory and C++ advantageous. Strong understanding of sensor technologies, interfacing methods, and custom measurement systems. LINUX system administration relevant to embedded/field hardware, backend systems and sensors. Demonstrated proficiency in software engineering, with expertise across the full software development lifecycle (requirements analysis, specification development, implementation, verification, and review). Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent Desirable • Creation and execution of test plans, code management , and performance analysis and reporting. • In depth understanding of communications technologies (Serial, Ethernet, analogue, RF), data transmission protocols (TCP Sockets, FTP, MQTT, SNMP) and remote communications (modem, cellular, satellite). • Development and construction of prototypes, including bench and field based implementations. • Proven capability in designing, implementing, and maintaining systems that support accurate and reliable observations Essential criteria Technical competence • Demonstrates the right skills and experience, a good understanding of the requirements, and works in an agile way. • Technical competence is also assessed by reviewing the candidates mandatory and desirable skills and experience. Experience • Demonstrated previous experience in a similar role. • Delivers on intended results and outcomes. Capability • Availability and capacity to undertake the role. • Ability to develop and contribute to innovative solutions. • Demonstrated ability to make decisions, involve others as appropriate, and resolve issues. Cultural contribution • How well the candidate will contribute to the organisation's culture. • Candidate's values and behaviour are similar to the organisations. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Employee assistance program
opportunities.description: FOL/25/7998 - WorkSafe Victoria Employee Assistance Program Work Safe Victoria is seeking the services of a suitably qualified organisation(s) to provide an Employee Assistance Program, Critical incident, and proactive Early Intervention services at all sites and locations of work. WorkSafe is seeking responses for the delivery of these services from October 2026, for an initial three-year term, with the option for extension by a further three-year term. Service - Employee Assistance Program (EAP) / Critical Incident (CI) EAP - WorkSafe’s EAP aims to assist and support WorkSafe employees reduce stress by resolving or working through personal, family and/or workplace issues. The EAP is a short term, confidential, professional, psychological counselling service, typically initiated by the employee. Employees retain responsibility for their own treatment and other choices. EAP services include telephone, online and face to face counselling options. CI - CI services are based on early intervention principles, to support challenging workplace events that have a probability of causing employee distress. Service - Early Intervention (EI) EI - programs are proactive psychological coaching services to employees in teams at risk of cumulative trauma. They are considered best practice control s in high-risk settings where employees have frequent exposure to traumatic events and content, violence and aggression, contacts in distress or contacts who demonstrate unreasonable and vexatious behaviours. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Community inspired festive banners / public artwork
opportunities.description: EOI Now Open for Artist to Create Community Inspired Festive Banners Exciting opportunity for a local artist Local artists are invited to apply for an exciting opportunity to create high-visibility public artwork in the heart of Margaret River The Shire of Augusta Margaret River is calling for Expressions of Interest from local creatives to design a new series of banners that celebrate the festive season in a way that is meaningful to our community. The project will see the successful artist run one to two creative workshops with school aged children, with their ideas brought together in professionally finished festive banners celebrating the Augusta Margaret River region. The final artworks will be installed along Margaret River’s main street in the lead-up to Christmas and throughout the peak tourist season. This initiative will help create a vibrant streetscape during the holiday season, in a way that reflects our people and place to foster community spirit and a sense of connection, joy, and togetherness. It’s also a fantastic opportunity for a local artist to gain exposure during the busiest time of the year. --- Scope of artwork The scope of this project is to create a unique suite of 2-3 original banner artworks, to be reproduced and display ed throughout the Margaret River main street during the festive holiday season, for a period of up to five years. As part of the creative process, the commissioned artist will be required to plan and deliver 1-2 creative workshops with school aged children. These workshops will generate visual material that is to be meaningfully and recognisably incorporated into the final, professionally resolved artworks, ensuring participating children can identify their creative contributions once the banners are installed. The artist will be responsible for all aspects of workshop delivery, including facilit ation, materials, associated costs and digitisation of artwork for inclusion in the final designs. The Margaret River Zone Room can be utilised as a workshop venue for no additional cost, pending availability. A total of 28 banners are located along the main street. The selected 2-3 designs will be reproduced across these banners. The artworks must be complementary in style, colour, and overall design, with the final digital design file provided in line with the provided template (as outlined in attachment Avia link provided). The arrangement of banners along the main street (e.g., alternating, in blocks, colour transition or other) can be proposed by the artist to best display the artworks as a street wide installation. The artist is responsible for delivering finished artwork using supplied template/s. The Shire is responsible for banner production and installation. 5. Budget The fee available for the suite of two to three artworks is $4,500 ex GST. There is an additional allowance of $2,500 ex GST to develop and run children’s creative workshops to engage children in the artistic process and to gain elements for incorporation into design work. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Please address any queries or requests for information to Tanya Perdue, Arts & Economic Development Coordinator on (08) 9780 5231 or tperdue@amrshire.wa.gov.au Expressions of Interest can either be submitted by email to: tperdue@amrshire.wa.gov.au with the subject line EOI Submission - Main Street Christmas Banner Artwork OR Sent as a hard copy in a sealed envelope, addressed as follows: EOI Submission - Main Street Christmas Banner Artwork Attention: Tanya Perdue Arts & Economic Development Coordinator Shire of Augusta Margaret River PO Box 61 Margaret River WA 6285

opportunities.title: Evaluating triage & deconfliction practices in countering violent extremism contexts
opportunities.description: WAPOL15125 - Evaluating Triage and Deconfliction Practices for Mixed, Unclear, and Unstable Youth in Countering Violent Extremism Contexts The Western Australia Police Force requires a qualified organisation to support improvements to its triage and deconfliction processes for individuals who may present grievance-fuelled or ideologically motivated risks. Clear and consistent practices between the WA Fixated Threat Assessment Centre (WAFTAC) and the WA Intervention and Support Program (WAISP) are essential to ensuring that individuals are appropriately directed to the most suitable intervention pathway. To assist with this, the Western Australia Police Force seeks a supplier to undertake a comparative evaluation of current practices and provide practical policy guidance. The services will include research, analysis of existing processes, stakeholder engagement, and the preparation of a policy recommendations report supported by a validation workshop and final revised report. The WAFTAC and WAISP teams are located at the Maylands Police Complex, 21 Swan BankRoad, Maylands WA 6051. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Oil field, facilities & infrastructure decommissioning phase (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: asset Retirement Chevron Australia Pty Ltd (Chevron Australia) is operator of the Barrow Island Joint Venture (known as WA Oil), located on Barrow Island off the northwest coast of Western Australia (WA). Since WA Oil production commenced in 1967, onshore production wells have gathered oil from the field beneath Barrow Island. Processing occurred on-island before oil was transported via pipeline to offshore tankers for export. In 2025, the Barrow Island oil field ceased production when the last production well and production station were shut-in and isolated. As a mature asset , the Barrow Island oil field and related facilit ies and infrastructure have now entered the decommissioning phase. For more details, visit the Chevron Australia website. === WORK PACKAGES Contract - EOI Close Date Any Opportunities - 01 Feb 2027. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Processing plant - expansion project (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: P2000 Expansion Project Overview PLS owns and operates the world's largest, independent hard rock lithium operation, the Pilgangoora Operation, located 120 kilometres from Port Hedland. The P2000 Project, is the proposed expansion of Pilgangoora which aims to increase production of spodumene concentrate to approximately 2 million tonnes each year. This expansion will be delivered through the design and construction of a new processing plant. The proposed flowsheet includes crushing and ore sorting, a wholeofore comminution circuit, preflotation magnetic separation and a tantalum byproduct recovery circuit. The new facilit y will be constructed adjacent to the existing Pilgan Plant to leverage established services , processing facilit ies, infrastructure, and operational labour synergies. PLS will publish all major tenders associated with the P2000 Project on the ICN WA Gateway in accordance with the Project schedule. We are committed to ensuring that local suppliers are provided with full, fair, and reasonable opportunities to participate in the Project. Pilbara-based businesses are encouraged to add their contact details to our Pilbara Business Register to be notified when contracts are published. Pilgangoora Operations Pty Ltd (POPL) is a wholly owned subsidiary of PLS Group Limited (PLS). === Work Package - Close Date Belt Feeder - 25 May 2026 Special Piping Items - 15 May 2026 Chiller Plant - 15 May 2026 LV and HV Transportable Switchrooms - 15 May 2026 Cables - 15 May 2026 Lights and Poles - 15 May 2026 Plant Buildings - 15 May 2026 Misc electrical Panels - 15 May 2026 Cyclones - 27 Apr 2026 Pebble Crusher - 27 Apr 2026 Actuated and Manual Valves - 25 May 2026 Vibrating Pan Feeder - 27 Apr 2026 Screens - 04 May 2026 Conveyor Idlers - 25 May 2026 Conveyor Pulleys - 25 May 2026 Compressors, Blowers, Dryers and Receivers - 04 May 2026 Conveyor Skirts - 15 May 2026 Conveyer Belts - 15 May 2026 Slurry Pumps - 27 Apr 2026.
