opportunities.title: Infrastructure project (various opportunities)
opportunities.description: Ministers North Project Overview BHP is undertaking a study to understand requirements around the development of Ministers North (MN); a high-grade, Brockman ore deposit located, ~13km south-east of Yandi in the Central Pilbara. As part of the project study, the Company is seeking expressions of interest (EOI) from suitably qualified contractors to gain an understanding of the capacity and capability of available contractors for the proposed project. An Engineering, Procurement, Construction management (EPCM) contracting model has been selected for the MN project. The EPCM contractor will act as agent for Company, for the supply of some goods and services . The Project scope is comprised of all facilit ies and infrastructure (permanent and temporary) required to support the potential MN development. The scope to be delivered can be characterised across the following categories: 1. Greenfield - Mine, and associated Infrastructure A new mine at MN; Construction of a combined use Light Vehicle (LV) /Surface Mining Equipment (SME) haul road. Dual lane LV/SME tunnel arch structure. New level crossing over an existing rail line provided for construction access , prior to completion of the bridge. water supply pipelines from supply location to MN and from MN to Yandi; New fuel storage and refuelling including associated facilit ies at MN; Communications networks including digital radio, internet and cellular services ; and Temporary construction infrastructure (including offices, warehousing, lay-downs, water and workshop). 2. Brownfield - Modifications and tie-ins New primary and secondary crushing facilit ies and associated screening facilit ies at Yandi including partial demolition of existing eastern front-end facilit y. Modifications to existing Yandi infrastructure (including overland conveyor, Coarse Ore Stockpile, scalping screens, tertiary crushers, product screens and conveyors) to produce fines products. Relocation of existing power line to avoid the MN orebody. Conversion of existing Train Loadout facilit y (TLO2). Modifications to the existing reclaimer, yard conveyor and transfer station. Upgrades to existing Yandi explosives storage facilit ies; and Extension of raw, fire, and potable water services at primary and secondary crushing facilit ies. 3. Other Non-Process Infrastructure to support the new operation Administration offices, first aid, crib rooms and ablution facilit ies at MN, including associated potable water , sewerage, and oily water systems. maintenance bays and fuel facilit ies at MN; Re-use and expansion of the existing Yandi Mobile Equipment workshop facilit ies; and Tie-ins to existing power, raw water , and communications networks. --- WORK PACKAGES Contract - Full Scope Closes Transport and Logistics services - 06 May 2026 TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior ICT / cyber security officer
opportunities.description: LH-06263 - 1 Senior ICT/Cyber security Officer The Cyber Project Coordinator is accountable under limited direction to perform complex work to provide oversight over the Agency’s Cyber Solutions team engagements to ensure that work completed by the team is appropriately managed and engagements are successful. This will include coordinating cyber security projects in a multi-vendor environment, creating and managing integrated project schedules that align dependencies and deliverables, track progress and minimise project risk. The Project Coordinator has a sufficient level of technical knowledge to understand technical discussions and directions in order to communicate information to required stakeholders, some of which may be non-technical. Key duties and responsibilities 1. Leadership and Accountability Manage the delivery of cyber security work packages for Agency projects including security requirements definition, integrating security testing schedules, aligning dependencies and tracking progress. Acquire, maintain and apply an in-depth knowledge of the Agency’s project management framework, methodologies and activities required to coordinate and support the Agency’s project and reporting requirements. Provide the coordination and support to ensure an Agency’s project is compliant with the assurance and governance processes for policy, security , clinical safety, and design; monitoring progress, gathering information to produce monthly project status reports and other key project documentation. Accountable to provide a high level of support to other Agency project managers to develop and maintain project risk and issue registers; identifying and reporting variances and issues; providing assistance and coordination for risk mitigation, response and treatment actions. Manage contracts, service agreements and purchase orders; monitor expenditure and emerging issues that may impact on Agency priorities engaging with risk and undertaking risk management activities for area of responsibility, ensuring compliance and delivery of outcomes. 2. management Diversity and Span Coordinate and provide support to monitor, track and report on the status of project, program deliverables to ensure schedules, cost and quality metrics are in line with approved project plans. 3. Job Context and Environment Conducting reviews to ensure risks and issues related to projects, programs under their responsibility are identified, assessed and mitigated and are in alignment with applicable Agency frameworks. Manage the delivery of security testing services through multiple external vendors. Define, plan, and execute the plans to achieve and maintain security certification requirements for the Agency. Collaborate with and manage the deliverables across the Cyber Solutions team for cyber security architects, risk leads and assurance and compliance leads. Provide a range of administration, project, program, support services to facilit ate the delivery of outcomes within the Branch. Work with teams, partners and/or clients to track progress and compliance against the requirements contained in detailed program, project, governance, communication and reporting plans. 4. Independence and decision-making Work with direction from Project management Office, against established Agency project and operational priorities, framework and methodologies to deliver quality outcomes ensuring project tools, templates and processes are adhered to. Support in activities to understand business needs and assist the Project management Office in documenting and implementing best practice processes to assist in successful delivery of technology projects. Research, anticipate and analyse activities to identify business needs, particularly those that may be conflicting. Manage and negotiate resolutions to complex or sensitive issues or use judgement to escalate. Co-ordinate the preparation of reports and briefing papers for executives to support evidence-based decision making 5. Stakeholder management Manage relationships with internal and external stakeholders to achieve project(s), operations, work area and agency goals; anticipating and responding to their needs and expectations ensuring stakeholder expectations are clearly articulated, documented and understood by the project delivery team. Communicate and negotiate with stakeholders; working under limited direction to deal with complex and contentious matters requiring persuasion and sensitivity to minimize oppositions and maximise acceptance and cooperation. Essential criteria 1. Relevant experience managing multiple projects and security activities in a large Organisation. Forecasting branch wide resource requirements and executing government procurement processes. 2. Relevant experience in managing delivery of cyber security work packages/projects. 3. Relevant knowledge of cyber security project deliverables e.g. security requirements definition, security testing types. 4. Relevant experience in delivery of security testing services through multiple external vendors. 5. Relevant project management experience to deliver technical projects. 6. Proven record of managing projects that achieve/uphold security certifications such as ISO27001 and ISM TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: EOI-00247 - Provision of services and Materials for Antifoam Package – Chemical Injection Skid The scope relates to the following Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) supplied equipment manufactured by Milton Roy: • Antifoam Package (1 unit) • Chemical Injection Skids (2 units) • Portable Chemical Injection Package (1 unit) The services sought through this EOI are integral to ensuring the continued safe, reliable, and efficient operation of these systems. Detailed Scope of services Supervision and maintenance services Respondents are expected to demonstrate capability to provide: · Specialist supervision services for the identified equipment packages where service intervals are six (6) months or more; · maintenance service labour for routine and non‑routine maintenance activities where service intervals are six (6) months or more; · Completion of service activities in accordance with OEM recommendations and COMPANY requirements; and · Provision of service documentation upon completion of activities. Where service frequencies are less than six (6) months, such activities are typically performed by COMPANY maintenance personnel. Materials and Consumables Support Respondents shall demonstrate capability to: · Support the use of Company‑supplied operational spares issued via COMPANY work orders, including return of unused or replaced items; · Supply low‑value consumables where items are not Company‑supplied, to facilit ate service and maintenance activities; · Manage consumables in a control led and audit able manner. Bulk procurement of equipment or materials is out of scope for this EOI. Technical and OEM Support Respondents shall demonstrate capability to: · Provide technical support for engineering and operational queries related to Milton Roy equipment; · Escalate issues to the OEM where required; and · Support troubleshooting, diagnostics, and technical advice aligned with OEM standards. Training Support Respondents shall demonstrate capability to: · Deploy service personnel with appropriate OEM or equivalent certifications where practical; · Provide on‑board refresher or familiarisation support to COMPANY personnel to maintain operational knowledge of the equipment. Training provided does not replace COMPANY’s competency management framework. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Short course - mobilising capital through insurance & pension reform
opportunities.description: RFT AM 13547 - Australia Awards in Indonesia - Mobilising Long-Term Capital through Insurance and Pension Reform in Indonesia Short Course Tetra Tech International Development, the Managing Contractor for the Australia Awards in Indonesia (AAI) is seeking tenders for the design and delivery of Mobilising Long-Term Capital through Insurance and Pension Reform in Indonesia short course. Tenders are sought in accordance with the AAI Short Course Guidelines and as per the details more fully described in the Request for Tender (RFT) documentation. Details of the course are included in Part B – Scope of services . To tender to design and deliver this course the lead business must be a Registered Training Organisation (RTO) in Australia or an Australian tertiary institution as defined in the Higher Education Support Act, 2003 Table A, B and C. AAI will exclude a tenderer on the grounds of bankruptcy, insolvency, false declarations, or significant deficiencies in performance of any substantive requirement or obligation under a prior contract with Tetra Tech International Development or AAI. This short course consists of a 3-day pre-course workshop in Indonesia, a 2-week course in Australia, 3-4 online mentoring sessions, and a 3-day post-course workshop in Indonesia. The final delivery dates and locations will be determined in consultation with DFAT and the course provider. The proposed schedule (dates may change based on course provider’s availability): • Pre-course workshop: August 2026 • Short course in Australia: September 2026 • Post-course workshop: January or February 2027 A 2-hour online briefing will be conducted jointly by the preferred tenderer and AAI approximately one month before departure. Date will be negotiated with the preferred tenderer. The course provider will offer 3-4 online Award Project mentoring sessions (2-3 hours per session). Two or three of these sessions take place between the in-Australia course and the post-course workshop to ensure the participants are supported to finalise their Award Projects by the post-course workshop. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Project manager
opportunities.description: LH-06281 - 1 Manager Project Manager The Department of Finance (Finance) is seeking to undertake an Approach to Market (ATM) process to procure Project management services . The Enterprise Systems and services Division (ESSD) delivers shared enterprise resource planning (ERP) services to 16 Australian Government entities. Within ESSD, the Digital services Branch is responsible for managing the SAP ERP platform and the delivery of digital initiatives to improve services , including a major modernisation activity converting an existing SAP ECC6 landscap e to SAP S/4HANA. The role is critical to the effective operation of the ESSD and is responsible for the end‑to‑end delivery of enterprise ICT infrastructure projects, including (but not limited to) the implementation of a new gateway and the migration of various services to cloud platforms. The role ensures infrastructure outcomes are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned to agreed milestones, while providing clear direction, actively managing risks and dependencies, and maintaining strong relationships with business and technical stakeholders. The Project Manager will contribute to governance activities and respond to stakeholder and project‑related enquiries. Reporting to the Director, Major Projects and the Assistant Secretary, Digital services Branch, the role ensures consistent delivery against approved schedules and project documentation. Working across multidisciplinary teams within the ESSD and broader Finance Department, the Project Manager will promote collaboration and accountability. As a senior role, strong written and verbal communication skills are essential to effectively engage the ESSD’s various stakeholders and support successful project outcomes. Key duties and responsibilities Key duties and responsibilities will include, but are not limited to: • Engaging with clients and internal ESSD technical and business teams to scope ICT infrastructure initiatives and develop clear, fit‑for‑purpose project plans and Statements of Work. • Planning and delivering ICT infrastructure projects (including upgrades, enhancements, and remediation activities) aligned to ESSD priorities and roadmaps. • Developing and maintaining detailed end‑to‑end project plans, from initiation through to implementation and go‑live, identifying critical paths and key dependencies. • Actively monitoring, managing, and reporting on project progress, risks, issues, and outcomes. • Identifying required technical and project resources, addressing capability gaps and coordinating specialist support as needed. • Proactively identifying, managing, and escalating infrastructure risks and issues, ensuring appropriate governance and stakeholder engagement. • Collaborating with other project managers and technical leads to manage cross‑project and cross‑platform dependencies. • Contributing to the continuous improvement of ESSD project governance, delivery frameworks, and infrastructure capability. • Resolving technical and delivery issues through informed analysis, option evaluation, and practical decision‑making. Highly desirable criteria include, but are not limited to: • Demonstrated knowledge of project management methodologies and experience managing and delivering ICT infrastructure and digital technology projects within the Federal Government. • Demonstrated experience in the delivery of new gateways and migration of services to the cloud. • Demonstrated capability in developing and managing integrated project schedules, including technical dependencies and critical paths. • Proven experience engaging and collaborating with a broad range of government, industry, and technical stakeholders to build trust and deliver infrastructure outcomes. • Ability to establish, strengthen, and embed project management practices and governance frameworks to support high‑quality delivery. • Demonstrated ability to produce project, technical, and governance documentation accurately and within tight timeframes. • Highly self‑motivated, with the ability to work effectively under pressure in a fast‑paced, operational ICT environment. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: LH-06279 - 1 Senior Dev-Ops specialist The Senior DevOps Technical / Systems Analyst will play a role in assisting the Digital Transformation and Delivery Division’s design, development and continuous improvement of an automated and secure DevOps platform and capability to improve the efficiency of Aged Care Design, Development, Build, Test and Release across the Digital Transformation and Delivery Division. The role requires extensive background and knowledge of devops platforms and tooling with the ability to identify, design and develop initiatives to improve the end-to-end automation of devops capabilities across multiple technologies on both cloud and traditional infrastructure platforms through the software development lifecycle. Key duties and responsibilities Deliverables The Deliverables for the Senior DevOps Technical / Systems Analyst include, but are not limited to the following: • Working with the DTDD DevOps Team within the Production Support Section, to supporting continual improvement of the efficiency, reliability and security of the end-to-end software development lifecycle across the division; • Contribute and provide insight into the Division’s DevOps platform design, stability and maintenance ; • Provide implementation advice and contribute to documentation to support strategic initiatives to improve speed, quality, security and efficiency of software development and release. • Administer the Division’s DevOps platform (Azure DevOps). • Build and support divisional reports, and assist power users in reporting against Azure Devops and related tools • Assist teams in establishing and refining their work processes in Azure Devops and other SDLC tools • Monitor, triage and action end user support requests. • Contribute to and participate in ensuring Devops practices are efficient, scalable and maintainable across the division. Skills and experience • Demonstrated experience in design, configuration and administration of devops platforms; • Demonstrated experience in developing and building DevOps capability • Demonstrated experience in stakeholder management • Demonstrated experience and understanding of Whole of Government IT frameworks. • Demonstrated experience in working within enterprise IT environment across all stages of the software delivery lifecycle. • Demonstrated experience in IT Delivery teams within a mid to large scale project/program environment. • Background and experience in both development and operational teams. Desirable experience • Knowledge and experience of broad range of devsecops tools, including but not limited to Azure DevOps, PowerBI, Artifactory, Thycotic Secret Server, Sonarqube. • Knowledge and experience of working within Federal Government IT security control s (ISM, Essential 8). opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior business analysts
opportunities.description: LH-06272 - 3 Senior Business Analysts The Cloud Applications and Business Engagement & Design Sections within DFAT's Information management and Technology Division have a requirement for up to (3x) experienced ICT Business Analysts to support the delivery and sustainment of DFAT ICT initiatives. The role requires resources with strong analytical capability, experience working within agile delivery environments, and the ability to translate business needs into clear, actionable requirements to support modern cloud-based application development and new and existing projects. Key duties and responsibilities Job Specific role description: The successful candidates: • will be experienced Business Analysts with the ability to work across multiple high priority work items concurrently; • must demonstrate a strong record of working with business and technical stakeholders to elicit, analyse and document requirements within agile delivery teams. • Experience contributing to delivery planning, backlog refinement and supporting implementation activities is required; and • will be analytical problem solvers who thrive in environments of rapid change and continuous improvement and who are focused on delivering tangible business outcomes. The Skills Framework for the Information Age (SFIA) has been used to inform the requirements. opportunities.title: High speed rail - Trains, systems & systems integration package
opportunities.description: 10031971. TSSI - Trains, Systems and Systems Integration. The Authority is the Australian Government’s principal adviser on the development of High Speed Rail (HSR). Its purpose is to develop a nationally integrated HSR network, connecting cities and key regional centres, enhancing Australia’s long-term rail investment through policy development, national co-ordination, and strategic planning. This is a once-in-a-generation project that is expected to require a blend of Australian expertise in major infrastructure delivery and international experience from those who successfully develop and operate high speed rail networks. Successful proponents for this Project will likely combine Australia’s proven capability and skills with specialist expertise from those who deliver HSR systems internationally. The Trains, Systems and Systems Integration (TSSI) package includes the design, supply (of nominated key components identified during the ECI Process), installation oversight, final test and commissioning and systems integration of the rail systems works. The TSSI Major Delivery Package is also responsible for the maintenance of the trains, rail systems and infrastructure. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: NOTE: This information is subject to change and should be verified through the AusTender Website. Manager Procurement tenders@hsra.gov.au

opportunities.title: High speed rail - Area Packages 1 & 3
opportunities.description: 10031970. Area Packages 1 and 3 (AP1/3). The Australian Government is planning a future High Speed Rail (HSR) network to connect Brisbane, Sydney, Canberra, Melbourne and regional communities across the east coast of Australia. The Authority is the Australian Government’s principal adviser on the development of HSR. Its purpose is to develop a nationally integrated HSR network, connecting cities and key regional centres, enhancing Australia’s long-term rail investment through policy development, national co-ordination, and strategic planning. This is a once-in-a-generation project that is expected to require a blend of Australian expertise in major infrastructure delivery and international experience from those who successfully develop and operate high speed rail networks. Successful proponents for this Project will likely combine Australia’s proven capability and skills with specialist expertise from those who deliver HSR systems internationally. Area Package 1 (AP1) covers the design and construction of all works in the geographical area including tunnelling, track infrastructure, and tunnel fit out works for the Ourimbah to Berowra section of the Project. Area Package 1 also includes the design and construction of the Central Coast Station, including the surrounding precinct and interchange. Area Package 3 (AP3) includes the design and construction of all works in the geographical area including tunnelling, track infrastructure, and tunnel fit out works for the Berowra to Sydney Central section of the Project. opportunities.title: Notice: CCTV and lighting improvements
opportunities.description: Increased CCTV, lighting for Byron Bay Increased CCTV, and lighting improvements are coming to Byron Bay’s town centre, along with broader initiatives like mobile CCTV, says Byron Shire Council. The project has been made possible thanks to a $1 million grant from the federal government’s Strengthening Community Safety - Election Commitment Funding grant opportunity.’ Lawson Street north and south carparks, Apex Park, the railway corridor, and the connecting laneways will be the priority locations for improved lighting and CCTV. The community has been very clear that they want to see improved safety and security across the town centre, with many feeling that poor lighting is a key contributor to people feeling vulnerable and unsafe,’ Byron Shire Mayor, Sarah Ndiaye, said. During feedback to Council, concerns were raised about antisocial behaviour, poor access ibility, and the limited availability of public amenities in the CBD. Completed by 2027 Over the coming months Council will finalise the technical requirements for the lighting and CCTV and then consult with key stakeholders and community to finalise the plans. The works will go for tender later this year and the project is due to be completed by April 2027. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Project controls services
opportunities.description: SR01014 - Project control s services for Hunter Transmission Project EnergyCo is seeking to procure one qualified Service Provider with an experienced Project control s team to oversee the planning, cost and risk of the HTP through the delivery phase. Project goals EnergyCo invites tenders from qualified service providers to deliver a specialist Project control s team for the Hunter Transmission Project. The engagement will provide five key roles responsible for schedule, cost and risk oversight during the project’s delivery phase, ensuring strong governance, integrated project support and effective monitoring of the Network Operator. The successful provider will demonstrate proven experience delivering Project control s services on major linear infrastructure projects over $500 million, the capability to supply a cohesive and skilled team, and an understanding of the complex regulatory and delivery environment. This 41‑month engagement will be delivered under an Upper Limiting Fee model and requires exclusivity from tenderers to protect probity and ensure confidence in EnergyCo’s oversight role. Scope The Project control s services scope includes: • Provision of a five‑role Project control s team responsible for schedule, cost and risk oversight for the Hunter Transmission Project. • Implementation and operation of project control s systems, tools and processes aligned with EnergyCo governance, frameworks and reporting requirements. • Schedule management , including development, analysis, progress tracking and performance reporting. • Cost and financial management , including forecasting, budget monitoring and variance analysis. • Risk management , including identification, assessment, mitigation planning and ongoing reporting. • Integration within the broader project team, working collaboratively with internal and external stakeholders. • Provision of qualified personnel meeting mandatory experience requirements, ensuring resource continuity and structured knowledge handover. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Notice: Event - Building the Future of Small Business
opportunities.description: Building the Future of Small Business on the Capricorn Coast Livingstone Shire Council is inviting local business owners, operators, and entrepreneurs to come together for an evening of connection, collaboration, and conversation at Leading Local – Building the Future of Small Business. Held as part of Queensland Small Business Month 2026, the free event will take place on Thursday, 14 May at the Keppel Bay Sailing Club, offering a relaxed and welcoming environment for businesses of all sizes and sectors. The event is designed to strengthen local networks and foster meaningful discussion around how collaboration can support a thriving and sustainable business community across the Capricorn Coast and broader Livingstone Shire. Mayor Adam Belot said the event highlights the important role local businesses play in shaping the region’s future. “More and more people are choosing Livingstone Shire as a place to call home and to do business, and Council is proactively responding to the challenges and opportunities that come with that growth,” Mayor Belot said. “Our role is to plan for our communities in a way that ensures we can accommodate this growth while maintaining the lifestyle that makes our region so special. “Events like this are important because they bring our business community together to be part of that journey—sharing ideas, building connections and helping shape a strong and sustainable future for our region.” Mayor Belot said collaboration between businesses is key to unlocking new opportunities and strengthening the local economy. “I encourage all businesses across all sectors to come along, share their experiences and ideas, and be part of the conversation about our region’s strong potential,” Mayor Belot said. The evening will begin with a short welcome from the Mayor, followed by an informal panel discussion featuring respected local business and community leaders. Guest speakers will include: Natalie Gesler – Principal Sales Agent of @Real Estate and leads the T@ble Capricorn Coast Business Community Tash and Ben Toohey – Directors of Lyke Homes Michael Polinghorne – Capricornia Chamber of Commerce representative and Small Business Specialist Esdale Sinclair & Associates The focus will be on open conversation, practical insights, and shared experiences, before moving into a networking session. This Queensland Small Business Month event is proudly supported by the Queensland Government. Light refreshments will be provided. Event Details: Leading Local – Building the Future of Small Business WHAT: Informal panel discussion and networking WHEN: Thursday, 14 May 2026 TIME: 5:30pm – 7:30pm WHERE: Keppel Bay Sailing Club (Telltales Bar) COST: Free event. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: BCC00569 - conserv ation Programs Biodiversity Contract 2026-29 (COB058623) This contract is split into two categories: 1. Bushcare and Native Vegetation Regeneration. Typical tasks include but are not limited to: Other minor reserve management works as required, monitoring biodiversity and revegetation etc, seed collection, debris and litter management , brush cutting for fuel reduction and biodiversity enhancement, revegetation and follow-up maintenance where required, protection of regenerating native vegetation, management of woody weed infestations and regrowth, selective herbicide application (spot spraying, cut and swab, drill and fill) and hand weeding of environmental and declared weeds. 2. General Reserve management - Weed control and Other Works Typical tasks include but are not limited to: Hand weeding and safe disposal of declared and environmental weeds, herbicide application using spot spraying, knapsack spraying and longline spraying techniques, control of woody weed regrowth using methods such as drill and fill, cut and swab and spot spraying, brush cutting for fuel control and biodiversity enhancement, vegetation and debris management , management of woody weed infestations and small trees, cutting, stacking and/or removal of fallen vegetation and debris, litter removal, mulch spreading, maintenance of drainage lines and sediment basins, minor erosion control works, maintenance of walking trails, pile burning, removal or repair of post and wire fencing, installation of temporary signage and barriers and other minor reserve management works as required. This contract is for 3 years with optional extensions of one (1) plus one (1) years. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: CCTV renewal project
opportunities.description: 2526-29 - Safe City CCTV Renewal Project || Phase 2 The City of Greater Dandenong invites tenders from suitably qualified and experienced contractors for the supply, installation, replacement, integration, testing, commissioning and handover of CCTV cameras and supporting infrastructure across multiple Council sites as part of the Safe City CCTV Renewal Project – Phase 2. The works include replacement of existing CCTV asset s, installation of new cameras where identified, rectification of power and data infrastructure, and integration into Council’s existing CCTV and network environment. The services will be delivered across multiple locations within the City of Greater Dandenong. Contractor attendance on site is required; services cannot be delivered remotely. The anticipated commencement of services is following contract award. Full details of the scope, requirements and contractual condition s are set out in the Tender Documents, including Section D – Specifications. Council operates an existing CCTV network and supporting ICT infrastructure. Tenderers must ensure that all proposed equipment, installation methods and integration approaches are compatible with Council’s current environment, as detailed in the Specifications. Site-specific information is provided in supporting schedules to assist tenderers in understanding local condition s, layouts and constraints. These schedules provide contextual information only and do not override the governing specifications in the Tender Documents. Tenderers are responsible for making their own enquiries, validating site condition s, and allowing for all requirements necessary to deliver the services in accordance with the Tender Documents. Council is seeking a delivery partner that can: • deliver a fully functional, reliable, and secure CCTV system • integrate seamlessly with Council’s existing infrastructure • undertake works in live operational environments with minimal disruption • meet agreed delivery timeframes and commissioning requirements • demonstrate strong safety, quality, and risk management practices Desired outcomes include improved system reliability, enhanced public safety outcomes, clear documentation and handover, and asset s that are fit-for-purpose over their expected lifecycle. Tenderers are also encouraged to demonstrate how their proposal supports Council’s social procurement, local content, and environmental objectives, as outlined in the Tender Documents. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Customer Service P: 8571 1000 E: council@cgd.vic.gov.au

opportunities.title: Canteen service
opportunities.description: Canteen Service at Montmorency Secondary College Request for Tenders / Expression of Interest Submissions are invited in accordance with the tender/EOI documents for the: Canteen services at Montmorency Secondary College Montmorency Secondary College is in the Northern Region of the Department of Education and Training which services a large residential area and surrounding community of inner northern metropolitan Melbourne approximately 18 km from the Melbourne CBD. The school has a current enrolment of 1,100 students in year 7 through to year 12. It is expected that enrolment will remain stable in the ensuing years. Physical features of the school include 56 classrooms. There is a 5-court Basketball complex, gymnasium, audit orium and outside basketball court etc The student population, assumed to be represented by a wide range of socio-economic backgrounds, is likely to be characterised by a reasonable percentage of families with both parents working and high socio-economic status. The canteen service is required to cater for a significant proportion of approximately 1,100 students and 100 staff members during all Victorian school term periods with the exception of public holidays and pupil free days. Site visit: The Principal of Montmorency Secondary College offers prospective providers an opportunity to view the school canteen facilit ies up until 8th of May. Service providers are required to advise the Contact Person of their intention to attend by close of business three days prior to the requested visit. No more than two (2) attendees from the same service provider may attend the same site visit. All attendees must sign the attendance record which will be provided at each site visit. A current canteen service exists at the school which has been the arrangement for several decades. facilit ies available for use by the successful service provider to deliver the services required include a commercial grade kitchen including one commercial refrigerator, one cold bain marie, two hot bain marie, one Woodson griddle, two chest freezers, two domestic fridges, one microwave, one oven, one domestic dishwasher and three portable food preparation trolleys. There is one storeroom. The School wishes to advise that currently the following practices occur that may impact on the numbers attending the service: • ‘The Healthy Eating Advisory Service’, that supports schools to provide and promote nutritious foods and drinks in line with department policy. • Outstanding canteen staff who know the students. • Menu that is both affordable and healthy. • Extensive range of foods which are suitable for our demographic. School Council statement: The School Council wants the service provider to provide an affordable and healthy canteen service to the school community. Both hot and cold meal options should be available, along with a variety of smaller, snack-sized items. All meals and snacks should be freshly prepared using high-quality, fresh ingredients. The overall quality and style of the food should be comparable to contemporary café-style offerings. The School Council believes that all employees that work in the canteen service should be facilit ators of positive development who provide a safe, engaging environment and nurture healthy interactions. The School will license the following facilit ies: • Toilets (M/F) for both staff and students. • Kitchen (Canteen) • Storeroom • Servery • Student waiting area. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: For information contact: Mrs Lisa Ball, Business Manager Montmorency Secondary College P: (03) 9422 1604 E: lisa.ball@education.vic.gov.au

opportunities.title: Lease of venue (hospitality operators)
opportunities.description: Expressions of Interest Open - Lease Opportunity Looking for an exciting new opportunity in a great location? The Shire of Boddington invites Expressions of Interest from experienced hospitality operators to activate a key venue in the heart of Boddington’s Community Precinct. This site is ideally located to serve library visitors, tourists, users of Hotham Park, and the wider community. With the recent completion of the Hotham Park redevelopment, the addition of six vibrant murals throughout the town, and a captivating sculpture trail, Boddington’s appeal as a tourist destination is stronger than ever. Situated within the town’s main tourism precinct, this is a prime opportunity to establish a business that enhances the vibrancy and profile of Boddington as an exciting regional destination. Previously operated as a café, the Shire is open to new ideas on how the space can best be utilised to service the community and growing tourism industry. The site is located within the Boddington Library and Visitor Centre building in the Hotham Park Precinct. Additional information, including the original architect's drawings and site photos, can be provided upon request. EOI Requirements Expressions of interest should address the following items: • Detail of use. • The extent to which the use will be complementary to the Hotham Park redevelopment and contribute to the overall vibrancy and profile of Boddington as an exciting regional destination. • Detail of hours and days of operation (minimum 9am - 4pm, 5 days per week, including weekends and public holidays, with seasonal flexibility). • Commitment to open additional hours when events and activities are held within the precinct and the Boddington area. • Detail of estimated staff (or volunteer) numbers that would perform activities as a part of the proposal. • Details of contingency resources to ensure the minimum opening hours can be achieved. • Experience in the proposed activity. • Preferred timeframe for commencing operations. • Proposed length of lease (expected 3 - 5 years). • Detail of any fit-out requirements that will be requested by the lessee to be undertaken by the Shire and estimated cost. • Financial consideration per annum, noting Council are willing to consider a reduced rent for year 1, dependent on the level of fit-out required. Visit the link provided for more information on the opportunity and requirements. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net
opportunities.contact_text: Site visits can be arranged in advance. Expressions of Interest can be submitted via email to shire@boddington.wa.gov.au All applications and enquiries will be treated as confidential.

opportunities.description: LH-06248 - Minimum of 1 EL1 SAP FI and Reporting Support / Senior Infrastructure Engineer Job details Infrastructure Engineers implement, manage and retire the infrastructure (including physical, virtual, networking and cloud infrastructure) that supports digital services . FI and Reporting Support This role is responsible for the maintenance and support of the Department of Social services (DSS) ERP system including the HR Payroll Administration function. The Role is responsible for the management and operation of the FI and Reporting Support team. The Assistant Director, FI and Reporting Support provides supervisory management and leadership for team members, including workforce management , performance management , and workload management . This role also provides technical leadership and guidance to the FI and Reporting Support team and is a specialist in the operation of SAP FI. Key duties and responsibilities Duties may include some or all of the following: 1. Primary duties involve the management of staff in the FI and Reporting Support team including: • Supervision of staff including workforce planning, compliance and performance management • Monitor and manage staff workloads in conjunction with the SAP FI Technical Lead • Championing and supporting change management activities within the team • Capability to undertake SAP FI configuration changes 2. Coordinate team responses to work package service requests, in conjunction with the SAP FI Technical Lead 3. Provide technical leadership, support and advice for the SAP FI and SAP BI modules across the DSS SAP Solutions section 4. Provide technical guidance to the FI and Reporting Support team in completion of support tasks and projects, and 5. opportunities.title: Invite only: Scrum master
opportunities.description: LH-06265 - Four (including Merit List) (SFIA4) Scrum Master Job details The Scrum Masters works across multiple teams and business areas using the Agile principles, processes and practices. As a leader, they balance delivery requirements with complex improvement initiatives fostering a culture of continuous improvement and Agile excellence. They facilit ate and deliver several complex Agile ceremonies across multiple teams, including program increments and sprint planning, daily stand-ups, scrum of scrums, retrospectives and showcases to internal and external stakeholders, including cross-agency attendees. The Scrum Master implements Agile operating models and strategies for newly formed projects and existing delivery areas while considering the current and historical context and delivery timeframes and targets. They develop and foster strong working relationships with internal, external and cross-agency partners and stakeholders. Key duties and responsibilities The Scrum Master • Uses the agile methodologies, techniques, processes, DevOps and other tools. • Alignment of the Agile Team and Product Owner to the Scrum process and Health Scaled Agile method. • Design and implement agile operating working models and strategies based on localised context and requirement. • Lead new or existing projects, meeting deadlines and requirements. • Identify and analyse risks, issues, opportunities, • Create change requests and monitor outcomes to foster a culture of continuous improvement. • Build and sustain relationships with all internal and external stakeholders to achieve delivery outcomes. • facilit ate and lead complex agile ceremonies, provide recommendations and manage outcomes. • Resolve complex issues using professional knowledge and experience and identify and analyse alternative courses of actions and their implications. • Negotiate deliverables, support levels, and workflows to ensure effective delivery of business outcomes. • Assist the team to prepare and contribute to the Agile Release Train (ART) System Demo (showcase). • Help foster code quality practices through ensuring peer reviews and IT Standards are followed. • Manage dependencies with other teams. • Ensure the Team’s Story and Scrum boards are transparent and kept up to date. • Ensure all key events take place and are effective and collaborative. This includes: o Iteration Planning. o Backlog Refinement. o Iteration Review. o Daily Stand-up; and o Iteration Retrospective. o Track Iteration progress (day-to-day work), removing impediments, to enable commitments to be fulfilled. • Contribute to Scrum Master Chapters and Communities. • Partner with Product owners, technical leads and architects to shape and prioritise backlogs for Microsoft D365 and related platform work • Delivery reporting in Altus providing status reports and burn up/burn down on budget. The seller must ensure that the Labour Hire worker performing the services during the term of this contract, facilit ate an effective transfer of skills and knowledge to the buyer to enable full use of services and deliverables and associated documents. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.description: LH-06286 - 1 Senior FMW Developer Job details The FMW developer roles provides level 3 technical support services within the Aged Care Production Support section. They undertake FMW product development, monitoring, and test activities in response to incident and defects raised for the Aged Care Gateway platform. FMW Developer (ACAS) specialists are required to work across CRM and integration related tasks accordingly. Key duties and responsibilities Responsibilities include: • Undertake FMW product development, test activities in response to incident and defects raised for the Aged Care Platform. • Define service contracts including message definitions, business rules and non-functional requirements. • Work with Business Analysts to understand business requirements and translate them to service requirements. • Define service composition, processing logic, business and mapping rules, error handling policies and error messages. • Produce suitable SOA service functional and technical designs for Oracle SOA 12c services that comply with the development and design standards, best practices and SOA architectural principles including data architecture design activities. • Develop logical and physical data design, optionality, multiplicity, entities and entity relationships and data validation. • Map logical entities to the enterprise Canonical Data Model. • Work with Information Architect to ensure that Informational integrity is maintained across the service portfolio • Apply with demonstrable proficiency, modelling tools such as Rational Software Architect, Sparx Enterprise Architect, Altova XMLSpy, OxygenXML or any other modelling tools. • Apply working knowledge of WS-* standards, WSDL, SOAP, XML, XML Schema, Schematron. • Contribute to critical major incident and defect triage and resolution. • Periodically review logs and call out high frequency or critical errors for in Production. • Escalate critical or blocked defects to the defect management team to ensure continued throughput of incidents and defect fixes. • Use defect/incident management toolsets (e.g. Agile Lifecycle Manager, ServiceNow) to track and record FMW Aged Care fix progress. opportunities.title: Invite only: Lead data analysts
opportunities.description: LH-06275 - 2 Lead Data Analysts The Department Employment and Workplace Relations collects apprenticeships data through the Apprenticeships Data management System (ADMS). The department uses data from ADMS and the National Centre for Vocational Education Research (NCVER) to deliver on their apprenticeship initiatives. The apprenticeships data has a warehouse that uses a modern database design, integration with other data to assist in assessing apprentices’ and employer eligibility to attract incentives payments, payment to providers and the determination of whether an apprentice remains in their apprenticeship. Using this data, analysts can provide information on program performance and actual market condition s which can be used to supplement the NCVER quarterly data when received and provide interim program data between NCVER releases. Key duties and responsibilities The key deliverables of the role include: • Transforming data from an SQL data warehouse into insights using a range of tools, such as PowerBI. • Collaborating with the team to create and maintain data models to support the work of the team. • Working with policy owners to produce statistical and analytic data to inform policy effectively, trend studies and policy development. • Working with program and contract managers to produce statistical and analytic data to inform program and contract management . • Working with service delivery areas to produce reports to support and manage service delivery. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Senior systems analyst
opportunities.description: LH-06255 - minimum of 1 Senior Systems Analyst Systems Analysts translate business requirements into technical requirements that align with relevant architectures. Systems Analysts (Senior) analyse datasets with a variety of tools to research and solve organisational issues and identify trends and insights to support organisational outcomes. Systems Analysts (Senior) are responsible for translating business needs into requirements and specifications for development of management information reporting solutions. It includes designing, analysing and documenting requirements to meet business expectations. They are responsible for writing functional specifications from the business requirements. Systems Analysts (Senior) understand customer data requirements and can analyse matching scenarios within the data management tools. The role assists with the management of the organisation’s data asset s in order to improve and maintain data quality. Systems Analysts (Senior) work under the limited direction of senior staff. They exercise both initiative and judgement in the interpretation of policy and in the application of practices and procedures. Decision making is dependent on judgement, skills and knowledge. Systems Analysts (Senior) manage and organise their own work in the context of competing priorities, including contributing to business planning, changes in workplace practices and business improvement strategies Key duties and responsibilities Duties may include some or all the following: • perform detailed analysis and evaluation of business processes and requirements • create solution designs and implement solutions to meet business needs • create and maintain solution documents • support daily operations and monitor performance of business solutions • adhere to delivery and support methodologies and contribute ideas for improvement • perform detailed technical application assessments considering feasibility, resource effort, risks and benefits • conduct testing activities such as test planning, execution coverage reviews and defect management • collaborate with stakeholders to define tasks, resolve issues, solve problems and ensure effective and timely implementation of projects • undertake project quality management tasks such as peer and quality reviews of specifications, design and testing documents • review research, process models and documentation of other staff, providing guidance and feedback • resolve or escalate issues as required and suggest alternative approaches and solutions • perform cost analysis activities as required • manage the life cycle of changes and improvements to systems • collaborate with stakeholders to resolve issues and ensure effective and timely implementation of projects • contribute to the development and implementation of improvements and best practice in processes and procedures • contribute to relevant business and people planning, and develop local strategies to improve against key performance measures • prepare correspondence such as reports, corporate and technical documentation for review by executive level staff • resolve or escalate issues as required and suggest alternative approaches • provide comprehensive technical support to senior management TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Invite only: Lead change & communication specialist
opportunities.description: LH-06246 - 1 Lead Change and Communication Specialist The Change and Communication Specialist is responsible for planning and delivering integrated change management and communication activities to support the successful implementation of programs, projects, and business improvements. The role focuses on the people side of change - ensuring stakeholders understand, are prepared for, and adopt new ways of working through structured engagement, communication, and change interventions. Key duties and responsibilities Change management • Deliver end‑to‑end change management support aligned to organisational change frameworks, including impact assessment, change readiness, and adoption planning. • Conduct change impact and stakeholder impact assessments to identify people, process, and capability impacts and inform mitigation strategies. • Develop and maintain change management artefacts such as change strategies, impact assessments, readiness assessments, and transition plans. • Work closely with project and business leads to ensure change activities are integrated into delivery schedules and governance arrangements. • Monitor change risks and issues, escalating as required, and report on change progress and outcomes to leadership and governance forums. Communication and engagement • Develop and deliver targeted communication and engagement plans to support project and program objectives. • Draft clear, access ible, and audience‑appropriate communication products, including intranet content, newsletters, talking points, FAQs, toolkits, and executive briefings. • Coordinate communication activities across agreed internal (and where required external) channels to ensure consistent and timely messaging. • Ensure communication activities clearly articulate the what, why, when, and how of change, including actions required of impacted stakeholders. • Support senior leaders and managers with change messaging and engagement with their teams. Stakeholder management • Identify and analyse key stakeholder groups and tailor change and communication approaches accordingly. • Build strong working relationships with business areas, project teams, internal communications, learning and development, and other enabling functions. • facilit ate workshops and engagement sessions to support change discovery, impact validation, and business readiness where required. Reporting and continuous improvement • Track and report on change and communication outcomes, including engagement, awareness, and readiness indicators. • Capture lessons learned and contribute to continuous improvement of change and communication practices, tools, and templates. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Environmental & social safeguards services
opportunities.description: Multiple destinations -- HQ26NF060_EOI for Environmental and Social Safeguards services The WFP ESS function requires specialized technical services to support the effective implementation of WFP’s ESS commitments across programmes, projects, and operational activities. These services are essential for ensuring that WFP interventions are designed and delivered in a manner that prevents, mitigates, and manages potential environmental and social risks, while enhancing positive impacts for affected communities. To meet these obligations, WFP seeks to establish LTAs for the provision of ESS services (including, but not limited to, risk screening, environmental and social impact assessments, stakeholder engagement support, monitoring, capacity strengthening, and development of safeguard tools and guidance). These services will directly support compliance with WFP’s Environmental and Social Sustainability Framework (ESSF) and help GHQ, ROs, and COs integrate safeguards considerations throughout the programme cycle. The LTAs will therefore ensure timely access to high‑quality, specialized expertise needed to support programme design, implementation, and oversight; uphold accountability to affected populations; and align WFP operations with international environmental and social standards, host‑country regulations, and donor requirements. TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

opportunities.title: Primary health care
opportunities.description: Papua New Guinea --- DFAT 1091 (DFAT-1091) - Primary Health Care for Papua New Guinea (PHC4PNG) This investment supports Papua New Guinea (PNG) to deliver on its National Health Plan 2021-2030 by strengthening the systems, governance, and services required for access ible, affordable and high quality primary health care, particularly in rural and remote areas where most of the population lives. It focuses on improving provincial and district leadership, financing coherence, and service delivery to achieve measurable improvements in health outcomes by 2035. The program supports the delivery of integrated packages of primary health care addressing high burden health priorities, including communicable diseases such as HIV; reproductive, maternal, newborn and child health (including family planning, immunisation and cervical cancer); and selected non communicable and nutrition related condition s. It also provides enabling support for district planning, supervision, outreach, facilit y connectivity and community governance, alongside targeted catalytic grants—such as a Health Innovation Fund—to strengthen Provincial Health Authorities through innovation, local problem solving and system improvements. Overall, the investment contributes to PNG’s vision of preventing ill health, responding to emerging risks, and ensuring inclusive, integrated primary health care for all. opportunities.title: PSC002845: RFI for a Self-Service Contract Management System (CMS)
opportunities.description: ANSTO is a corporate Commonwealth entity and Australiaâs nuclear research and technology organisation. ANSTO is responsible for delivering specialised advice, scientific services and products to government, industry, academia and other research organisations. It is our mission to deliver knowledge, value and trust through the application of nuclear science, technology and engineering. ANSTO has a fully implemented contract management system that has been in place for circa 5 years.  ANSTO seeks to review what is available in the market to determine its planning for a software-as-a-service solution (ie not a bespoke build) contract management system in future years and to understand the technology options available. ANSTO seeks information regarding suitably qualified and experienced Suppliers of, and their plan to deliver, a contract management system that meets the requirements ANSTO has currently identified below (the System ).   The details sought for the System are comprised of: a)    Details on functionality and capacity, and options for implementation (including data migration capability) and information on data hosting, security and management, including cyber security, by reference to the requirements set out below; b)    Indicative pricing for an off the shelf product; and c)     Additional information about the Supplier. These can be provided by completing Part B and the table in this Part C. This is not an approach to market at this stage.  This is a request for information to gather information about what is available, and a mechanism for ANSTO to understand suitable contract management systems available for a corporate Commonwealth entity in Australia. If you choose not to respond to this request, you may still respond to any future open approach to market by ANSTO for a contract management system.  Please be aware that ANSTO may use information provided in this RFI including to inform future planning including development of requirements in a future approach to market (which may change from this RFI) and budgeting, and as otherwise outlined in Part D of this RFI.

opportunities.title: ATM_2026_6621: Sediment Monitoring Program - Maugean Skate Program
opportunities.description: The Department is seeking a provider to design and implement a field-validated Methodology for: quantifying the oxygen demand from sediment in Macquarie Harbour, and assess the drivers and context of oxygen demand; updating the bathymetry of key areas of Macquarie Harbour; determining, and possibly implementing, an appropriate Method for investigating Maugean skate prey species; and consolidating relevant environmental datasets The work will include the collection of sediment cores and associated samples across multiple sites in a manner that is statistically representative of the harbour, with repeat sampling of a subset of sites to assess seasonal influences. The work will require the design of a Methodology, consisting of a number of Methods, and its implementation through the collection and laboratory analysis of organic and inorganic sediment parameters, including assessment of sediment composition and microbial activity. The objective is to generate a comprehensive and integrated understanding of the sediments and benthic processes of Macquarie Harbour - in particular a scientifically defensible estimate of benthic oxygen demand across Macquarie Harbour, including the identification of areas with elevated oxygen demand arising from a number of possible sources; to enhance understanding of Macquarie Harbourâs physical environment through high-resolution bathymetry - in particular the dynamic environment of mouth of the Harbour -, if possible, to assess the historic and current availability of key Maugean skate prey species, and to support broader program analyses and the National Recovery Team for the Maugean skate.
